She earned degrees in physics and English literature from Stanford University, followed by a master’s and Ph.D. in astrophysics, specializing in laser physics and the interaction of X-rays with interstellar matter.
In 1977, Ride responded to a NASA recruitment announcement for the Space Shuttle program that, for the first time, actively sought women scientists. She was selected from more than 8,000 applicants as part of NASA’s historic astronaut class of 1978. Her groundbreaking spaceflight occurred on June 18, 1983, aboard the Space Shuttle Challenger, where she served as a mission specialist and flight engineer. During this mission, she helped deploy satellites and operate

item.title: Jeanne Klein, 1950s Fashion Model and Muse of Mid-Century Couture Photography
item.description:
Jeanne Klein was a French-American fashion model who rose to prominence in the 1950s and became widely recognized for her refined beauty, elegance, and strong presence in high-fashion photography. She worked extensively with leading photographers of the era and appeared in prestigious publications such as Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar. Her career was closely associated with some of the most influential designers of mid-century fashion, including Balenciaga, Christian Dior, Schiaparelli, and Jean Patou, showcasing couture garments that defined post-war elegance and sophistication.
Beyond her modeling career, Jeanne Klein played an important artistic role through her close collaboration with her husband, photographer William Klein. She frequently served as a muse and subject in his fashion and street photography, contributing to his development as a pioneering visual artist. Her presence in his work helped bridge the world of traditional fashion editorial imagery with more experimental and

item.title: Thomas Massie files 2028 candidacy paperwork, sparking speculation about future political plans
item.description:
Representative Thomas Massie, a Republican from Kentucky and frequent critic of former President Donald Trump, has sparked renewed political speculation after filing paperwork with the Federal Election Commission for the 2028 election cycle. The filing, made shortly after Massie lost a primary race to a Trump-backed challenger, allows him to raise funds for potential future campaigns while maintaining flexibility over his next political move.
In a public statement posted on social media, Massie clarified that he has not yet decided which office he intends to pursue in 2028. He explained that the FEC filing is a procedural step enabling him to continue fundraising for his ongoing political operations, both as a current officeholder and as a potential future candidate for federal office. His ambiguity about his political intentions immediately fueled discussion across political media and online platforms.
The announcement quickly drew reactions from commentators and political figures.

item.title: Former GOP strategist criticizes Trump and MAGA influence on the U.S. military
item.description:
Steve Schmidt, a former Republican political strategist and co-founder of The Lincoln Project, issued strong criticism of former President Donald Trump and the MAGA movement during Memorial Day reflections. Speaking in a commentary highlighted by Raw Story, Schmidt focused on the sacrifices made by U.S. service members and the emotional weight carried by military families, particularly those who have lost loved ones in combat.
Schmidt argued that the United States has been profoundly shaped by decades of war and warned against what he described as efforts to politicize or improperly influence the military. He expressed concern about what he characterized as attempts by Trump and his allies to reshape the armed forces for political purposes, suggesting this would undermine the institution’s integrity and independence.
In his remarks, Schmidt used particularly harsh language to condemn Trump, calling him a figure who disrespects military service and the sacrifices of American soldiers.

item.title: CNET Editor Questions Design and Reliability of Trump Mobile Smartphone During CNN Interview
item.description:
A CNN segment featuring CNET Managing Editor Patrick Holland drew attention to President Donald Trump's new business venture, Trump Mobile, and its gold-colored smartphone known as the T1. During the interview with CNN anchor Brianna Keilar, Holland criticized both the appearance and the lack of technical transparency surrounding the device. He described the phone’s gold finish as inconsistent depending on lighting conditions, comparing it at times to Scrooge McDuck’s gold coins and at other times to a “urine sample.” While the unusual color attracted headlines, Holland emphasized that his main concerns were related to missing information about the phone’s specifications and long-term support.
According to Holland, the company has not publicly clarified important details such as the processor powering the device or how long software and security updates will be provided. He contrasted this with major manufacturers like Samsung and Google, which commonly guarantee up to seven years of

item.title: A Look Back at Teri Garr’s Rise to Fame Through 1970s Portraits and Film Roles
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday highlights the career and public image of American actress, comedian, and dancer Teri Garr during the 1970s, accompanied by a collection of 20 vintage portraits. Born Terry Ann Garr in 1944, Garr began her entertainment career as a dancer and background performer in the 1960s. She worked in youth-oriented television programs and films choreographed by David Winters, who became an important mentor in her early professional life. During this period, she also appeared as a go-go dancer on musical variety shows alongside Toni Basil.
Garr gradually transitioned from dancing to acting, earning small speaking roles in productions such as The Monkees’ 1968 film “Head,” written by Jack Nicholson. Her breakthrough came in the 1970s when she became a regular cast member on “The Sonny & Cher Comedy Hour,” where she demonstrated both comedic talent and versatility in sketch performances. She later reunited with Cher on the singer’s solo television show.
The artic

item.title: Doctor explains how diet and gut health may influence anxiety and mental wellbeing
item.description:
This article explores the growing scientific understanding of the connection between nutrition, gut health, and mental wellbeing, particularly anxiety. According to Dr. Kunal Sood, an anaesthesiologist and interventional pain medicine physician, everyday dietary choices can significantly influence how the body responds to stress through the gut-brain axis, the biological communication system linking the digestive system and the brain. He explains that while anxiety is often considered a psychological condition, it can also be shaped by physical factors such as inflammation and microbiome balance.
Dr. Sood highlights that alcohol consumption may initially reduce anxiety but can later trigger a rebound effect, leading to increased anxiety once its effects wear off, especially with chronic use. Similarly, diets high in processed foods and refined sugars are associated with higher anxiety levels due to their role in promoting inflammation and disrupting gut health, both of which are linke

item.title: NYT analysis finds frequent praise and flattery in Trump cabinet meetings
item.description:
A New York Times report examined the tone and content of Trump administration cabinet meetings, concluding that a significant portion of the discussion is devoted to praising President Donald Trump. According to the analysis, which reviewed hours of cabinet meeting footage and transcripts, cabinet members frequently offered statements that credited Trump for policy outcomes, defended his political positions, or criticized his opponents. The report estimated that, on average, at least one out of every six sentences spoken during these meetings included either direct flattery of the president, attribution of achievements to him, or criticism of political adversaries.
The findings suggest a notable shift in the role of the cabinet compared to traditional expectations, where cabinet members are primarily expected to advise the president on policy and governance. Instead, the report described a dynamic in which expressions of loyalty and praise are a recurring feature of official meetings.

item.title: The Story of Y Bwthyn Bach, Queen Elizabeth II’s Childhood Cottage in Windsor Great Park
item.description:
Y Bwthyn Bach, meaning “The Little House” in Welsh, was a miniature cottage gifted to Princess Elizabeth, the future Queen Elizabeth II, for her sixth birthday in 1932. The playhouse was presented by the people of Wales as a symbolic and heartfelt gift. Designed by architect Edmund Willmott, the cottage was built in the style of a traditional Welsh home using leftover materials from the reconstruction of Llandough Hospital. Despite its small dimensions, the house was fully functional and carefully detailed to suit royal children.
Located within Windsor Great Park near the Royal Lodge, the cottage included a living room, kitchen, bedroom, and bathroom. It also featured modern conveniences for the time, such as running hot and cold water, electricity, a gas cooker, a refrigerator, and even a heated towel rail. Many of the furnishings were custom-made miniature versions of full-sized household items, including books, china, and a tiny telephone.
Princess Elizabeth and her younger sister

item.title: JD Vance Invites Republican Attorneys General Earlier Than Democrats for Fraud Task Force Meeting
item.description: Vice President JD Vance has been tasked by President Donald Trump with leading the administration's efforts to combat fraud. As part of this initiative, a White House meeting was scheduled for Tuesday afternoon to discuss the fraud task force with state attorneys general. According to a Politico report, invitations to Republican attorneys general were sent about a week in advance, while Democratic attorneys general received their invitations on Friday with a tight RSVP deadline of Saturday. This staggered approach has been described by administration insiders as a strategic political move. The meeting was initially planned to include only Republican attorneys general, but Vance reportedly pushed back against that idea. Approximately 15 Republican attorneys general are expected to attend in person, including those from Utah, South Dakota, Idaho, Oklahoma, and Indiana. Democrats are largely expected to skip the meeting, though some may send staff members instead. The timing and selective

item.title: The Knicks’ Winning Streak and the Danhausen ‘Curse’ Narrative Explained
item.description:
The article explores a humorous and speculative sports storyline linking the New York Knicks’ recent winning streak to professional wrestler Danhausen and his self-described “curse.” The narrative begins when Danhausen, a WWE-affiliated wrestler known for his theatrical persona, appeared on ESPN and jokingly cursed sports commentator Stephen A. Smith after feeling disrespected. Following this staged curse, the Knicks experienced a notable stretch of success in the NBA playoffs, prompting fans and online commentators to connect the team’s performance to Danhausen’s antics.
According to the article, the Knicks went 2–1 in the playoffs after the initial “curse,” and their performance improved further after a fan used Cameo to pay Danhausen to lift the curse by making an offering referred to as “human money.” After this, the Knicks reportedly won six consecutive games, fueling the viral superstition that the team’s success was tied to Danhausen’s supernatural gimmick rather than purely at

item.title: Vintage Photographs Capture Daily Life at Chicago’s Riverview Amusement Park in 1962
item.description:
The article highlights a collection of vintage photographs taken by photographer Laird Scott during the summer of 1962 at Chicago’s famous Riverview Amusement Park. Once considered one of the largest and most popular amusement parks in the United States, Riverview was a major entertainment destination for families, couples, and children before it permanently closed in 1967. Scott’s portraits document the atmosphere of the park during its final years of operation, capturing visitors enjoying rides, games, and social moments.
The photographs focus less on the amusement park attractions themselves and more on the people who visited the park. Through candid portraits and carefully observed scenes, the images reflect the culture and everyday life of mid-century America. Children are shown reacting with excitement and curiosity, young couples appear enjoying dates and conversations, and families are depicted spending leisure time together during the summer season.
The article emphasizes Sc

item.title: Grigori Rasputin: Historical Portraits and His Role as Advisor to the Russian Imperial Family
item.description: Grigori Yefimovich Rasputin was a Russian mystic and faith healer born in 1869 in a poor Siberian peasant family. Known by nicknames such as the 'Mad Monk' or 'Holy Devil,' he rose to prominence as a controversial advisor to Tsar Nicholas II and Tsarina Alexandra. Despite limited formal education and a reputation for wild behavior in his youth, Rasputin became a wandering holy man after a religious conversion. He married and had children but continued his itinerant lifestyle. Rasputin arrived in St. Petersburg around 1903-1905, where his mystical presence captivated elite society. His influence peaked after he helped alleviate the suffering of the Tsarevich Alexei, who had hemophilia. The royal couple credited him with easing the boy's bleeding episodes, granting Rasputin significant sway over court appointments and decisions, especially during World War I when the Tsar was away at the front. Critics accused him of debauchery, drunkenness, and political meddling, viewing him as a symbo

item.title: Online backlash emerges over UFC stage design for White House Freedom 250 event
item.description:
A UFC stage design set up for an upcoming event at the White House has sparked widespread online criticism and ridicule. The UFC Freedom 250 stage is being constructed on the South Lawn in Washington, D.C., ahead of a scheduled June 14 event that will commemorate both the 250th anniversary of the United States and President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday. Construction activity includes visible structural elements such as cranes and a large metal arch intended for lighting above the stage, signaling the scale and spectacle planned for the event.
As images and design details circulated on social media, users quickly reacted with sarcasm and concern. Some commentators mocked the concept outright, with one post stating “Pray for rain,” while others questioned the priorities behind hosting such a large-scale entertainment and sporting setup at the White House. Another critic suggested that if such a production could be staged, it might undermine the need for additional infrastructure project

item.title: Rare 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster Showcases Pre-War Automotive Design
item.description:
The article highlights the elegance and historical significance of the 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster, one of the most admired luxury sports cars from the pre-war automotive era. Designed by Jean Bugatti, the son of company founder Ettore Bugatti, the Type 57 series represented the brand’s commitment to combining engineering excellence with artistic craftsmanship. The ‘Grand Raid’ version stood out because of its aerodynamic styling, sleek proportions, and racing-inspired appearance.
According to the article, the vehicle featured a supercharged 3.3-liter straight-eight engine that delivered both strong performance and refined driving characteristics for its time. The roadster’s low-slung body and aggressively angled windshield contributed to a streamlined profile that reflected the growing influence of speed and aerodynamics in 1930s automobile design. Many examples of the model were custom-bodied, making each car unique and highly desirable among collectors.
The article

item.title: Florrie Holmes and the Forty Elephants: Women of London’s Notorious All-Female Crime Gang
item.description:
The article presents a historical portrait of Florrie Holmes, one of the known members of the Forty Elephants, an all-female criminal gang that operated in London between 1873 and the 1950s. Based in South London, this group became infamous for its organized and highly coordinated shoplifting operations, particularly targeting high-end stores in the West End. Unlike many criminal organizations of the time, the Forty Elephants stood out for being entirely female-led and remarkably strategic in their methods.
Florrie Holmes initially entered this world not as a thief, but as the girlfriend of a gangster. Over time, however, she became fully involved in the gang’s activities and adopted its criminal lifestyle. The Forty Elephants developed clever techniques for theft, including specially designed clothing with hidden compartments and voluminous garments that allowed them to conceal stolen goods. Their targets included jewelry, luxury fashion items, furs, leather goods, and lingerie, ofte

item.title: Antonelli wins Canadian Grand Prix as Russell retires after Mercedes power unit failure
item.description:
Kimi Antonelli secured a dominant victory at the Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix after Mercedes teammate George Russell retired from the race due to a power unit failure. The race at Circuit Gilles Villeneuve featured intense early battles between the two Mercedes drivers, including multiple exchanges for the lead and a brief on-track contact at the final chicane. Russell initially led the race and was in strong contention for his first win of the season, but his challenge ended on lap 30 when a Mercedes power unit issue forced him to stop and retire. His exit dramatically reshaped the race outcome and the championship standings.
With Russell out, Antonelli controlled the remainder of the Grand Prix and went on to claim his fourth consecutive win, significantly strengthening his position in the drivers’ championship. The result extended his lead over Russell to 43 points, giving the young Italian a major boost in the title fight.
Behind the Mercedes driver, Lewis Hamilton finished seco

item.title: Early Christmas Cover Art from The New Yorker in the 1920s and 1930s
item.description:
The article presents a retrospective look at 51 Christmas-themed covers from The New Yorker magazine, focusing on works produced during the 1920s and 1930s. Founded in 1925 by Harold Ross and Jane Grant, The New Yorker quickly became known for its distinctive blend of journalism, satire, fiction, and especially its iconic illustrated covers. During its early decades, the magazine’s holiday editions reflected both traditional Christmas imagery and the evolving urban life of New York City. Artists such as Rea Irvin, Helen E. Hokinson, Ilonka Karasz, and Constantin Alajalov contributed memorable illustrations that captured festive scenes with humor, elegance, and social observation.
The covers from this era often depicted Santa Claus in unconventional settings, integrating him into everyday city life—whether interacting with office routines, navigating busy streets, or appearing in humorous domestic situations. Alongside Santa, other recurring holiday motifs included carolers, Christmas

item.title: U.S. Military Targets Iranian Missile Facilities and Boats Near Strait of Hormuz Following Attacks on Naval Ships
item.description:
The United States carried out military strikes against Iranian facilities near Bandar Abbas after Iranian forces allegedly attacked U.S. warships operating in the Strait of Hormuz. According to reports cited by CNN, the strikes targeted missile launch sites and military boats believed to be connected to the attacks on American naval vessels. Explosions were reportedly heard around the targeted areas and nearby cities following the operation.
U.S. officials described the action as an act of self-defense amid rising tensions between Washington and Tehran. The military response comes during an ongoing period of diplomatic negotiations related to Iran’s nuclear program and broader regional conflict. President Donald Trump has publicly stated that discussions are continuing, although no agreement has been reached.
Iranian officials signaled that they are unwilling to back down from their current position. A senior Iranian representative speaking on state media reportedly declared that the

item.title: White House Prepares Large UFC Event Amid Ongoing Construction and Concerns About Insects
item.description:
Preparations for the upcoming UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House have drawn attention as construction activity expands across the grounds ahead of the celebration planned for June 14, 2026. According to reports, cranes and large metal structures were installed near the Executive Residence to support lighting and staging for the UFC event, which is being organized as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the United States. The event is also tied closely to President Donald Trump, who will turn 80 on the same date.
Media coverage described the White House grounds as increasingly resembling a major entertainment venue due to the ongoing construction work. Temporary infrastructure for the UFC stage, including large lighting rigs and support arches, has been erected on the South Lawn while separate ballroom construction projects continue nearby.
Additional attention was brought to the event after UFC CEO Dana White discussed concerns about insects around the venue. D

item.title: A Look Back at Nastassja Kinski’s International Film Career in the 1970s and 1980s
item.description:
The article highlights the career and cultural impact of German actress and former model Nastassja Kinski, who became one of the most recognizable international film stars of the late 1970s and 1980s. Born in 1961, Kinski first entered the entertainment industry as a model before transitioning into acting at a young age. Her early breakthrough came through her collaboration with acclaimed German New Wave director Wim Wenders in the 1975 film “The Wrong Move.”
Kinski achieved worldwide fame after starring in Roman Polanski’s 1979 drama “Tess,” an adaptation of Thomas Hardy’s classic novel. Her performance earned her a Golden Globe Award and established her as a major presence in both European and Hollywood cinema. The article emphasizes her distinctive screen presence, often described as ethereal and emotionally intense, which helped her stand out during a highly competitive era in film.
Throughout the 1980s, Kinski appeared in several notable productions, including Francis Ford Coppo

item.title: Frank Sinatra’s Early Life and Childhood in Hoboken, New Jersey
item.description:
The article explores the early life and childhood of Frank Sinatra, one of the most influential American singers of the 20th century, through rare photographs and biographical details. Sinatra was born on December 12, 1915, in Hoboken, New Jersey, into a working-class Italian-American family. His birth was reportedly difficult: he was an unusually large baby delivered with forceps, which caused lasting physical scars and a punctured eardrum. He was initially thought to be stillborn until he was revived by his grandmother. As an only child, Sinatra grew up in a household shaped by strong parental figures. His father, Marty Sinatra, was a former boxer who worked as a firefighter, while his mother, Dolly Sinatra, was a midwife and politically active local figure known for her strong personality and intense devotion to her son.
Sinatra’s childhood was marked by both privilege and discipline. Despite modest means, his parents provided him with good clothing and personal comforts, earning h

item.title: John Bolton says Iran is exploiting Trump’s urgency for a negotiated deal
item.description:
Former U.S. National Security Advisor John Bolton warned that Iran is taking advantage of what he describes as President Donald Trump’s strong desire to reach a negotiated agreement in ongoing tensions with Tehran. In comments made during a CNN interview, Bolton argued that Iranian leaders have perceived Trump as “desperate” to secure a deal that he can present as a political and economic victory, including potential benefits such as lower global oil and gasoline prices. According to Bolton, this perceived urgency is shaping how Iran engages in negotiations, allowing its leaders to prolong talks and delay concessions while strengthening their own strategic position.
Bolton also criticized what he sees as a long-standing belief held by Trump that most international disputes can be resolved through deal-making. He suggested that this approach may not apply to adversaries like Iran, which he characterized as ideologically driven and resistant to compromise in the same way as traditional

item.title: Ursula Andress in Hammer’s 1965 Fantasy Film 'She' and Its Production Legacy
item.description:
The article focuses on the 1965 British adventure-fantasy film She, produced by Hammer Film Productions and adapted from H. Rider Haggard’s 1887 novel of the same name. The film is highlighted as a visually rich Technicolor production that blends adventure, romance, and supernatural elements, standing out within Hammer’s typically horror-focused catalog. It stars Ursula Andress, who had recently gained international fame for her role in Dr. No, in the central role of Ayesha, an enigmatic and immortal queen known as “She Who Must Be Obeyed.”
The narrative of the film follows three English explorers who journey into a remote and mysterious region of Africa, where they discover a hidden civilization ruled by Ayesha. Her character is portrayed as both powerful and tragic, driven by an ancient desire tied to the reincarnation of a lost lover. The article emphasizes the film’s exotic settings, elaborate costume design, and atmospheric cinematography, all of which contributed to its distinct

item.title: Report Claims JD Vance Is Reconsidering a 2028 Presidential Campaign Amid Foreign Policy Disputes
item.description:
A report published by The Daily Mail and highlighted by Raw Story claims that Vice President JD Vance is facing increasing political isolation within President Donald Trump’s administration and may be reconsidering a potential presidential campaign in 2028. According to unnamed insiders cited in the report, Vance has become politically vulnerable following the resignation of Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, who was reportedly one of the administration’s leading non-interventionist voices. The report states that both Vance and Gabbard privately expressed concerns about U.S. military involvement related to Iran.
Sources quoted in the article describe Vance as the “lone dove” remaining in Trump’s cabinet and suggest that his cautious foreign policy stance has put him at odds with the president, who is increasingly embracing a more aggressive wartime leadership image. The article also claims Vance unsuccessfully urged Trump behind closed doors to limit military action agai

item.title: Early 20th-Century Studio Portraits Featuring Artificial Snow Effects
item.description:
The article explores a popular photographic trend from the early 20th century in which studio portraits were designed to simulate snowy winter scenes. At a time when outdoor photography was often difficult due to harsh weather conditions, photographers creatively recreated winter environments inside their studios. These staged portraits commonly featured children, couples, or families posed in front of painted backdrops depicting snow-covered landscapes, forests, or cozy interior scenes with wintry views outside a window.
To enhance the illusion of snowfall, photographers used simple and accessible materials such as cotton batting, shredded paper, and white powder. These props were carefully arranged around subjects, at the base of the set, or placed on branches and decorative elements to mimic freshly fallen snow. The controlled studio setting allowed photographers to carefully manage lighting, composition, and exposure, resulting in polished and visually appealing images that would

item.title: Photo Captures John Lennon and Yoko Ono Interrupting Their 1969 Peace Protest for Hotel Housekeeping
item.description:
The article revisits a well-known moment from John Lennon and Yoko Ono’s 1969 “Bed-In for Peace” protest in Amsterdam, where the couple used their honeymoon as a public demonstration against war and violence. Staying in the Presidential Suite of the Hilton Hotel after their marriage, Lennon and Ono invited journalists into their room while remaining in bed for several days as a symbolic call for peace, love, and nonviolence during a period marked by global conflict and the Vietnam War.
The featured photograph, taken by photographer Charles Ley and published in the Daily Mirror on March 26, 1969, shows an amusing interruption to the protest. Lennon and Ono were temporarily forced to leave their bed so hotel maid Maria de Soledade Alves could change the sheets in their luxury suite. The moment highlighted the contrast between the couple’s idealistic public activism and the practical realities of everyday hotel life.
According to the original newspaper article, the protest’s calm atmosp

item.title: Thomas Massie Signals Possible 2028 Political Return After Losing GOP Primary
item.description:
Kentucky Representative Thomas Massie, a Republican known for frequently breaking with his party leadership and President Donald Trump, is signaling that he may not be done with electoral politics despite recently losing his GOP primary. After being defeated by Trump-backed challenger Ed Gallrein in Kentucky’s 4th Congressional District, Massie filed paperwork with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) indicating participation in the 2028 election cycle. The filing allows him to continue fundraising and maintain his political operation while he decides whether to pursue another House seat or a different federal office.
Massie stated publicly on X that he had filed the paperwork to keep his options open, emphasizing that he has not yet made a final decision about which office he may seek. According to reporting, he designated his Kentucky campaign committee as his primary committee for 2028 and authorized additional fundraising structures to support his political activities. These mech

item.title: WHO warns worsening Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda is outpacing response efforts
item.description:
World Health Organization Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that the Ebola outbreak affecting the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is spreading rapidly and may worsen before improving. According to Tedros, delays in detecting infections allowed the epidemic to expand quickly, forcing health authorities to catch up with transmission already underway. The WHO reported more than 100 confirmed cases and 10 confirmed deaths in Congo, along with hundreds of suspected infections and deaths that indicate the outbreak may be significantly larger than official confirmed numbers suggest. Uganda has also confirmed several infections and at least one death.
Tedros explained that the response is being complicated by armed conflict, population displacement, and distrust of outside authorities in affected regions such as Ituri and North Kivu. Security incidents targeting health facilities have further hindered containment efforts. The WHO said it is coordinating with the gover

item.title: Mammoth Lakes Film Festival Announces Winners, with ‘Mono 222’, ‘Dreaming of Lions’ and ‘I Got Bombed at Harvey’s’ Taking Top Honors
item.description:
The 12th annual Mammoth Lakes Film Festival, held from May 20 to May 24 in the California mountain town, has announced its award winners, recognizing a diverse slate of independent films across narrative, documentary, and short film categories. The jury prize for Best North American Narrative Feature went to “Mono 222,” directed by Quinton Dominguez, which also received a $1,000 cash award and a $15,000 Panavision camera rental grant. In the Best International Narrative Feature category, “Dreaming of Lions,” written and directed by Paolo Marinou-Blanco, secured top honors along with a $1,000 prize, while “Tony Odyssey” received an honorable mention.
In documentary features, “I Got Bombed at Harvey’s,” directed by Amy Bandlien Storkel and Bryan Storkel, was named Best Documentary Feature. The documentary Bravery Award went to “Sugarfly” by Max Schoenfeld. Audience awards highlighted broader viewer engagement, with “Danny Is My Boyfriend” winning Best Narrative Feature Audience Award, “

item.title: Journalist claims alleged directed energy weapon attacks caused lasting injuries, offers no supporting evidence
item.description:
Veteran journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodríguez has publicly claimed that she has been “permanently injured” by what she describes as alleged directed energy weapon attacks. In a post published on Substack, she stated that these supposed attacks occurred in private settings such as her home and while she was in bed, and that they were designed not to kill immediately but instead to cause prolonged pain and illness. She further alleged that such methods are used to target journalists and critics in ways that could lead them to doubt their own experiences, describing the phenomenon as involving pulsed energy that could affect individuals selectively.
Valdes-Rodríguez connected her claims to her reporting on Jeffrey Epstein, including investigations into his New Mexico compound and alleged surveillance activities involving sensitive U.S. facilities such as nuclear weapons laboratories. She suggested that her work on Epstein-related topics and missing American scientists may have made her a ta

item.title: Memorial Day Tech Sales Highlight Limited-Time Discounts on Popular Electronics and Gadgets
item.description:
The article covers a roundup of limited-time tech discounts released during the Memorial Day shopping period, emphasizing that many of these deals are expected to expire quickly. It highlights how rising costs driven by inflation and tariffs have made meaningful discounts in consumer electronics less frequent, increasing the appeal of seasonal sales events like Memorial Day.
Among the featured offers is a discounted Apple MagSafe charger, promoted as a convenient wireless charging solution for iPhones and accessories. Other portable tech items include a compact rechargeable handheld fan, a JBL Clip 4 waterproof portable speaker, and a multifunctional 3-in-1 charging station designed to power phones, AirPods, and Apple Watches simultaneously. The list also features an extra-bright rechargeable lantern that doubles as a phone power bank and a heated handheld massager aimed at relieving hand strain.
Larger-ticket electronics include a discounted 11th-generation iPad, a Hisense framed-st

item.title: SpaceX Completes Falcon 9 Mission Carrying 29 Starlink Satellites From Cape Canaveral
item.description:
SpaceX continued the expansion of its Starlink internet satellite constellation with a Falcon 9 launch from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station on Memorial Day. The mission successfully deployed 29 additional Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit, increasing the company’s already massive network to more than 10,000 satellites. The launch marked SpaceX’s 60th orbital mission of 2026, highlighting the company’s rapid launch cadence and ongoing focus on global satellite internet coverage.
According to launch details reported by Spaceflight Now, the Falcon 9 rocket lifted off successfully and completed all major mission milestones. Approximately 8.5 minutes after launch, the reusable first-stage booster, identified as B1078, landed safely on the autonomous drone ship “A Shortfall of Gravitas” stationed in the Atlantic Ocean off the South Carolina coast. The landing represented the 151st successful recovery for the drone ship and the 614th booster landing achieved by SpaceX overall.
The

item.title: California Chemical Leak Emergency Forces Mass Evacuations Near Garden Grove Aerospace Facility
item.description:
Authorities in Orange County, California, continue to respond to a dangerous chemical emergency involving a tank containing methyl methacrylate at a GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove. The incident prompted large-scale evacuations affecting at least 50,000 residents after concerns emerged that the tank could explode due to the volatile nature of the chemical. Methyl methacrylate is a highly flammable industrial substance commonly used in the production of plastics and resins for aerospace manufacturing.
According to reports, California Gov. Gavin Newsom and President Donald Trump declared a national emergency in response to the ongoing threat. State Sen. Tony Strickland stated that conditions had improved compared to earlier in the week, though officials warned the danger had not been completely eliminated. Emergency crews and hazardous materials teams have remained at the scene monitoring tank stability and local air quality.
The Orange County Fire Authority announced that the i

item.title: Behind-the-scenes Polaroids of Steve Buscemi in the 1994 film 'Somebody to Love'
item.description: The article highlights a set of rare behind-the-scenes Polaroid photographs taken during the production of the 1994 film “Somebody to Love,” directed by Alexandre Rockwell. The film is a romantic drama set in Los Angeles that follows the interconnected lives of struggling artists and dreamers. It draws inspiration from Federico Fellini’s “Nights of Cabiria” and features a notable ensemble cast, including Rosie Perez, Harvey Keitel, Anthony Quinn, Stanley Tucci, and a cameo by Quentin Tarantino. Within the film, Steve Buscemi plays Mickey, a character described as a transvestite who works in the same bar as the protagonist Mercedes, portrayed by Rosie Perez. His performance was noted for its emotional depth and authenticity, with critic Roger Ebert praising Buscemi for delivering a “surprisingly convincing” portrayal. The focus of the article is on rare Polaroid images captured by artist Scott Williams, which show Buscemi in full costume and makeup while portraying his character. These

item.title: DOJ Filing Defending Trump White House Ballroom Project Draws Criticism for Unusual Language
item.description:
A Justice Department court filing defending President Donald Trump’s controversial White House ballroom construction project has sparked criticism and online attention because of its unusual wording and stylistic similarities to Trump’s social media posts. Former CBS News reporter Scott MacFarlane highlighted several aspects of the six-page filing, arguing that the document resembled a Truth Social message more than a traditional legal filing. According to MacFarlane, the filing contained irregular capitalization, exaggerated phrasing, and even the apparent invented word “invaulable” while describing Trump’s contribution to the country.
The filing was submitted in support of the ongoing ballroom and East Wing construction project at the White House. It described the project as “underbudget,” despite reports that Senate Republicans had recently been asked to approve approximately $1 billion in taxpayer funding related to the project. The funding request reportedly contributed to politi

item.title: Sen. Chris Murphy criticizes reported Iran ceasefire deal, says U.S. leaves conflict weakened
item.description:
Sen. Chris Murphy (D-CT) issued a strong critique of a reported ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran following a period of conflict. While Murphy said he would welcome an end to the war, he argued that the terms of the deal appear to favor Iran and leave the United States in a weaker strategic position. In comments shared on social media, Murphy suggested that Iran has effectively negotiated from a position of strength, characterizing the agreement as a “humiliating” outcome for the U.S.
According to Murphy’s assessment, the deal reportedly includes significant concessions, including the possibility of Iran regaining financial resources through sanctions relief and maintaining strategic advantages in the region. He also raised concerns that Iran may retain influence over critical maritime routes such as the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint that has been central to regional tensions.
Murphy further argued that the nuclear-related provisions of the a

item.title: Video sparks reactions after Trump appears to close his eyes during Memorial Day ceremony
item.description:
A video circulating online has sparked widespread discussion after showing U.S. President Donald Trump appearing to close his eyes during a Memorial Day ceremony honoring fallen soldiers. The footage, shared by political video accounts and amplified on social media, appears to show Trump seated during the event while others stand at attention listening to remarks from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. In the clip, Trump is seen with his eyes closed for an extended period and briefly tapping his knees, which led some viewers to suggest he may have been dozing off or disengaged.
The video quickly generated a range of reactions across social media platforms. Critics and political commentators used the moment to question the president’s attentiveness and fitness for office, with some describing the behavior as disrespectful during a solemn occasion. Others interpreted the footage differently, suggesting that Trump may have been squinting due to bright sunlight or briefly resting his eyes wh

item.title: Wendell Pierce Balances Theater, TV, and Film Roles in a Busy Career Milestone
item.description:
Wendell Pierce reflects on a decades-long acting career marked by versatility across stage, television, and film. In a recent interview, the actor explains how a personal moment in 2019—seeing himself simultaneously on a London billboard for ‘Jack Ryan’ while also performing in theater and screening a film at a festival—led him to create a personal “trifecta” mantra. His goal became to annually participate in theater, television, and film projects, a discipline he describes as central to his identity as a “journeyman actor.”
Now, Pierce says he has exceeded that goal by effectively expanding it into a “quadfecta.” In a single period, he is performing in Shakespeare’s ‘Othello’ with the Shakespeare Theatre Company, appearing in the Season 3 finale of CBS’s ‘Elsbeth,’ and starring in the Prime Video action film ‘Jack Ryan: Ghost War.’ He also continues his role in the ‘Power Book III: Raising Kanan’ universe, further demonstrating his range across genres and platforms.
Pierce discusses

item.title: Trump Scheduled for Routine Medical Checkup at Walter Reed, Sparking Online Speculation
item.description: President Donald Trump is scheduled to visit Walter Reed Medical Center for a routine annual physical and dental checkup. This marks his third visit to the facility in 13 months, following an annual physical in April 2025 and a scheduled follow-up in October. The White House announced the visit in advance on May 11th. However, the news triggered significant online reactions, with some users expressing alarm and one liberal influencer asking, 'Is this the day?' in response to a Daily Mail alert. Conservative voices, including influencer Catturd, dismissed the coverage as misleading, emphasizing that it was a standard procedure and criticizing sensational headlines. European correspondent Bastian Brauns also questioned the 'breaking news' label, noting the advance notice from the White House and calling it sensationalism. The administration has consistently described these visits as routine medical appointments. The story highlights the polarized nature of media coverage and social media

item.title: The Relationship Between Elvis Presley and June Juanico During the Rise of His Fame
item.description:
The article explores the romantic relationship between Elvis Presley and June Juanico, which lasted on and off from 1955 to 1957 during the early years of Presley’s rise to international fame. June Juanico, a former beauty queen from Biloxi, Mississippi, met Elvis after one of his concerts in June 1955. According to her recollections, Elvis was immediately attracted to her and they spent hours talking in his car until sunrise. Their bond quickly became emotionally deep, and Juanico later described Elvis as the love of her life.
The story highlights how their relationship developed during a crucial period in Presley’s career. Juanico explained that Elvis valued family and lifelong commitment, and he openly shared personal details about his life, including the death of his twin brother. Despite their closeness, the pressures of Elvis’s growing celebrity status created constant challenges. Fans, reporters, and publicity demands made privacy almost impossible, while Elvis’s manager, Colon

item.title: Paul McCartney Recreates the Iconic Abbey Road Crossing Photo with His Dog Arrow for the 'Paul Is Live' Album Cover in 1993
item.description: In 1993, Paul McCartney returned to the famous Abbey Road zebra crossing in London to recreate the iconic Beatles album cover from 1969 for his live album 'Paul Is Live.' The original 1969 photograph, taken by Iain Macmillan, showed the Beatles walking across the street and later sparked the 'Paul Is Dead' conspiracy theory due to elements like Paul's barefoot appearance and the Volkswagen Beetle's license plate. For the 1993 shoot, McCartney was joined by his Old English sheepdog Arrow, the offspring of his earlier dog Martha. Unlike the original where he was barefoot on a hot day, McCartney wore shoes this time. The new image was digitally combined with the 1969 photo, and the Beetle's license plate was changed to '51 IS,' referencing McCartney's age at the time. McCartney addressed the old rumors, explaining the barefoot detail was simply due to comfort in warm weather and dismissing conspiracy interpretations. The 'Paul Is Live' album featured live recordings from tours in Australi

item.title: Overview of Railway Wonders of the World Magazine and Its Historical Coverage of Railways
item.description:
The article explores the history and significance of the British magazine “Railway Wonders of the World,” which was published between 1935 and 1936 by the Amalgamated Press. Edited by Clarence Winchester, the fortnightly publication ran for a total of thirty-seven issues and was designed to educate the public about the development, engineering, and global cultural impact of railways. It became known for its richly illustrated content, combining detailed written articles with photographs and artistic illustrations that captured the technological ambition of the railway age.
The magazine covered a wide range of subjects related to rail transport, including famous international railway lines such as the Trans-Siberian Railway, as well as the mechanics behind locomotive operation. It also highlighted major engineering achievements, including the construction of tunnels, bridges, and other infrastructural feats that enabled railway expansion across challenging landscapes. In addition, it f

item.title: President Trump Scheduled for Routine Medical Visit at Walter Reed, His Third in 13 Months
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to visit Walter Reed Medical Center on Tuesday for a scheduled medical and dental checkup, marking his third hospital visit in the past 13 months. According to reports, the 79-year-old president previously went to the facility in April 2025 for his annual physical and again in October 2025 for a follow-up. The White House has described these visits as routine, but former White House physicians have raised concerns about transparency regarding the president's health. Former cardiologist Jonathan Reiner noted that older individuals often face medical issues and criticized the administration for what he sees as a lack of candor. Public opinion polls reflect growing skepticism, with a Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos survey from April showing only 40% of Americans believe Trump has the mental sharpness for the presidency, down from 47% in September. On physical fitness, approval dropped from 54% to 44%. Trump was diagnosed with chronic venous insufficiency

item.title: How funding cuts and VA staffing shortages are affecting Veterans Treatment Courts and justice outcomes for veterans
item.description:
This article examines how U.S. veterans are being affected by gaps in healthcare access, staffing shortages at the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), and reductions in public health funding, and how these challenges intersect with the criminal justice system. It highlights the role of Veterans Treatment Courts, specialized judicial programs designed to divert eligible veterans away from incarceration and into treatment and support services. These courts address underlying issues such as substance use disorders, mental health conditions, homelessness, and reintegration difficulties that often contribute to criminal behavior among veterans.
Veterans Treatment Courts, which now operate in more than 745 courthouses across the United States, rely heavily on coordinated services including VA clinicians, counseling, housing assistance, and addiction treatment programs. While they are supported by a mix of federal, state, and local funding, their effectiveness depends on stable staffing and

item.title: Failed Experimental Embalming Caused Decomposition Problems at Pope Pius XII’s 1958 Funeral
item.description:
The article describes the widely reported embalming disaster that occurred after the death of Pope Pius XII in October 1958. According to historical accounts, the Pope’s personal physician, Riccardo Galeazzi-Lisi, used an unconventional preservation technique instead of standard embalming procedures traditionally employed for deceased popes. Pius XII had reportedly requested that his internal organs not be removed, leading Galeazzi-Lisi to attempt an experimental method involving aromatic oils and wrapping the body in cellophane.
The procedure failed badly. Due to warm weather conditions, lack of proper refrigeration, and the ineffective embalming process, the Pope’s body rapidly decomposed. Reports from the time stated that the corpse became swollen, discolored, and emitted a severe odor during the public viewing. Swiss Guards assigned to stand near the body allegedly had to be replaced frequently because of the smell, and some accounts claim parts of the body deteriorated visibly. A

item.title: Casey Anthony reemerges publicly as LGBTQ advocate, sparking debate and backlash
item.description:
Casey Anthony, who was acquitted in 2011 of charges related to the death of her two-year-old daughter Caylee Anthony, has recently re-entered public discourse with a new online presence that has generated widespread controversy. According to reports, Anthony has launched a Substack account titled “Beyond Case,” where she describes herself as a researcher, consultant, advocate, activist, author, and LGBTQ+ ally. Her profile also includes politically charged messaging, including the phrase “We are the Resistance. We do not bow to mediocre white men,” and has reportedly attracted more than 11,000 subscribers.
Her return to visibility quickly circulated on social media platforms, particularly after screenshots of her profile were shared widely over the Memorial Day weekend. The reaction has been sharply divided. Some commentators and media personalities expressed strong disapproval, with critics questioning the sincerity and appropriateness of her public advocacy given her highly controve

item.title: Trump faces internal and external pressure over ongoing Iran peace negotiations
item.description:
A report published by Raw Story alleges that President Donald Trump is facing significant political pressure from both domestic and international allies regarding ongoing diplomatic efforts with Iran. According to the article, Trump has been engaged in discussions aimed at reaching a preliminary agreement intended to end U.S. military involvement in conflict with Iran. Although a framework for a potential deal has reportedly been agreed upon in principle, final approval has not yet been reached.
The article claims that influential political figures and media personalities, including former CIA Director Mike Pompeo, Senators Ted Cruz and Lindsey Graham, and activist Laura Loomer, have urged Trump to abandon the negotiations entirely. Loomer is quoted making inflammatory remarks against the possibility of peace with Iran. In addition, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and media mogul Rupert Murdoch are described as applying external pressure that may be influencing the administr

item.title: MBC Shahid Acquires John Achkar’s Arabic Stand-Up Special Filmed at Paris’ L’Olympia
item.description:
Middle Eastern streaming platform MBC Shahid has acquired Lebanese comedian John Achkar’s stand-up comedy special “Tryin’” (“Aam Jarrib”), marking a significant moment for Arabic-language comedy on streaming platforms. The special was filmed at the historic L’Olympia concert hall in Paris during Achkar’s performance on Oct. 23, becoming the first Arabic-language comedy performance ever staged at the iconic venue. L’Olympia has previously hosted internationally recognized comedians such as Chris Rock, Gad Elmaleh, and Eddie Izzard.
The special will premiere exclusively across the Middle East and North Africa region on MBC Shahid starting May 27. Achkar’s global comedy tour, which included sold-out performances in 85 cities, was produced by Dubai-based company Front Row Filmed Entertainment. The company also confirmed that “Tryin’” is the first project in a planned six-title Arabic stand-up comedy slate currently in development.
According to the producers, the show combines fast-paced

item.title: Supercars pit lane order confirmed for Darwin Triple Crown after Tasmania Super440
item.description:
The Supercars pit lane order for the upcoming Darwin Triple Crown has been confirmed following the results of the Tasmania Super440 round. Triple Eight Race Engineering continues to lead the teams’ championship and retains the prime pit exit garage position for the event at Hidden Valley, extending its advantage over Grove Racing to 68 points. Grove Racing remains second overall despite a mixed weekend in Tasmania, although it recovered strongly with a podium double from Matt Payne and Kai Allen in the Sunday race.
Walkinshaw Andretti United (Walkinshaw TWG Racing) holds third place in the standings, maintaining consistency after a competitive weekend that included Chaz Mostert securing a first win in Toyota machinery. Tickford Racing stays fourth despite lacking headline results at Symmons Plains, but its steady points haul was enough to preserve position.
The most notable change in the midfield sees Team 18 climb into fifth place, overtaking Dick Johnson Racing. Team 18 benefited f

item.title: Enhanced Games Launch in Las Vegas Amid Debate Over Performance-Enhancing Drugs in Sports
item.description: The Enhanced Games, a new sports competition allowing athletes to use performance-enhancing drugs under medical supervision, are set to take place in Las Vegas. The privately funded event features more than 40 athletes competing in swimming, sprinting, hurdles, weightlifting, and strongman contests for a total prize pool of $25 million. Organizers say the competition is designed to explore the limits of human performance while openly embracing scientific enhancement methods that are banned in traditional sports such as the Olympics. The event is backed financially by Donald Trump Jr.’s venture capital firm, 1789 Capitol, along with technology investor Peter Thiel. The company behind the games also plans to market peptides and supplements while collecting data on athlete enhancement practices. According to organizers, most participants are using substances such as testosterone, human growth hormone, and stimulants under physician supervision. However, many medical experts and anti-dopin

item.title: Biography of Mina Mazzini, Legendary Italian Pop Singer Known as “The Tiger of Cremona”
item.description:
The article presents a retrospective look at Mina Anna Mazzini, one of Italy’s most celebrated and influential pop singers. Born in 1940, Mina rose to fame in the late 1950s and quickly became a dominant force in Italian and European popular music. Her extraordinary vocal ability, often described as spanning four octaves, and her expressive, emotionally charged performances earned her the nickname “The Tiger of Cremona.” Throughout the 1960s and early 1970s, Mina achieved widespread commercial success, consistently topping Italian music charts and gaining recognition across Europe.
Her repertoire was notably diverse, blending genres such as rock and roll, swing, soul, and traditional ballads. Among her most iconic songs are “Il cielo in una stanza” and “Parole parole,” both of which remain classics in Italian music history. Mina was also known for her bold stage presence and her ability to reinterpret international musical styles into her own distinctive sound.
Despite her immense po

item.title: Frog-based milk preservation in Russia and Finland and its link to antimicrobial skin compounds
item.description:
Before refrigeration became widely available, rural communities in Russia and Finland developed practical methods to keep milk from spoiling. One of the most unusual practices involved placing a live brown frog (Rana temporaria) directly into containers of fresh milk. This tradition, documented in the 19th and early 20th centuries, was passed down through generations as an empirical solution to a common food preservation problem. People observed that milk lasted longer when a frog was present, although they did not understand the scientific mechanism behind it.
Modern scientific research has since provided an explanation for this phenomenon. A study conducted around 2012–2013 at Moscow State University, led by Dr. Albert Lebedev, analyzed the skin secretions of the brown frog. Researchers found that the frog’s skin produces antimicrobial peptides, natural compounds with antibacterial and antifungal properties. Among these compounds, substances such as Brevinin 1Tb were shown to inhibi

item.title: NYT reports Trump remarks on Sen. Susan Collins could influence 2026 Senate race dynamics
item.description:
A New York Times report highlighted how a past public statement by President Donald Trump could become a significant factor in the 2026 U.S. Senate elections. According to the report, Trump’s January comments targeting Republican Senator Susan Collins of Maine may inadvertently provide Democrats with a strategic advantage in one of the most competitive Senate races. After Collins voted with Democrats to limit Trump’s war powers following military action in Venezuela, Trump publicly stated on Truth Social that she “should never be elected to office again.”
The New York Times suggests that Democrats could later use this quote in campaign messaging, not necessarily to convince Republican voters to switch parties, but to discourage Republican turnout in Maine. Political strategists often view voter demoralization as a powerful electoral tool in closely contested races. Collins currently holds a Senate seat that Democrats need to flip if they want to regain control of the chamber, making h

item.title: Uganda Confirms Two Additional Ebola Cases Linked to Congo Outbreak
item.description:
Ugandan health authorities announced two new Ebola infections on May 25, 2026, increasing the total number of confirmed cases in the country to seven. Officials said all identified cases are connected to the ongoing Ebola outbreak in neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo, where authorities officially declared the outbreak on May 15. The situation in Uganda began after a 59-year-old Congolese man traveled to Kampala for medical treatment on May 11 and died three days later before doctors confirmed he had Ebola. Additional Congolese nationals seeking treatment in Uganda later tested positive for the virus.
Uganda previously confirmed its first locally transmitted infections involving a driver and a health worker who had contact with the deceased patient. The latest cases involve two additional health workers at a private hospital in Kampala. According to health officials, both patients were admitted to a specialized treatment unit and are receiving medical care.
President Yoweri Mus

item.title: HR professional Portia Napaaba honored for leadership in mining, manufacturing, and energy sectors
item.description:
Portia Napaaba, a Senior Professional in Human Resources International (SPHRi), has been recognized by the HR Certification Centre (HRCC) for her significant contributions to human resource development across the mining, manufacturing, and energy sectors. The recognition was part of the 2026 International HR Day celebrations, which highlighted outstanding HR professionals for their impact on workplace transformation and community development. Napaaba’s career spans over a decade of hands-on HR leadership, during which she has built a strong reputation for advancing talent development, employee engagement, and organizational effectiveness.
Her professional philosophy emphasizes that Human Resources is more than an administrative function; instead, it is a strategic driver that shapes organizational culture, enhances productivity, and contributes to sustainable business success. Beyond her corporate responsibilities, Napaaba is actively involved in youth empowerment and mentorship. She

item.title: How tax burdens compare across wealthy countries and what it means for middle-class workers
item.description:
This opinion piece from the Washington Post Editorial Board examines how tax burdens vary across wealthy countries, focusing on comparisons between the United States and other members of the OECD. It explains the concept of the “tax wedge,” which includes income taxes and social security contributions paid by both employees and employers. Using an example of a U.S. worker earning around $79,000 in total labor costs, the article shows how roughly 30 percent of compensation is absorbed by taxes and payroll contributions before reaching take-home pay.
The piece argues that while many Americans look to European countries for inspiration in building stronger welfare systems, those systems are often financed not primarily by taxing the rich but by broad-based taxes on middle-income workers. It notes that average tax rates across OECD countries have risen for multiple consecutive years, reaching a decade high of 35.1 percent in 2025. In many cases, governments are increasing revenue through

item.title: The Artistic Legacy of Jean-Jacques Grandville and His Anthropomorphic Illustrations
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday explores the life and artistic contributions of French illustrator and caricaturist Jean-Jacques Grandville, born Jean Ignace Isidore Gérard in 1803. Grandville became one of the most influential visual satirists of the 19th century, particularly during the Romantic era in France. He gained recognition for his imaginative and highly detailed illustrations that combined human and animal characteristics, creating striking anthropomorphic figures used to critique politics, society, and cultural behavior during the July Monarchy.
His artwork frequently appeared in prominent satirical publications such as La Caricature and Le Charivari, where he used humor and fantasy to comment on social customs and political issues. The article highlights Grandville’s ability to blend surreal visual concepts with sharp satire, making him an important precursor to later artistic movements such as Surrealism.
Among his most celebrated works are his illustrations for La Fo

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight the Making of Léon: The Professional and the Early Career of Natalie Portman
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday revisits the production of the 1994 cult classic “Léon: The Professional” through a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs featuring actors Jean Reno and Natalie Portman. Directed and written by Luc Besson, the French neo-noir action thriller became widely recognized for its emotional storytelling, memorable performances, and distinctive visual style. The film marked Natalie Portman’s movie debut when she was only 12 years old, introducing audiences to a young actress whose performance received critical praise for its maturity and emotional depth.
The story centers on Léon, a solitary professional hitman living in New York City, portrayed by Jean Reno. After the murder of her family by corrupt DEA agents led by the violent Norman Stansfield, played by Gary Oldman, Mathilda seeks refuge with Léon. Over time, the two develop an unusual but meaningful bond as Léon reluctantly becomes her protector and mentor. The film explores themes such as lonelin

item.title: Trump criticizes GOP senators and congressman amid internal party tensions over Iran negotiations
item.description:
President Donald Trump sparked controversy after posting a lengthy early-morning message on Truth Social in which he directly attacked several Republican lawmakers who have criticized his approach to negotiations with Iran. In the post, written around 6 a.m., Trump named Sen. Thom Tillis of North Carolina, Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana, and Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky, using harsh language to describe them and accusing them of undermining his political agenda.
Trump suggested that Tillis would soon be out of office, while referencing Cassidy’s recent primary defeat to a Trump-backed challenger. He was especially critical of Massie, whom he labeled with derogatory terms and claimed had been defeated after repeatedly breaking with him politically. The president also used the post to broadly attack critics within the Republican Party, labeling them as ineffective and divisive figures who fail to understand his ongoing diplomatic efforts with Iran.
The comments come amid heightened d

item.title: Historic 1945 Photograph Captures Jewish Mother and Daughter After Liberation From Bergen-Belsen Evacuation Train
item.description:
The article recounts the story behind a famous World War II photograph taken during the final days of Nazi Germany in April 1945. As Allied forces advanced across Germany, the SS attempted to evacuate prisoners from concentration camps in order to conceal evidence of Nazi crimes. Between April 6 and April 10, 1945, several trains carrying thousands of Jewish prisoners departed from the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp toward the Theresienstadt ghetto in occupied Czechoslovakia.
One of these trains became stranded near Magdeburg after days of travel under horrific conditions, with little food, water, or sanitation available to the prisoners. Many of the people onboard belonged to Bergen-Belsen’s so-called “exchange” section, where prisoners with foreign citizenship or documents from neutral countries were kept alive as potential bargaining tools by the Nazi regime. Unlike many concentration camp prisoners, they wore civilian clothing instead of striped uniforms.
As American forces app

item.title: Megyn Kelly Faces Backlash After Discussing Allegations About Trump’s Marriages on Podcast
item.description:
Political commentator Megyn Kelly drew widespread criticism and debate after making controversial remarks about Donald Trump’s personal life during an appearance on the Hodgetwins Podcast. In the discussion, Kelly claimed that Trump had been unfaithful in each of his marriages and revisited allegations made during Trump’s 1990 divorce proceedings with his first wife, Ivana Trump. Ivana had previously accused Trump of rape during the divorce dispute but later clarified that she did not intend the statement to be interpreted in a literal or criminal sense. Kelly acknowledged that she could not independently verify the allegation.
Kelly also questioned Trump’s faithfulness in his current marriage to Melania Trump, making comments that quickly circulated online and generated strong reactions from across the political spectrum. Far-right activist Laura Loomer sharply criticized Kelly, accusing her of opportunism and attacking her past involvement with Trump-related events.
Other conservat

item.title: Fashion Photography of Arik Népo in the Early 1950s
item.description:
Arik Népo (1913–1961) was a highly influential fashion and portrait photographer whose work helped shape the visual identity of mid-20th century fashion imagery. Born in Moscow, he later became active in major fashion hubs such as Paris and New York, where he developed a distinguished career spanning from the 1930s through the 1950s. His photographs frequently appeared in leading publications like Vogue, showcasing haute couture designs from prominent fashion houses including Maggy Rouff, Lucien Lelong, Hermès, Lanvin-Castillo, Schiaparelli, and Jacques Fath.
Népo was recognized for his refined photographic style, characterized by dramatic yet elegant posing, careful composition, and a strong emphasis on texture and garment structure. His black-and-white gelatin silver prints are especially valued today for their ability to capture both the sophistication of post-war fashion and the cultural aesthetics of the era. He often worked with well-known models such as Bettina Graziani, Jackie

item.title: 8-year-old discovers 1,700-year-old Roman statuette fragment in Israel’s Negev Desert
item.description:
An 8-year-old boy visiting southern Israel made an unexpected archaeological discovery when he found a small Roman-era statuette fragment during a family trip to the Ramon Crater in the Negev Desert. The child, identified as Dor Wolynitz from Rehovot, was participating in a weekend retreat organized by a paratrooper reserve unit when he noticed an unusual stone while exploring the area. Intrigued by its striped appearance and shape, he picked it up and later showed it to an archaeologist friend of his family.
Experts from the Israel Antiquities Authority (IAA) confirmed that the object is a rare fragment of a 1,700-year-old figurine dating back to the fourth century A.D. The piece, measuring approximately six by six centimeters, depicts part of a human figure wearing finely sculpted drapery. Archaeologists believe it was made from a local phosphorite-type mineral, suggesting it was crafted in the region rather than imported.
Researchers noted that the craftsmanship indicates a high l

item.title: Privacy-Focused Gay Dating Apps Launch as Alternatives to Grindr Amid User Backlash
item.description: As concerns grow over data privacy, monetization tactics, and bot infiltration on mainstream gay dating platforms like Grindr, several new apps are positioning themselves as community-driven alternatives. Grindr, boasting 15 million monthly active users, faces criticism for heavy advertising and premium subscriptions, including a new $500 monthly tier. Similarly, Sniffies drew backlash after receiving a $100 million investment from Match Group. In response, entrepreneurs are developing apps that prioritize user control and privacy. MeetMarket, launched in March by Calum Bowden, operates as a web app built on a decentralized identity system. It stores no user emails, passwords, or personal data centrally, offers end-to-end encrypted messaging, and remains ad-free. Users maintain full ownership of their information on their devices. Within days of launch, it attracted over 12,000 sign-ups and has since reached 60,000 users. Bowden emphasizes its suitability for queer individuals in poten

item.title: Boots Riley Comments on Cannes Film Festival Rejections of His Projects and Selection Choices
item.description:
Filmmaker and musician Boots Riley has publicly commented on what he describes as repeated rejections from the Cannes Film Festival. Speaking on social media, Riley claimed that Cannes did not select his films and television projects, including his 2018 feature debut “Sorry to Bother You,” his recent crime comedy “I Love Boosters,” and his 2023 series “I’m a Virgo.” He also noted that, in his view, the festival chose other works such as “The Idol” instead of his own projects, suggesting a pattern in their programming decisions regarding his work.
Riley’s remarks came during the promotional period for his new film “I Love Boosters,” which premiered in theaters on May 22. The film is an absurdist crime comedy centered on a group of characters who steal high-fashion items from wealthy individuals and resell them at lower prices, acting as a kind of modern Robin Hood narrative. The movie features an ensemble cast including Keke Palmer, Naomi Ackie, Taylour Paige, Poppy Liu, Eiza González,

item.title: Mid-20th Century Christmas Morning Gifts in 1950s–1960s Photos
item.description: The article presents a nostalgic look at Christmas mornings during the mid-20th century, particularly the 1950s and 1960s, using a collection of vintage photographs that capture people with their holiday gifts. It highlights how the post-war economic boom and the rise of mass consumer culture shaped the experience of Christmas during this era. For children, Christmas morning was filled with excitement as they unwrapped iconic toys that defined the period. Boys commonly received items such as Matchbox cars, Erector Sets, and later early G.I. Joe action figures, while girls often received Barbie dolls, Chatty Cathy dolls, or miniature Easy-Bake Ovens. Simple yet engaging toys like Silly Putty, Play-Doh, and Etch A Sketch also reflected the importance of imagination in play during that time. The article also explores adult gift-giving traditions, which often emphasized practicality and emerging modern comforts. Women frequently received household appliances like toasters, blenders, or ele

item.title: Economic confidence decline raises concerns for Republicans ahead of the midterm elections
item.description:
A recent Gallup poll indicating a sharp drop in public confidence about the U.S. economy has sparked concern among Republican lawmakers and political analysts as the country approaches the midterm elections. According to the poll, only 16% of Americans rate current economic conditions as excellent or good, while 49% describe them as poor. In addition, 76% of respondents believe the economy is getting worse, marking the highest level of pessimism since 2023. Just 20% think conditions are improving.
The report, discussed by MS NOW commentators Ali Vitali and Jonathan Lemire, suggests that economic dissatisfaction could pose a significant political challenge for Republicans, who are widely expected to be judged on “kitchen table” issues such as inflation and household costs during the election cycle. Lemire emphasized that economic performance has historically played a decisive role in midterm outcomes.
Vitali added that while Republican leadership publicly maintains optimism, private c

item.title: Analyst says Trump’s criticism of Obama-era Iran deal highlights similarities between nuclear agreements
item.description:
Veteran diplomat and foreign policy analyst Richard Haass argued that Donald Trump’s repeated criticism of Barack Obama’s 2015 Iran nuclear agreement may inadvertently highlight how similar the two administrations’ approaches to Iran have been. Speaking in response to Trump’s social media attacks on the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), Haass suggested that both the Obama-era deal and Trump’s current approach aim to impose limits on Iran’s nuclear program rather than fully eliminate it. According to Haass, neither framework would completely remove Iran from nuclear development but instead would place a “ceiling” on its capabilities.
Haass further stated that the diplomatic situation may have worsened over time, noting that a deal reportedly offered to Trump’s negotiators, including Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff, earlier in the year could have matched or even exceeded the terms now achievable through renewed negotiations. He described the current trajectory as potentially l

item.title: The 1984 John Boulware Crash Photo and the Controversy of the “Screaming Spirit” Image
item.description:
The article recounts a tragic 1984 car accident involving 16-year-old John Boulware, who was a passenger in a vehicle traveling with three friends to a Prince concert in St. Paul, Minnesota. On December 27, the driver lost control of the car, which crashed head-on into a tree near the southeast corner of Highland Park Golf Course. John Boulware died instantly in the collision, while the other three teenagers survived with only minor injuries.
Following standard procedure, police officer Brian Coyle documented the scene using a 35mm film camera. When the photographs were later developed, one image appeared to show an unusual blurred shape resembling a human face above the vehicle’s driver-side area. The distorted figure, with an open mouth suggesting a scream, was quickly interpreted by some viewers as resembling the deceased teenager. This image became widely known as “The Screaming Spirit” or “The Screaming Soul.”
The photograph sparked debate and fascination over the decades. Paran

item.title: Environmental coalition proposes expanding local water supply in Southern California to 85% by 2045, opposing Delta tunnel project
item.description:
A coalition of environmental and conservation groups is urging Southern California to dramatically increase its reliance on locally sourced water, arguing that the region could meet up to 85% of its water needs locally by 2045. Currently, about half of Southern California’s water is imported from the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta and the Colorado River, both of which are facing increasing stress from climate change, drought, and ecological degradation. The groups released a 34-page strategy calling for expanded investment in water recycling, stormwater capture, groundwater cleanup, and conservation measures as an alternative to large-scale infrastructure projects that transport water over long distances.
A central point of contention is the proposed Delta Conveyance Project, a 45-mile tunnel beneath the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, which state officials estimate could cost over $20 billion, though critics warn the final cost could be several times higher. The coalition argues that a c

item.title: Trump Pressures Middle Eastern Nations to Join Abraham Accords as Part of Iran Deal
item.description:
Former President Donald Trump announced a controversial diplomatic demand directed at several Muslim-majority countries in the Middle East and South Asia, including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, and Bahrain. In a post published on Truth Social, Trump stated that these nations should join the Abraham Accords as a condition for being included in any future agreement involving Iran. The Abraham Accords are normalization agreements between Israel and several Arab nations that were initiated during Trump’s presidency.
Trump argued that countries refusing to sign the accords demonstrate “bad intention” and should therefore be excluded from broader regional negotiations.
