The exchange stemmed from Bowden’s criticism regarding Loomer’s connection to sensitive information allegedly linked to RFK Jr. Loomer pushed back against the characterization, arguing that the interaction consisted of only a small number of messages and rejecting claims of harassment or emotional escalation. She responded sharply, accusing Bowden of misrepresentation and using insults during the exchange, while insi

item.title: A curated photo collection highlighting Sissy Spacek’s career and film roles during the 1980s
item.description:
The article presents a curated collection of photographs featuring American actress and singer Sissy Spacek during the 1980s, a defining decade in her long and successful career. Spacek, born in 1949, is widely recognized for her acclaimed performances in both film and music, earning numerous awards including an Academy Award, Golden Globes, and multiple nominations across major entertainment institutions. The piece highlights how the 1980s solidified her reputation as one of Hollywood’s leading actresses.
A major focus of the article is Spacek’s Oscar-winning role as country music legend Loretta Lynn in 'Coal Miner’s Daughter' (1980), a performance in which she also provided her own singing vocals. The success of this role not only earned her an Academy Award for Best Actress but also led to a Grammy nomination for the film’s soundtrack. Following this breakthrough, she expanded her artistic work by releasing her own country music album, 'Hangin’ Up My Heart' in 1983.
Throughout the

item.title: The 1959 Chevrolet El Camino and Its Role in Mid-Century American Automotive Design
item.description:
The article examines the 1959 Chevrolet El Camino as an iconic example of late-1950s American automotive design, highlighting its blend of utility and style during a period of strong industrial optimism. Introduced as Chevrolet’s answer to the Ford Ranchero, the El Camino was built on the Brookwood station wagon platform, combining the practicality of a pickup truck with the comfort and visual appeal of a passenger car. The 1959 model is especially noted for its dramatic styling cues, including the bold “batwing” rear fins and distinctive cat-eye taillights that defined Chevrolet’s design language at the time.
The vehicle’s low, streamlined profile and wrap-around windshield contributed to its futuristic appearance, reflecting the broader “Space Age” aesthetic that influenced American consumer products in the late 1950s. Extensive chrome detailing further emphasized its role not only as a functional utility vehicle but also as a statement of style and modernity. The El Camino’s design

item.title: Ro Khanna suggests Trump is putting JD Vance in politically difficult positions
item.description:
Rep. Ro Khanna, a Democratic congressman, raised concerns during a recent NBC News appearance that former President Donald Trump may be deliberately placing Vice President JD Vance in politically risky and difficult positions. Khanna’s remarks came after questions about Vance’s defense of controversial administration actions, including an alleged IRS-related “Jan. 6 slush fund,” which has drawn criticism and political scrutiny. According to Khanna, Vance is repeatedly tasked with publicly defending some of the administration’s most contentious policies, including issues related to foreign policy such as tensions involving Iran, as well as other politically sensitive controversies like the so-called Epstein-related funding claims and broader criticisms of elite political connections referred to by critics as the “Epstein class.”
Khanna suggested that this pattern could be interpreted as intentional political “sabotage,” arguing that Vance is being positioned to defend policies that may

item.title: Martin Sheen Endorses Chris Pappas in Competitive New Hampshire Senate Race
item.description:
Actor Martin Sheen, widely recognized for portraying fictional President Josiah Bartlet on the television series 'The West Wing,' has endorsed Democratic candidate Chris Pappas in New Hampshire’s closely watched Senate race. Sheen highlighted the symbolic connection between his TV character and the state, since Bartlet was depicted as a former governor from New Hampshire. In his endorsement, Sheen emphasized his long history of political activism and framed the election as an important contest for the future direction of American democracy.
The Senate race is considered one of the most significant contests of the 2026 midterm elections. Polling reportedly shows a highly competitive race, with Democrats viewing New Hampshire as a crucial opportunity in their effort to gain control of the Senate. The article also notes that President Donald Trump recently endorsed Pappas’s Republican opponent, making the race a direct political test of Trump’s influence during the midterm cycle.
The pi

item.title: Los Angeles voters to decide City Council races in eight districts, including two open seats
item.description: Los Angeles voters are set to participate in the June 2 primary election, casting ballots in eight of the city’s 15 City Council districts, including two races for open seats due to term limits. The most competitive and heavily financed contests include District 11, where incumbent Councilmember Traci Park faces challenger Faizah Malik. Park has raised about $1.3 million, while Malik has raised roughly $520,000, with millions more spent by independent expenditure committees. The race has centered on issues such as housing development, wildfire recovery in the Westside, and policing levels, including debates over LAPD staffing targets and the future of projects like the Venice Dell housing proposal. In District 9, six candidates are competing to replace outgoing Councilmember Curren Price, with divisions over police funding and homelessness enforcement policies such as Municipal Code 41.18. District 3 features three candidates vying for the seat being vacated by term-limited Bob Blumenf

item.title: Trump Signals Continued U.S. Pressure on Iran as Nuclear Deal Negotiations Progress Amid Regional Tensions
item.description:
The live coverage reports on escalating diplomatic and military tensions in the Middle East as the United States continues negotiations with Iran over a potential nuclear-related agreement. U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the blockade on Iranian ports will remain in “full force” until a formal deal is reached, emphasizing that talks are ongoing but should not be rushed. He characterized the negotiations as “orderly and constructive,” while insisting that Iran must not develop or obtain nuclear weapons under any circumstances.
The report also highlights that, according to U.S. officials and media sources, a final agreement is not expected to be signed immediately due to unresolved technical and political details, with approvals potentially taking several days. Meanwhile, Secretary of State Marco Rubio described diplomatic progress as “significant,” presenting the United States as a stabilizing force internationally during remarks made in India.
Regional dynamics remain highl

item.title: Geraldine Chaplin’s Lasting Influence on International Art-House Cinema
item.description:
The article reflects on the career and artistic legacy of Geraldine Chaplin, emphasizing her importance in both Hollywood and European cinema. As the daughter of legendary filmmaker and actor Charlie Chaplin, she inherited a connection to film history but built a distinguished career through her own talent and artistic choices. The piece highlights her unique screen presence, shaped in part by her classical ballet training, which contributed to her graceful and emotionally expressive performances.
Geraldine Chaplin first gained widespread international recognition for her role as Tonya in David Lean’s 1965 epic film Doctor Zhivago. Her performance introduced her to global audiences and established her as a serious dramatic actress. However, the article notes that her most significant artistic achievements emerged through her collaborations with acclaimed Spanish director Carlos Saura. Working together on several films, including the celebrated 1976 drama Cria Cuervos, Chaplin became c

item.title: Waymo Faces Operational Challenges as Robotaxi Expansion Continues Across U.S. Cities
item.description:
This edition of TechCrunch Mobility focuses on the current state of the robotaxi industry, highlighting the gap between public perception and the operational reality faced by autonomous vehicle companies. While Waymo has expanded robotaxi services into multiple U.S. cities and appears to be leading the commercial autonomous driving market, the company continues to encounter significant technical limitations. Heavy rain, flooded roads, and construction zones have forced Waymo to pause or restrict robotaxi operations in several cities including Atlanta, Dallas, Houston, Austin, Nashville, San Francisco, Los Angeles, Phoenix, and Miami. These interruptions demonstrate that large-scale deployment of autonomous vehicles still depends heavily on environmental conditions and unresolved edge cases. The article argues that commercial launch does not necessarily mean the technology is fully mature.
The newsletter also examines the growing interconnectedness of Elon Musk’s business empire follow

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson accused of blocking war powers vote related to U.S. policy on Iran
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson has been accused of abruptly removing a war powers resolution from the House floor after it appeared to have enough bipartisan support to pass. According to Representative Greg Meeks, the ranking Democrat on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, lawmakers had already secured sufficient Republican backing to advance the measure, which aimed to assert congressional authority over ongoing U.S. military actions connected to the conflict involving Iran. Meeks claimed that Johnson intervened directly to halt the vote, stating that the Speaker pulled the resolution from consideration and indicated it would be revisited after the congressional recess. Meeks described the decision as a deliberate effort to prevent Congress from constraining presidential military authority, particularly in relation to former President Donald Trump’s Iran policy and military engagement. He asserted that the bipartisan coalition supporting the measure demonstrated that there was enough po

item.title: Hands-on review of Amazon’s Bee AI wearable highlights productivity benefits and privacy concerns
item.description:
A TechCrunch reviewer tested Bee, an AI-powered wearable wrist device recently updated after Amazon’s acquisition of the startup behind it. The gadget functions as a personal assistant that continuously or selectively records conversations, then generates transcriptions and AI-written summaries through a connected mobile app. Users can manually control recording via a physical button, with a green indicator light signaling when audio capture is active. The system can also sync with calendars to deliver reminders and contextual alerts, positioning it as a productivity tool for managing meetings and daily schedules.
In practice, the reviewer found Bee particularly useful in professional settings. During business calls, it produced structured summaries that helped recall discussion points without needing to replay full recordings. However, the device’s transcription quality was imperfect, occasionally missing sections of dialogue and struggling to correctly identify speakers, sometimes r

item.title: Duke University researchers restore nerve function in chronic pain models using mitochondrial transfer
item.description:
Researchers at Duke University School of Medicine have identified a potential new strategy for treating chronic nerve pain by restoring healthy mitochondria in damaged nerves. The study, published in Nature, focused on peripheral neuropathy linked to diabetes and chemotherapy, conditions that often cause severe pain, tingling, and numbness. Scientists have long suspected that malfunctioning mitochondria, which generate energy inside cells, contribute to nerve degeneration and chronic pain.
Using both mouse models and human tissue samples, the research team demonstrated that transferring healthy mitochondria into damaged sensory neurons significantly reduced pain-related behaviors. In some experiments, the relief lasted up to 48 hours. Rather than blocking pain signals temporarily, the approach aims to repair the underlying cellular damage by restoring the energy supply needed for normal nerve function.
The researchers discovered that satellite glial cells, which support sensory neuro

item.title: Democrats elevate Epstein-related allegations as a campaign strategy in the 2026 midterms
item.description:
A report from Axios, republished by Raw Story, describes how Democratic candidates and strategists are increasingly incorporating references to the Jeffrey Epstein case into their messaging ahead of the 2026 U.S. midterm elections. What was once viewed within parts of the Democratic Party as an unlikely or politically risky line of attack has now become a more prominent campaign tool in competitive races.
Representative Ro Khanna, who has advocated for the release of the full Epstein files, argued that earlier skepticism from political insiders underestimated voter concern about perceived inequality and elite privilege. He suggested that the Epstein case resonates with broader public frustration about a system perceived as favoring wealthy and powerful individuals.
Several Democratic campaigns are now using Epstein-related messaging in advertising. In Ohio’s Senate race, ads supporting Sherrod Brown have focused on ties between Republican Senator Jon Husted and individuals connected

item.title: Lenovo, Dell, and HP Commit Major Funding to the Linux Vendor Firmware Service
item.description:
The Linux Vendor Firmware Service (LVFS), a platform that allows hardware manufacturers to securely distribute firmware updates to Linux users, has received major financial backing from Lenovo, Dell, and HP. According to the report, all three companies have joined as Premier sponsors, each contributing approximately $100,000 per year to help sustain and expand the project. LVFS was originally developed in 2015 by GNOME maintainer Richard Hughes and has become a critical part of the Linux ecosystem by simplifying firmware updates for laptops, desktops, and other hardware devices.
Before LVFS, Linux users often had difficulty obtaining firmware updates directly from manufacturers, with many relying on unofficial third-party sources or complicated manual installation methods. The service provides a centralized and secure distribution platform that integrates with tools such as GNOME Software and fwupdmgr, allowing firmware updates to be delivered in a way similar to operating system upda

item.title: Orange County evacuates 50,000 residents amid overheating chemical tank at aerospace facility
item.description:
About 50,000 residents in Orange County, California, were ordered to evacuate after authorities warned of a potential explosion involving an overheated chemical tank at a GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove. The incident began Thursday when officials responded to a vapor release from a storage tank containing methyl methacrylate (MMA), a chemical used in industrial manufacturing that can cause respiratory and skin irritation if exposed. Fire officials reported that the tank’s internal temperature continued to rise after its relief systems activated but failed to fully cool the contents due to clogged valves, reaching around 90 degrees Fahrenheit. MMA is typically stable at much lower temperatures, and officials warned that continued heating could lead to a leak or catastrophic explosion.
Emergency crews attempted to stabilize the situation by using a method described as freezing the chemical from the outside inward, similar to forming an ice cube, though authorities acknowledged th

item.title: Aleardo Villa and the Art Nouveau Posters of the Belle Époque
item.description:
Aleardo Villa (1865–1906) was an Italian painter and illustrator whose work became closely associated with the elegance and refinement of the Belle Époque period. He studied at the prestigious Brera Academy in Milan, where he initially trained as a traditional painter before shifting his focus toward illustration and commercial art. Over time, Villa emerged as one of the leading figures of the Italian Art Nouveau movement, known locally as Stile Liberty.
His artistic style is characterized by a delicate, idealized portrayal of women, often depicted with flowing garments, soft expressions, and surrounded by floral and ornamental motifs. These compositions emphasized beauty, harmony, and an ethereal atmosphere, reflecting the broader aesthetic values of the period. Beyond fine art, Villa gained significant recognition for his contributions to advertising and graphic design, an emerging field at the turn of the 20th century.
He created iconic posters for major commercial brands such as

item.title: Legal experts and judges warn of severe strain on U.S. rule of law amid concerns over presidential power
item.description:
A Los Angeles Times column reports on growing concerns among legal experts, including federal judges, attorneys, and law professors, about the state of the U.S. rule of law during President Donald Trump’s administration. The piece is based on a survey conducted by Bright Line Watch and UCLA’s Safeguarding Democracy Project, which gathered responses from judges, legal scholars, political scientists, and members of the public. Many respondents expressed alarm over what they perceive as executive overreach, politicization of the Justice Department, and weakening compliance with judicial rulings.
According to the survey findings, a significant majority of legal experts believe federal officials frequently fail to comply with court orders, and many also suspect political appointees in the Justice Department of misleading judges. The article highlights concerns that the Supreme Court and Congress have not sufficiently checked presidential power, contributing to what some respondents describ

item.title: Critique of Small Modular Nuclear Reactors as a Viable Energy Solution
item.description:
The article argues that small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs), despite growing interest from governments, tech companies, and some environmental advocates, are unlikely to solve the core problems of nuclear energy or the global energy crisis. It begins by describing the renewed enthusiasm for nuclear power driven by climate goals, rising electricity demand from artificial intelligence data centers, and geopolitical energy disruptions. SMRs are promoted as cheaper, safer, and faster to build than conventional nuclear plants, with backing from major investors and political support in several countries.
However, the author contends that these claims are largely unproven. Historically, nuclear power has faced persistent issues including extremely high construction costs, long build times, unresolved radioactive waste storage, uranium resource constraints, safety risks, and vulnerability to water scarcity and climate-related disasters. The article argues that SMRs do not fundamentally reso

item.title: Michael Flynn warns Trump against pursuing Iran nuclear negotiations, claims regime is deceptive
item.description:
Retired Lt. Gen. Michael Flynn, a prominent former Trump national security adviser and long-time supporter of the former president, publicly urged Donald Trump to abandon ongoing nuclear negotiations with Iran. In a message posted on X, Flynn directly warned Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio that Iran is not negotiating in good faith, stating that the Iranian government is “lying” to U.S. officials and should not be trusted in diplomatic talks. Flynn argued that any potential agreement would be meaningless unless Iran’s regional influence networks, including groups such as Hezbollah, Hamas, and the Houthis, are fully dismantled.
While acknowledging Trump’s stated desire to avoid prolonged conflict and bring stability to the Middle East, Flynn insisted that a negotiated settlement would ultimately fail if it allowed the current Iranian regime to remain in power. He also raised concerns about reports suggesting a potential financial component in the emerging deal, referencing a p

item.title: Backlash from Trump supporters over Iran negotiations highlights internal tensions within his political base
item.description:
A recent Truth Social post by Donald Trump praising ongoing negotiations with Iran as “productive and professional” has sparked an unexpected backlash from within his own supporter base. Rather than criticism coming primarily from political opponents, many of Trump’s most loyal followers expressed anger and frustration, arguing that any diplomatic engagement with Iran represents a repeat of past U.S. foreign policy mistakes. Some commenters invoked comparisons to the Obama administration’s 2015 nuclear deal, insisting that Iran cannot be trusted under any agreement and calling instead for a more aggressive military approach, including regime change or the destruction of Iranian nuclear capabilities.
The reactions on Trump’s platform reflected a deeper ideological divide among his supporters regarding foreign policy. While Trump emphasized that any potential agreement would be “the exact opposite” of Obama-era diplomacy and insisted that sanctions and pressure on Iran would remain in p

item.title: Evidence suggests intermediate-mass black holes form through repeated mergers in dense star clusters
item.description:
Astrophysicists have found new evidence explaining the origin of so-called “impossible” black holes—objects whose masses fall between roughly 40 and 100 times the mass of the Sun. These black holes are too large to be formed by the collapse of individual stars, yet too small to be classified as supermassive black holes found at galactic centers. For years, their existence puzzled scientists because standard stellar evolution models could not account for them.
A new international study analyzing gravitational wave data provides a compelling explanation. Using observations from leading gravitational wave observatories, researchers examined 153 confirmed black hole merger events. Among these, 34 involved unusually massive black holes. By comparing their properties, scientists identified two distinct populations. Lower-mass black holes—up to about 40 solar masses—tended to have aligned, low spins consistent with formation from collapsing stars. However, above roughly 45 solar masses, bla

item.title: Archival Photographs of Metallica’s Early Years at Their El Cerrito Headquarters (1983–1986)
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday presents a curated set of candid photographs documenting Metallica during a crucial formative period between 1983 and 1986 at their informal headquarters known as the “MetalliMansion,” located at 3132 Carlson Boulevard in El Cerrito, California. After forming in Los Angeles in 1981, Metallica relocated to the San Francisco Bay Area in 1983, a move influenced by bassist Cliff Burton, who agreed to join the band only if they established themselves in his home region. The rented house became both a residence and creative hub for James Hetfield, Lars Ulrich, and their collaborators. Within this modest property, the garage emerged as the band’s most important space, functioning as a rehearsal and songwriting environment where much of the material for two landmark albums—Ride the Lightning (1984) and Master of Puppets (1986)—was developed. The article highlights how this era captured Metallica’s transition from a little-known underground act into one of heav

item.title: Democratic Lawmaker Forecasts Growing Republican Distance From Trump Ahead of Midterms
item.description:
A senior House Democrat has predicted that internal divisions within the Republican Party will deepen in the coming months, particularly as lawmakers return to their districts during congressional recess and face direct pressure from constituents. Rep. Greg Meeks of New York, the ranking member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, argued that many Republican members of Congress will increasingly distance themselves from Donald Trump once they confront local voter concerns and move past primary elections. Speaking in a televised interview, Meeks suggested that while some Republicans remain firmly aligned with Trump, others—especially retiring members or those feeling politically freer—are beginning to question his influence and policy direction.
Meeks pointed to recent Senate pushback against a proposed $1.8 billion fund, which critics claim could indirectly benefit individuals linked to the January 6 Capitol attack, as an early indication of fractures within the GOP. He described R

item.title: Free Software Foundation reports 46 LibreLocal meetups across six continents, with more events planned
item.description:
The Free Software Foundation (FSF) announced that its global initiative encouraging community meetups, called LibreLocals, has seen strong international participation. As part of LibreLocal month in May 2026, free software supporters organized 46 in-person events across six continents, with additional meetups still being scheduled. The initiative was designed to bring together advocates of free and open-source software to exchange ideas, collaborate, and promote software freedom principles.
According to the FSF, the events reflect a growing global interest in digital rights and software freedom. Participants gathered in local communities to discuss free software philosophy, share technical knowledge, and build collaborative projects. The FSF also highlighted that some events received financial support from the organization, while many others were independently organized by volunteers who contributed additional effort to expand the movement.
FSF executive leadership emphasized that th

item.title: Study suggests banana-containing smoothies may reduce absorption of beneficial flavanols
item.description:
A study from the University of California, Davis suggests that common smoothie combinations may significantly influence how the body absorbs beneficial plant compounds. Researchers found that adding bananas to fruit smoothies can sharply reduce the absorption of flavanols, natural compounds linked to heart and brain health. The effect is attributed to polyphenol oxidase (PPO), an enzyme found in high amounts in bananas that triggers browning reactions in fruits. This enzyme appears to break down flavanols found in berries, grapes, cocoa, and other fruits before they can be fully absorbed by the body.
In controlled experiments, participants consumed different smoothie types, including banana-based smoothies, mixed berry smoothies, and flavanol capsules as a control. Blood and urine analysis showed that those who consumed banana smoothies had up to 84% lower flavanol levels compared with the control group. In contrast, berry-based smoothies with low PPO activity resulted in flavanol lev

item.title: Experts Advise Grandparents to Avoid Certain Common Phrases That May Harm Children’s Emotional Well-Being
item.description:
The article explores how well-intentioned comments from grandparents can sometimes negatively affect their grandchildren’s emotional development, self-esteem, and sense of safety. According to pediatric and clinical psychologists, certain common phrases—though often said out of affection or habit—can unintentionally create insecurity, undermine parental authority, or contribute to unhealthy attitudes in children.
Experts emphasize that grandparents play an important role in a child’s life, but stress the need for mindful communication. One key concern is encouraging secrecy from parents, such as telling a child not to share certain information, which can weaken trust within the family and potentially expose children to unsafe situations. Another harmful category includes comments about physical appearance or weight, which may contribute to body image issues and long-term self-esteem problems.
The article also highlights the risks of commenting on eating habits, such as praising or cr

item.title: The Tradition of “Penny for the Guy” in Mid-20th Century Britain and Its Decline
item.description:
“Penny for the Guy” was once a central part of British childhood culture, especially during the mid-20th century around Bonfire Night on November 5th. Before Halloween traditions like trick-or-treating became widespread in the United Kingdom, children engaged in this communal activity by creating effigies of Guy Fawkes. These figures were made from old clothing stuffed with materials such as newspaper, straw, or leaves, and were often topped with makeshift heads made from pillows or cardboard masks. Once completed, children would transport the effigies through neighborhoods using prams, wheelbarrows, or carts, displaying them in public areas like street corners, outside shops, pubs, or transport stations. While doing so, they would ask passersby for donations by chanting phrases like “Penny for the Guy, mister?” or “Penny for the Guy, lady?”
The money collected during this activity was typically used to purchase fireworks, including sparklers, rockets, and Catherine wheels, or small t

item.title: Trump Praises Progress in Nuclear Negotiations With Iran Despite Past Hostility
item.description:
President Donald Trump said on Truth Social that relations between the United States and Iran are becoming more “professional and productive” as both countries continue negotiations over Iran’s nuclear program. Trump described the talks as constructive and orderly, emphasizing that both sides are taking time to pursue a careful and verifiable agreement. His remarks marked a notable shift in tone compared to his first administration, during which he withdrew the United States from the 2015 Iran nuclear agreement negotiated under President Barack Obama and launched a “maximum pressure” sanctions campaign against Tehran.
The article highlights the contrast between Trump’s current diplomatic language and his earlier actions toward Iran, including the 2020 drone strike that killed Iranian General Qasem Soleimani and nearly triggered a direct military conflict between the two countries. Trump also criticized the Obama-era Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, repeating his long-standing claim

item.title: Vintage Postcards Highlight Marlene Dietrich’s Iconic Style and Hollywood Career in the 1930s
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 50 vintage postcards featuring actress and singer Marlene Dietrich during the 1930s, a decade that established her as one of Hollywood’s most recognizable and influential stars. Born in Germany in 1901, Dietrich rose to international fame after starring as cabaret singer Lola Lola in the 1930 film “The Blue Angel.” Her performance and distinctive appearance quickly turned her into a global sensation and led Paramount Pictures to bring her to the United States as a rival to Greta Garbo.
The piece explains how director Josef von Sternberg carefully shaped Dietrich’s glamorous public image through lighting, costume design, and stylized cinematography in a series of Hollywood films. During this period, Dietrich became famous for her sophisticated and androgynous fashion sense, often wearing men’s suits, trench coats, and trousers at a time when such choices were considered controversial for women. The article highlights a famous 1

item.title: Hip surgeon warns against delaying medical care for persistent hip pain
item.description:
A hip surgeon interviewed in a wellness feature emphasizes the importance of not ignoring persistent hip pain, warning that delaying medical attention can lead to more serious long-term joint problems. While many people associate hip pain primarily with aging or eventual hip replacement surgery, experts note that hip issues can affect younger adults as well. Conditions such as labral tears—damage to the cartilage that helps stabilize the hip joint—are relatively common and may account for a significant share of hip pain cases, particularly among younger patients. The article explains that these injuries are often not caused by a single traumatic event but can develop gradually and go unnoticed.
Medical research cited in the report suggests that untreated hip impingement or labral tears can significantly increase the risk of developing osteoarthritis over time. One study highlighted a dramatically higher likelihood of arthritis within a decade among individuals experiencing specific hi

item.title: 1940s Photobooth Portraits Reveal the Fashion and Personality of Young Women During Wartime America
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday explores a collection of photobooth portraits featuring teenage girls and young women from the 1940s. These black-and-white images provide a candid glimpse into everyday life during a decade shaped by World War II and major social changes. Unlike formal studio photography, photobooths allowed young people to experiment with self-expression in a more relaxed and personal environment. The article describes these portraits as an early form of social media, where individuals could quickly create and share playful or emotional snapshots of themselves.
The featured images highlight many recognizable fashion trends of the era, including victory rolls, carefully styled curls, collared blouses, and modest wartime-inspired clothing. The expressions captured in the portraits vary widely, from shy smiles to confident and rebellious poses, reflecting the individuality and spirit of the young women photographed. The article emphasizes how these small photo strips p

item.title: Opinion: Political tensions and media conflicts highlight debate over Trump’s sense of humor and free speech
item.description:
This opinion piece argues that Donald Trump’s political career reflects a significant decline in his ability to engage with humor, particularly in contrast to previous U.S. presidents such as Barack Obama and Joe Biden, who are portrayed as more self-deprecating and receptive to comedic criticism. The author claims that Trump has historically struggled with satire and public roasting, referencing incidents such as his reaction to comedian Seth Meyers at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner and suggesting that this sensitivity has influenced his broader political behavior.
A major focus of the article is the relationship between Trump and late-night television, particularly allegations that he pressured media allies to take action against critics in entertainment, including Stephen Colbert and “The Late Show.” The author frames these actions as part of a broader pattern of hostility toward political satire and free expression. The piece also criticizes perceived political influence o

item.title: Mary Trump criticizes Donald Trump over comments about attending family wedding
item.description:
Mary Trump, niece of former U.S. President Donald Trump and a frequent public critic of her uncle, has once again drawn attention to the long-running family tensions by commenting on his recent remarks about attending a family wedding. According to a column published in her Substack newsletter, Mary Trump responded to her uncle’s uncertain statements regarding whether he would attend the upcoming wedding of his son, Donald Trump Jr., to Bettina Anderson in the Bahamas.
In the remarks cited by Mary Trump, the former president suggested that he might attend but described the timing as complicated due to political responsibilities and ongoing international issues, including Iran. He also implied that attending or not attending would lead to criticism either way, framing the decision as politically sensitive. Additionally, he referred to the bride in a way that Mary Trump and observers found awkward and unclear.
Mary Trump used her commentary to mock and criticize the statements, arguing

item.title: Found Color Slides Reveal a 1953 New Year’s Eve Costume Party
item.description:
The article presents a collection of rare, found color slides that document a New Year’s Eve costume party held on December 31, 1953. Shared via the Vintage Everyday blog, the images offer a vivid glimpse into mid-20th-century social life, capturing the playful and creative spirit of partygoers as they celebrated the arrival of the new year. The attendees appear dressed in a variety of homemade and imaginative costumes, ranging from superhero-inspired outfits to traditional masquerade styles, reflecting both the cultural trends and personal inventiveness of the early 1950s.
Although the slides have suffered noticeable water damage over time, the article emphasizes that the resulting imperfections add a layer of texture and historical authenticity to the images. Rather than diminishing their value, the aged and weathered appearance enhances their emotional impact, making them feel more like fragile artifacts from a distant past.
The post highlights how such visual records provide insi

item.title: Analysis of Trump’s Proposed White House Ballroom and Its Political Symbolism
item.description: This opinion article by Paul Blumenthal examines the controversy surrounding Donald Trump’s proposed White House ballroom project during his second term, framing it as a continuation of his long-standing focus on large symbolic construction projects. The piece contrasts Trump’s past promises, such as the U.S.-Mexico border wall—which he claimed would be paid for by Mexico but ultimately cost American taxpayers billions—with his current push for a massive ballroom addition to the White House complex. According to the article, the ballroom project began as a privately financed idea but quickly escalated in scale and cost, shifting from an estimated $250 million to potentially over $1 billion in taxpayer funding after revisions and political developments. The construction reportedly involved the demolition of the East Wing in 2025 and the hiring of an architecture firm, alongside the appointment of Trump-aligned officials to relevant planning bodies. The article argues that the ballroom,

item.title: Democratic Socialists host Dungeons & Dragons-themed political fundraiser in Hollywood
item.description:
A Los Angeles Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) fundraising event brought together local political candidates and fans of the tabletop role-playing game Dungeons & Dragons for an unconventional campaign gathering in Hollywood. Held at the Fonda Theatre, the event featured six DSA-endorsed candidates participating in an election-themed D&D game in front of an audience of about 400 people. The fundraiser raised approximately $30,000 for local campaigns, a modest amount compared to traditional large-scale political fundraisers in Los Angeles.
The event focused less on generating major donations and more on engaging supporters through interactive storytelling and gaming. Candidates adopted fantasy characters inspired by their political identities and roles. Among them, City Councilmember Hugo Soto-Martinez portrayed a barbarian dubbed “Hugo the Organizer,” while city attorney candidate Marissa Roy played a paladin wielding a gavel. Eunisses Hernandez took on the role of a druidic dra

item.title: Transgender Athlete AB Hernandez Qualifies for California State Track Meet in Multiple Events
item.description: Transgender athlete AB Hernandez from Jurupa Valley High School continued to make headlines at the 2026 CIF Southern Section Track & Field Masters Meet in Moorpark, California. On May 23, 2026, Hernandez secured first-place finishes in the long jump, triple jump, and high jump, earning qualification for the state championship meet in Clovis the following weekend. In the triple jump, Hernandez shared first place on the podium per CIF rules requiring co-champions in such cases. This marks the second straight year Hernandez has qualified for state in these events. Last year, the athlete won two state titles. The situation has sparked ongoing debate about fairness in girls' sports, with critics arguing biological males competing in female categories create an uneven playing field. High school girls, including Reese Hogan, have been directly affected as some missed qualification opportunities. Parents and observers expressed concerns about competitive equity, while Hernandez's mother previo

item.title: State of emergency declared in California as chemical storage tank threatens explosion in Orange County
item.description:
California Governor Gavin Newsom declared a state of emergency on Saturday in response to a developing chemical hazard in Orange County, where officials are warning of a possible explosion or major leak at an industrial facility. The emergency centers on a storage tank at a GKN Aerospace-owned site in Garden Grove containing between 6,000 and 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a highly flammable chemical used in the production of resins and plastics. According to local authorities, the tank began off-gassing and showed signs of structural instability, raising fears that it could either explode or release its contents into surrounding areas.
As a precaution, approximately 40,000 residents have been ordered to evacuate, and state agencies have been mobilized to support Orange County responders. The governor’s office of emergency services is coordinating efforts and making state-owned facilities available as temporary shelters for evacuees. Governor Newsom emphasized that protecting r

item.title: Johanna Matz, Beloved Star of 1950s Austrian Cinema, Dies at 93
item.description:
Johanna Matz (1932–2025) was a prominent figure in German-language cinema during the 1950s, widely remembered for her radiant presence and “girl next door” appeal. Trained as a dancer at Vienna’s prestigious Max Reinhardt Seminar, she brought a refined sense of movement and natural elegance to her performances, which helped define her screen persona. Matz rose to fame in post-war Europe through her roles in popular Heimatfilms and romantic comedies, genres that offered audiences warmth, optimism, and escapism during a period of reconstruction and cultural recovery.
Among her most notable appearances were her performances in The White Horse Inn (1952) and The Forester’s Daughter (1952), films that showcased her charm and ability to embody lighthearted, approachable characters. Her success in German-language cinema eventually attracted international attention, including from Hollywood director Otto Preminger, who cast her in the German-language version of The Moon Is Blue (Die Jungfrau

item.title: Maggie Smith’s Early Stage Role in the 1960 Play 'Strip the Willow'
item.description:
In 1960, a 25-year-old Maggie Smith appeared in the stage production “Strip the Willow,” a play that would later be seen as an important early milestone in her rise to international fame. The work was written by Beverley Cross, who would eventually become Smith’s second husband, and he specifically crafted the lead role with her in mind. The character, Kathy Dawson, was described in the script as a young woman in her mid-twenties who was exceptionally beautiful, elegant, and comparable in sophistication to a top international model, while also possessing a playful sense of charm.
The production premiered at the Newcastle Theatre Royal in November 1960 before touring other venues, including the Theatre Royal in Brighton. One of the most talked-about aspects of the play involved scenes in which Smith’s character participated in strip poker, which led to widely circulated promotional photographs showing her in a partially undressed state while holding playing cards. These images contribu

item.title: Portraits and Career Highlights of Denzel Washington During the 1980s
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at actor Denzel Washington during the 1980s, combining biographical information with highlights from his early acting career. It begins by outlining Washington’s background as an American actor, producer, director, and minister, noting his later recognition as one of the most acclaimed performers of his generation. The piece emphasizes his early professional development, starting with his screen debut in the 1977 television film Wilma, followed by his first Hollywood appearance in Carbon Copy (1981). A significant milestone in his career was his role in the television series St. Elsewhere (1982–1988), where he portrayed Dr. Phillip Chandler, helping establish him as a prominent television actor. During this period, he also participated in theater work, including A Soldier’s Play, for which he received critical recognition. The article highlights his transition into major film roles in the mid-to-late 1980s, including A Soldier’s Story (1984), H

item.title: Historic 200-Year-Old Nail-Free Wooden Bridge in Dagestan Showcases Traditional Engineering
item.description:
The article highlights a remarkable wooden bridge located near the village of Gulli (Juli) in the Tabasaran region of Dagestan, Russia. Estimated to be around 200 years old, the structure stands approximately 10 meters (about 33 feet) above the ground and remains a striking example of traditional construction techniques. What makes the bridge especially notable is that it was built entirely without the use of metal nails or modern fasteners. Instead, local Tabasaran builders relied on carefully crafted interlocking wooden joints and wooden pegs, often referred to as trunnels or dowels, demonstrating advanced carpentry skills and engineering knowledge for its time.
Constructed using locally sourced wood and stone, the bridge reflects sustainable building practices rooted in the resources available to the community. Its design allows for slight flexibility, enabling it to adapt to changes in temperature and humidity. This structural adaptability is one of the reasons it has survived for

item.title: Video game developers expand unionization efforts, citing remote work policy changes and workplace conditions
item.description:
A growing number of video game developers working on major franchises have joined labor unions under the Communication Workers of America (CWA), reflecting an ongoing shift in the industry toward collective bargaining. Teams associated with well-known titles such as Call of Duty, Fallout, Overwatch, Diablo, and World of Warcraft have already organized or joined union efforts. In a recent development, developers working on Magic: The Gathering Arena also announced their intention to form a union. However, instead of voluntarily recognizing the union, owner Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast have opted to proceed toward an official vote overseen by the National Labor Relations Board, scheduled for June.
Reporting from Aftermath highlights that changes in remote work policies have been a significant catalyst for organizing efforts. Some developers noted that new workplace rules required employees to relocate across the country or move to higher-cost regions in order to maintain their jobs.

item.title: California’s 2026 primaries shaped by voter frustration, party infighting, and political uncertainty
item.description:
California’s upcoming June 2 primary elections are unfolding in an atmosphere of widespread political uncertainty and voter frustration, according to political analysts and campaign observers. The race includes contests for key offices such as Los Angeles mayor, California governor, and several congressional seats, all of which are being influenced by dissatisfaction with both President Donald Trump and the state’s Democratic establishment.
Across the political spectrum, voters are described as increasingly disillusioned. Republicans aligned with Trump are campaigning on criticism of long-standing Democratic leadership in California, arguing that it has failed to address persistent issues such as homelessness, housing affordability, and economic pressures. At the same time, Democrats are struggling to defend their record while also engaging in internal conflicts, with multiple candidates competing for influence and blaming each other for policy shortcomings.
In Los Angeles, Mayor Kar

item.title: CDN Vulnerability 'Underminr' Enables Hidden Malicious Traffic via Trusted Domains
item.description:
Security researchers have disclosed a serious vulnerability in shared content delivery network (CDN) infrastructure dubbed “Underminr,” which allows attackers to conceal malicious communications behind legitimate, trusted domains. The flaw affects an estimated 88 million domains and undermines key security mechanisms such as DNS filtering and protective DNS services.
At the core of the issue is a mismatch between the Server Name Indication (SNI) and HTTP Host headers versus the actual IP address being contacted. In practice, attackers can present a legitimate domain in the SNI and HTTP Host fields while routing traffic to the IP address of a different tenant hosted on the same shared CDN edge infrastructure. This behavior effectively enables traffic to appear trustworthy while being redirected to malicious or unintended destinations.
Security analysts note that the exploit resembles and expands on earlier domain fronting techniques, which were historically used to bypass censorship

item.title: World of Warcraft PTR Update Suggests Blizzard May Delay Patch 12.0.7
item.description:
Blizzard may be adjusting the release schedule for World of Warcraft patch 12.0.7, titled “Revelations,” according to changes recently spotted on the Public Test Realm (PTR). The update is expected to continue the ongoing Midnight storyline, introducing two new zones focused on the Void invasion and featuring a new single-boss raid encounter against Rotmire in Sporefall. The patch is also notable for Blizzard’s experiment with more flexible Mythic raid sizes ranging from 15 to 25 players.
One of the most discussed additions in patch 12.0.7 is the return of the Turbulent Timeways event, a popular Timewalking feature that rotates older dungeons more rapidly while rewarding players with stacking experience buffs and exclusive cosmetics. Players who complete the full event cycle will be able to earn the Spawn of Vyranoth mount, alongside access to additional Dragonflight dungeons.
Originally, PTR data suggested the event would begin on June 16, leading many players to assume the patch wo

item.title: North Dakota fossil site offers evidence of conditions immediately after the dinosaur-killing asteroid impact
item.description:
The article examines the Tanis fossil site in the Hell Creek Formation of North Dakota, which has been proposed as a rare record of events occurring shortly after the Chicxulub asteroid impact 66 million years ago. A 2019 study led by Robert DePalma described an “event deposit” containing densely packed fish fossils, burned wood, marine material, and glassy spherules formed during the asteroid impact. These spherules are of particular interest because some were found lodged in the gills of fossilized fish, suggesting the animals were alive when debris from the impact fallout entered the water. This detail has been interpreted as evidence that the deposit captured events occurring within minutes to hours after the impact.
The site also includes an iridium-rich clay layer that marks the global Cretaceous-Paleogene boundary, reinforcing its connection to the extinction event. Researchers propose that the sediment was deposited by a sudden high-energy surge, potentially triggered by seism

item.title: Critics Highlight JD Vance’s Past Trump-Hitler Comparison After Mike Lee Condemns Similar Rhetoric
item.description:
A political dispute erupted after Republican Senator Mike Lee criticized Democrats for comparing former President Donald Trump to Adolf Hitler, arguing that such rhetoric could encourage political violence. In a social media post, Lee stated that there was no legal or moral justification for violence against Trump and urged Democrats to publicly reject such comparisons.
However, critics quickly pointed out that Vice President JD Vance himself had made a similar comparison years earlier. Activists, commentators, and Democratic lawmakers responded by referencing Vance’s 2016 private messages in which he described Trump as possibly being “America’s Hitler.” Those remarks resurfaced publicly after Vance became a close political ally of Trump and have continued to draw attention because of the dramatic shift in Vance’s political stance.
Among those responding was activist Harry Sisson, who argued that Vance was the most prominent political figure to make the comparison Lee condemned. Repr

item.title: Researchers Demonstrate Hidden Audio Attacks Against AI Voice Assistants
item.description:
Researchers from China and Singapore have demonstrated a new type of cyberattack that uses inaudible audio signals to manipulate AI-powered voice assistants and chatbots. The study, presented at the IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy, shows how attackers can embed hidden adversarial audio commands into ordinary media such as podcasts, songs, or YouTube videos. While human listeners cannot detect these sounds, AI voice systems may interpret them as legitimate commands.
According to the researchers, the attack works by training a malicious audio signal that can consistently exploit a targeted AI model regardless of what the user is saying. Once created, the signal can be inserted into background audio content and potentially trigger unauthorized actions on connected devices. These actions could include accessing personal files, sensitive information, or connected online services.
Lead researcher Meng Chen explained that current defensive measures are ineffective because AI systems

item.title: 1851 Daguerreotype Documents the Life of Caesar, an Enslaved Man in New York
item.description:
The article examines the historical significance of an 1851 daguerreotype portrait of Caesar, an enslaved African American man who lived in New York during the 18th and 19th centuries. The image is considered one of the earliest known photographs of an African American person in the United States. Caesar is believed to have been born around 1737 on the Bethlehem House estate in Bethlehem, New York, where he was enslaved by the Nicoll family for most of his life. He reportedly served several generations of the same family and continued living with them even after he was allowed to retire around 1817.
The story also highlights the gradual abolition of slavery in New York. Although most enslaved people in the state were freed by 1827, Caesar was apparently not officially emancipated until approximately 1841, when remaining forms of legal slavery ended. In 1851, likely at the request of a member of the Nicoll family, Caesar sat for a daguerreotype portrait that survived along with a handw

item.title: White House Communications Director Sparks Backlash After Profane Response to Mike Pompeo Criticism
item.description:
White House Communications Director Steven Cheung drew widespread criticism after issuing a profanity-laced response to former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo. The dispute began when Pompeo publicly criticized a reported Trump administration proposal involving Iran, warning that it resembled past diplomatic frameworks he believes could strengthen Iran’s military capabilities and undermine U.S. interests. Pompeo argued the emerging deal was inconsistent with an “America First” approach and urged stronger action regarding strategic waterways.
In response, Cheung sharply attacked Pompeo on social media, dismissing his expertise and using explicit language, including telling him to “shut his stupid mouth.” Cheung also accused Pompeo of lacking current information about ongoing policy discussions and suggested he was unqualified to comment on the matter. The tone and language of the statement quickly generated backlash from journalists, legal experts, and political commentators across the i

item.title: Gunila and the Elegance of Mid-Century High Fashion Photography
item.description:
Gunila was a prominent fashion model of the 1950s and 1960s, widely recognized for embodying the refined “Nordic look” that captivated the international fashion industry. With her sharp cheekbones, expressive eyes, and poised, sculptural presence, she became a favored muse for some of the most influential photographers of the era, including Irving Penn, Richard Avedon, and Philippe Pottier. Her career flourished during a transformative period in fashion when haute couture was shifting from rigid post-war elegance to more modern, experimental silhouettes.
Throughout her modeling work, Gunila showcased designs from legendary fashion houses such as Dior, Balenciaga, Pierre Cardin, Jean Patou, Lanvin, and Nina Ricci. She was frequently photographed in structured wool suits, elegant satin cocktail dresses, and elaborate couture gowns featuring lace, tulle, and dramatic tailoring. These garments highlighted her ability to give clothing a sense of movement and architectural form, rather than

item.title: Dr. Janette Nesheiwat Joins Walter Reed Team Treating Patients With Havana Syndrome
item.description:
Dr. Janette Nesheiwat, a physician and former Fox News contributor, has begun a new position at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center, where she is helping treat military personnel, intelligence officials, diplomats, veterans, and family members affected by Havana Syndrome and related neurological conditions. In interviews with Fox News Digital, Nesheiwat described the role as a “profound honor” and emphasized her commitment to caring for service members who suffer from traumatic brain injuries, blast exposure, and complex neurological symptoms.
Havana Syndrome refers to a collection of unexplained neurological and vestibular symptoms reported by U.S. officials and their families stationed overseas. Although some officials have suggested that a hostile foreign actor could be responsible through the use of directed-energy weapons, U.S. intelligence agencies and congressional investigators have not reached a definitive conclusion about the cause.
Nesheiwat explained that her wor

item.title: Brigitte Bardot Dies at 91 After Decades as Film Star and Animal Rights Activist
item.description:
French actress, singer, and cultural icon Brigitte Bardot died at the age of 91 at her home in southern France, according to a statement released by her foundation on December 28, 2025. Bardot became one of the most recognizable international celebrities of the 1950s and 1960s, gaining worldwide fame after starring in the 1956 film “And God Created Woman.” Nicknamed “B.B.,” she appeared in 56 films and became a symbol of glamour, sexual liberation, and French cinema during her career. In 1969, her image was chosen as the model for Marianne, the national emblem of France, further cementing her cultural significance.
At the peak of her fame, Bardot retired from acting in 1973 at the age of 39 and shifted her focus toward animal welfare activism. She founded the Brigitte Bardot Foundation in 1986 and campaigned for decades against animal cruelty, seal hunting, animal testing, and the consumption of horse and dog meat. Her advocacy work became a defining part of her later life.
The artic

item.title: Researchers Show Micro-Rough Surfaces Can Reduce Aerodynamic Drag More Effectively Than Smooth Ones
item.description:
Researchers at Tohoku University in Japan have reported experimental evidence suggesting that extremely fine, irregular surface textures can reduce aerodynamic drag more effectively than perfectly smooth surfaces under certain conditions. For decades, aeronautical engineering has relied on the assumption that smoother surfaces always minimize drag by maintaining laminar airflow and delaying the transition to turbulence. This principle was largely based on aerodynamic studies dating back to the 1940s. However, the new research challenges that long-standing belief by demonstrating that distributed micro-roughness (DMR) can significantly improve aerodynamic performance.
The research team, led by associate professor Aiko Yakino at the Institute of Fluid Science, tested microscopic roughness patterns consisting of glass beads and sandblasted textures. These irregularities were extremely small, measuring only a fraction of the airflow boundary layer thickness. Using a specialized magnetic s

item.title: Debate grows over disclosure rules for paid political influencers in California gubernatorial race
item.description:
The article examines the growing influence of social media creators in American politics and the increasing concern over undisclosed payments to influencers promoting political candidates. During the 2024 election cycle, influencers became more visible than ever, attending party conventions, campaign events, and White House functions as campaigns sought to reach younger and less traditional voters through social media platforms. Experts quoted in the report explain that influencers can appear more authentic and relatable than traditional advertising, making their endorsements especially persuasive.
The controversy intensified in California’s gubernatorial race after allegations emerged that several influencers received payments from Democratic candidate Tom Steyer’s campaign without properly disclosing sponsored political content. California is one of the few states with laws requiring influencers to clearly state when political posts are paid promotions and identify the sponsoring ca

item.title: Democratic Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner declines to apologize for resurfaced post mocking wounded soldier
item.description:
Democratic Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner is facing renewed scrutiny after a resurfaced Reddit post in which he mocked a wounded U.S. soldier. According to reporting from Fox News Digital, Platner did not apologize when confronted about the post, which referred to a video involving Pfc. Ted Daniels, a service member who was injured in combat and later awarded a Purple Heart. The post, written under a username linked to Platner, included derogatory language about the soldier and suggested he did not deserve to live, prompting widespread criticism from veterans and political opponents.
When asked directly by reporters near his home in Maine, Platner initially declined to respond and later rejected the premise that he disrespected veterans. He emphasized his own military service, stating that he completed multiple infantry tours and arguing that any claim of disrespect toward veterans was offensive and inaccurate. He also questioned how many of his friends had received Purple Hear

item.title: Talkative guide disrupts romantic nature walk, prompting advice on handling future encounters
item.description:
In this advice column installment of “Asking Eric,” a reader describes a social situation that unfolded during a group nature walk. The writer had joined the outing with a friend they hoped could become something more than friendship, and the early part of the walk created an enjoyable, even romantic atmosphere. The physical activity and natural surroundings supported relaxed conversation between the two, allowing their connection to develop comfortably for most of the excursion. However, the dynamic changed when another participant, described as a talkative man, joined the group during a break.
According to the letter, the man quickly dominated the interaction with continuous speech, questions, and commentary. He focused his attention primarily on the writer’s companion, who appeared increasingly tired and uncomfortable with the one-sided conversation. Attempts to limit engagement or provide minimal responses did not deter the man, who continued to steer the discussion and draw out r

item.title: A Collection of Rita Hayworth Portraits from the 1942 Film “My Gal Sal”
item.description:
This article presents a curated collection of portraits of Rita Hayworth taken during the production of the 1942 musical film “My Gal Sal.” The movie is a Technicolor Hollywood production that pays tribute to the lively spirit of the 1890s, often referred to as the “Gay Nineties.” It follows a fictionalized version of songwriter Paul Dresser’s rise from performing in traveling medicine shows to achieving success in New York City’s Broadway scene. Alongside Victor Mature, Rita Hayworth plays a central role in bringing charm and emotional depth to the story.
The highlight of the feature is the set of behind-the-scenes and studio portraits showcasing Rita Hayworth at the height of her career. Known for her elegance and screen presence, Hayworth is depicted in elaborate Victorian-era costumes that reflect the film’s rich production design. The Technicolor cinematography enhances the visual appeal, emphasizing warm stage lighting, luxurious fabrics, and the vibrant atmosphere of musical pe

item.title: Apple challenges court ruling extending Epic Games App Store injunction to all developers
item.description:
Apple is continuing its long-running legal battle with Epic Games over App Store commissions by asking the U.S. Supreme Court to review recent lower court decisions. In its latest filing, Apple argues that the injunction issued in the Epic Games case should not be broadly applied to all developers on the U.S. App Store. The company claims that the original dispute was specific to Epic and should not automatically impact other major developers such as Microsoft or Spotify, who were not part of the lawsuit.
The dispute stems from a court order requiring Apple to allow app developers to include links directing users to external payment systems outside the App Store. While Apple complied by allowing such links, it introduced a 27% fee on purchases made through these external pathways. A U.S. appeals court later issued a civil contempt order, concluding that Apple’s fees undermined the intent of the injunction, which was designed to promote competition in payment processing options.
Apple

item.title: Researchers Observe Reversal of Angular Momentum in Crystal Lattice Vibrations
item.description:
An international team of physicists has directly observed how angular momentum moves through a crystal lattice, revealing a previously unseen quantum effect in which atomic rotations unexpectedly reverse direction. The research, led by scientists from Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR) and the Fritz Haber Institute of the Max Planck Society, used intense terahertz laser pulses to excite vibrations inside the quantum material bismuth selenide. By tracking the motion with ultrafast laser techniques, the researchers were able to watch angular momentum transfer between coupled lattice vibrations in real time.
The experiment uncovered an unusual phenomenon: as angular momentum moved from one vibrational mode to another, the direction of rotation flipped. According to the researchers, this reversal is caused by the rotational symmetry of the crystal structure, where certain rotational states become physically equivalent even if they spin in opposite directions. The team compared th

item.title: 1940s Lone Ranger Promotional Ring Used a Small Amount of Radioactive Polonium-210
item.description:
The article examines the unusual history of the Lone Ranger Atomic Bomb Ring, a promotional toy offered by Kix cereal in 1947 during the height of America’s fascination with atomic energy. For 15 cents and a cereal box top, children could receive the ring, which was marketed as a fun science-themed novelty connected to the popular Lone Ranger character. The toy reflected the widespread excitement surrounding nuclear technology in the years following World War II, when atomic power was often portrayed as futuristic and beneficial.
What made the ring remarkable was that it actually contained a tiny amount of polonium-210, a highly radioactive isotope. The device also included a zinc sulfide screen that produced visible flashes when hit by alpha particles emitted by the radioactive material. Although manufacturers considered the product safe as long as the radioactive contents were not inhaled or swallowed, modern understanding recognizes polonium-210 as an extremely toxic substance.
Th

item.title: Russia conducts large-scale missile and drone assault on Kyiv, including hypersonic weapon
item.description:
Russia carried out one of its most extensive aerial assaults on Ukraine’s capital region since the start of the war, launching a combined barrage of approximately 600 drones and 90 missiles overnight. Among the weapons used was the Oreshnik hypersonic ballistic missile, which U.S. officials classify as an intermediate-range system capable of carrying multiple conventional or nuclear warheads. Ukrainian authorities reported that the missile struck near Bila Tserkva in central Ukraine and noted that this was only the third known deployment of the system. Ukrainian air defenses claimed to have intercepted 604 of the incoming weapons, though officials acknowledged that several ballistic missiles penetrated defenses and caused damage, with Kyiv identified as the primary target of the attack. At least four people were reported killed in the strikes, and significant destruction was recorded in parts of the capital and surrounding areas.
The Russian government described the assault as retalia

item.title: Opinion piece links U.S. public health policy changes under Trump to concerns about Ebola outbreak preparedness
item.description:
The article is an opinion commentary discussing a reported Ebola outbreak in parts of the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, describing more than 500 suspected cases and around 130 deaths tied to a rare strain of the virus. It notes that the strain mentioned lacks widely available vaccines or treatments, raising concerns about containment efforts in affected regions. The piece outlines responses from U.S. authorities, including travel advisories and restrictions affecting individuals who have recently been in impacted countries, while stating that the overall risk to the American public is considered low.
The author argues that recent changes in U.S. public health policy—particularly the withdrawal from the World Health Organization and reductions in staffing and capacity at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention—have weakened the country’s ability to detect and respond rapidly to international disease outbreaks. It claims that reduced participation in global surveillanc

item.title: Collection of Early 20th-Century Cigarette and Cigar Advertising Posters
item.description:
The article published by Vintage Everyday presents a curated collection of 35 advertising posters promoting cigarettes, cigars, and cigarette papers from the early 20th century. These advertisements, dating mainly from the 1900s and 1910s, showcase how tobacco companies used artistic design and visual storytelling to attract consumers during a period when smoking products were becoming increasingly associated with sophistication and modern lifestyles.
The post explains that tobacco advertising evolved significantly during this era. Unlike the text-heavy newspaper advertisements common in the 19th century, these posters relied on colorful lithographic printing, bold illustrations, and influences from popular artistic movements such as Art Nouveau and early Art Deco. Tobacco brands often commissioned talented illustrators to create memorable imagery that linked smoking with elegance, luxury, romance, and social status.
Several featured posters promoted products such as Murad Turkish Ci

item.title: Trump says US-Iran peace framework largely negotiated amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced that a potential peace agreement with Iran has been “largely negotiated,” signaling major progress in talks aimed at ending ongoing hostilities and stabilizing key regional shipping routes. According to the latest updates, the proposed framework would include steps toward reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit chokepoint, while gradually lifting restrictions tied to the US blockade of Iranian ports. Trump indicated that final details are still being worked out, but regional leaders have urged acceptance of the current framework following mediation efforts involving multiple countries.
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio confirmed that “significant progress” has been made in negotiations, while reiterating Washington’s core demand that Iran must never obtain a nuclear weapon. He also criticized Iran’s threats to commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, calling them illegal under international law and warning against norma

item.title: Study Finds Garlic Compound Disrupts Mosquito Reproduction and Egg Laying
item.description:
Researchers from Yale University have identified a chemical compound in garlic that can interfere with the reproductive behavior of mosquitoes and other flying insects. The study, published in the journal Cell, examined 43 fruits and vegetables to determine whether natural substances could affect insect mating and egg-laying behavior. Scientists used fruit flies as a model because they commonly mate around food sources. While most foods tested had little effect, garlic completely prevented mating and reproduction in the insects.
The researchers conducted additional experiments to determine whether the effect was caused by smell or taste. Their findings showed that taste was responsible for the reproductive inhibition. Chemical analysis revealed that diallyl disulfide, a sulfur-containing compound naturally present in garlic, triggered the response. The substance activates a taste receptor called TrpA1, which is associated with detecting harmful or bitter substances. Activation of this

item.title: The Criminal Career and Life of Bertha Liebbeke, ‘Fainting Bertha,’ a Notorious Midwestern Pickpocket
item.description:
The article recounts the life and criminal history of Bertha Liebbeke, also known by several aliases and widely referred to as “Fainting Bertha,” one of the most infamous pickpockets in the American Midwest during the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Born in March 1880 in Council Bluffs, Iowa, Bertha experienced early tragedy with the death of her father and was later diagnosed with Sydenham’s chorea, a neurological disorder that caused involuntary movements. Due to her condition and behavior, she was institutionalized in multiple facilities, including hospitals for the mentally ill in Iowa.
After her release, Bertha reportedly entered a life of crime, claiming she was taught pickpocketing by a man named Gunther. She became known for a unique method of theft in which she would pretend to faint in public, attracting sympathetic men who would catch her—only to have their wallets, jewelry, or valuables stolen in the process. This tactic earned her the nickname “Fainting Bertha” and ma

item.title: A Look at Marianne Faithfull’s Transformation in the 1970s Through Portraits
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 30 portrait photographs of Marianne Faithfull taken during the 1970s, a decade that marked one of the most dramatic transformations in her life and career. Faithfull, an English singer and actress who rose to fame in the 1960s with the hit As Tears Go By, was widely known as part of the British Invasion and for her high-profile relationship with Mick Jagger. The piece frames the images within the broader narrative of her personal struggles and artistic reinvention throughout the 1970s.
After the end of her relationship with Mick Jagger in 1970, Faithfull experienced a period of severe personal hardship, including the loss of custody of her son, addiction issues, and a time living on the streets of London’s Soho district. During this era, her health and voice were deeply affected, shifting from a pure soprano to a rough, husky tone. Despite these challenges, she made several recording attempts, including the shelved 1971 projec

item.title: A Look Back at Mary Tyler Moore’s Career and Cultural Influence in the 1970s
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday highlights the life, career, and cultural impact of actress and producer Mary Tyler Moore during the 1970s, accompanied by a collection of 40 photographs from the decade. Moore, born in 1936 and passing away in 2017, became one of the most influential television figures of her era through her performances and pioneering work in entertainment. The feature revisits her most famous television roles, particularly on The Dick Van Dyke Show and the groundbreaking sitcom The Mary Tyler Moore Show, which aired from 1970 to 1977.
The article explains that The Mary Tyler Moore Show helped redefine the portrayal of women on American television. Moore’s character, Mary Richards, worked as an associate producer at a Minneapolis television station and represented an independent, career-focused woman navigating professional and personal challenges. The series was widely praised for addressing workplace equality, female friendship, and the changing social expectation

item.title: Jim Acosta Demonstrates Cognitive Assessment Cited in Political Debate Over Trump’s Claims
item.description:
Journalist Jim Acosta participated in a televised demonstration of a cognitive assessment that former President Donald Trump has publicly claimed to have taken and passed multiple times. The exercise was featured on a Saturday episode of Acosta’s program and was presented as both an educational demonstration and a political commentary on Trump’s repeated references to his performance on such tests. Acosta stated that the purpose of taking the test was to show viewers what it is like and to emphasize that it should not be used as a political talking point or a measure for public boasting by elected officials.
The cognitive exam referenced is widely believed to be the Montreal Cognitive Assessment, a screening tool designed to detect signs of cognitive decline and dementia. The test includes tasks such as drawing geometric shapes like a cube, identifying animals, repeating sequences of numbers and words, counting backward by sevens from 100, and drawing a clock. During the segment, Acos

item.title: Republican Lawmakers Defend Trump’s Involvement in GOP Primaries After Thomas Massie’s Defeat
item.description:
Republican lawmakers are largely dismissing concerns that President Donald Trump’s strategy of targeting Republican incumbents in primary elections could damage party unity or hurt the GOP’s legislative agenda. The discussion intensified after Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky lost his Republican primary to challenger Ed Gallrein following months of criticism and political pressure from Trump.
Massie had frequently opposed parts of Trump’s agenda in Congress, especially on spending issues and other legislative disputes. Trump openly criticized Massie and supported efforts to remove him from office, making the Kentucky primary a closely watched example of the former president’s continued influence within the Republican Party.
Several Republican lawmakers defended Trump’s right to endorse and campaign against candidates as he chooses. Rep. Byron Donalds said the party should move past primary battles and refocus on serving the American people. Rep. Beth Van Duyne emphasized Trump’s politi

item.title: Victorian Painter Richard Ansdell Remembered for His Animal and Highland Scenes
item.description:
