Richard Ansdell (1815–1885) was an important British painter of the Victorian period who became widely recognized for his detailed depictions of animals, sporting life, and rural landscapes. Born in Liverpool into modest circumstances, he rose to prominence through his artistic talent and eventually became a respected member of the Royal Academy. During his career, he was frequently compared to the celebrated animal painter Sir Edwin Landseer because of his skill in portraying wildlife, hunting scenes, and working animals with realism and emotion.
The article highlights Ansdell’s remarkable ability to combine precise anatomical detail with strong storytelling. His paintings often captured scenes from the Scottish Highlands, including shepherds, hunters, livestock, dogs, and dramatic outdoor settings. Works such as “The Hunted Slaves” also demonstrated his interest in larger social and humanitarian themes beyond sporting art.
A large number of his paintings are referenced in the artic

item.title: Rare LIFE Magazine Photos Capture Lucille Ball and Vivian Vance Performing in a 1962 Episode of The Lucy Show
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday revisits a memorable episode of the classic television series “The Lucy Show” titled “Chris’s New Year’s Eve Party,” which originally aired on CBS on December 31, 1962. The feature highlights a collection of black-and-white photographs taken by renowned LIFE magazine photographer Ralph Crane during the filming of the episode. The images focus on actresses Lucille Ball and Vivian Vance as they perform comedic scenes and entertain teenage party guests with a Charlie Chaplin-inspired routine.
According to the article, the episode centers on Lucy Carmichael and her friend Viv attempting to help Lucy’s daughter Chris after her New Year’s Eve party becomes unsuccessful and awkward. Chris had insisted on hosting the party without Lucy’s involvement, forcing Lucy and Viv to stay away at first while neighbor Harry Connors supervises the teenagers. However, after learning that the gathering has become dull and disappointing, Lucy and Viv return to rescue the ev

item.title: Daniela Bianchi and Her Legacy as a 1960s European Cinema Icon
item.description:
Daniela Bianchi, born in 1942 in Rome, Italy, is an Italian actress widely recognized for her role as Tatiana Romanova in the 1963 James Bond film “From Russia with Love.” Her performance as the Soviet corporal and Bond’s love interest became one of the defining portrayals of the early Bond franchise, remembered for its blend of sophistication, emotional depth, and classic 1960s cinematic style. Before achieving international fame in film, Bianchi built a strong foundation in the performing arts as a professional ballet dancer and later gained further public attention as a successful fashion model. She notably placed as the first runner-up in the Miss Universe 1960 pageant, which helped elevate her profile on the global stage.
Her transition into acting led to a brief but highly influential film career. Despite the success and lasting recognition from her role in the James Bond series, Bianchi chose not to pursue a long-term career in Hollywood or European cinema. In 1970, she retired

item.title: Norman Seeff’s 1969 Portrait Session with Patti Smith in New York
item.description:
In 1969, Patti Smith was still an emerging figure within New York’s underground art and literary circles, far from the international recognition she would later achieve as a pioneering musician and cultural icon. At the time, she was primarily known as a poet, performing in small downtown venues and moving through the bohemian spaces of Manhattan, including the Chelsea Hotel scene. Her style was understated and instinctive, often borrowing men’s clothing and prioritizing expression, language, and attitude over any sense of polished image. She lived on the margins, surrounded by artists, writers, and musicians who were shaping the city’s experimental cultural landscape.
Photographer Norman Seeff was also at an early stage in his career. Rather than focusing on celebrity, he was interested in capturing creative energy and psychological intensity in artists. When he photographed Patti Smith, his approach was informal and experimental. The session reportedly took place in a studio on West

item.title: Reopened Train des Merveilles railway connects Nice with the Southern French Alps
item.description:
The article describes the reopening of the Train des Merveilles (Train of Wonders), a scenic railway line in southeastern France that links the glamorous Côte d’Azur with the rugged landscapes of the Southern French Alps. After major maintenance work, the line reopened and now offers travelers a two-hour journey from Nice to the mountain town of Tende, climbing about 1,000 meters through tunnels, viaducts, and dramatic alpine terrain. The route passes through historic and picturesque stops, including Sospel, a medieval town with a 13th-century bridge and lively market, and La Brigue, known for its narrow streets and the Chapel of Our Lady of Fountains, which contains remarkable 15th-century frescoes often compared to a small “Sistine Chapel of the Alps.”
As the train continues deeper into the Mercantour National Park region, passengers experience increasingly remote and elevated scenery, with steep valleys, dense forests, and jagged peaks. The line’s engineering, originally developed

item.title: Vera-Ellen, Hollywood Musical Dancer and MGM Star of the 1940s–1950s
item.description:
Vera-Ellen (1921–1981) was an American actress, singer, and dancer who became one of the standout performers of Hollywood’s Golden Age of musical films. She was widely admired for her extraordinary technical dancing ability, precision, and athletic grace, as well as her energetic stage presence and charismatic smile. Her physical agility and expressive movement made her especially suited for the demanding choreography of mid-20th-century film musicals.
During her career, Vera-Ellen starred in several major MGM productions and became a recognizable figure in classic Hollywood entertainment. She notably performed alongside Fred Astaire in “Three Little Words” (1950), showcasing her refined dance technique and screen chemistry in elaborate musical numbers. She also appeared in “The Belle of New York” (1952), further establishing her reputation as a leading musical performer of the era.
One of her most memorable roles came in the holiday classic “White Christmas” (1954), where she shared

item.title: French court finds Air France and Airbus responsible for 2009 Flight 447 disaster
item.description:
A French appeals court has found Air France and aircraft manufacturer Airbus guilty of corporate manslaughter in connection with the 2009 crash of Air France Flight 447, which killed all 228 people on board during a journey from Rio de Janeiro to Paris. The ruling came after an eight-week trial and overturns a previous 2023 decision that had cleared both companies of criminal responsibility. The court concluded that the airline and manufacturer were “solely and entirely responsible” for the tragedy.
The accident occurred when the Airbus A330 entered an aerodynamic stall during a severe storm over the Atlantic Ocean. Investigators determined that a combination of technical and human factors contributed to the crash. Ice accumulation temporarily disrupted the aircraft’s airspeed sensors, known as pitot tubes, which produced unreliable readings. This triggered the autopilot to disconnect, leaving the aircraft under manual control. In the confusion that followed, the co-pilots incorrectly

item.title: 1902 Panhard et Levassor Type A2 and the Early Development of Modern Automobile Design
item.description:
The 1902 Panhard et Levassor Type A2 represents an important moment in the early evolution of the automobile, when vehicle design was transitioning away from horse-drawn carriage influences toward more standardized engineering principles. Produced by the influential French manufacturer Panhard et Levassor, the Type A2 embodied the innovative Système Panhard, which became foundational to modern car architecture. This system placed the engine at the front of the vehicle and used a rear-wheel-drive layout, a configuration that would later become the dominant standard in automotive design for much of the 20th century.
The Type A2 was typically equipped with a Centaure two-cylinder engine, generating approximately 5 to 7 horsepower. While modest by modern standards, this output was sufficient for the lightweight construction of early automobiles, allowing them to achieve practical though limited speeds suitable for the roads of the time. The vehicle often featured wooden-spoked wheels and

item.title: Behind-the-scenes photos of Gong Li during the filming of Raise the Red Lantern (1991)
item.description:
This article highlights a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs of acclaimed actress Gong Li during the production of the 1991 film “Raise the Red Lantern,” directed by Zhang Yimou. The film is widely regarded as a masterpiece of world cinema and marks one of the most important collaborations between Gong Li and Zhang Yimou, following their earlier success with “Red Sorghum.” In the film, Gong Li plays Songlian, a 19-year-old educated woman who becomes the Fourth Mistress of a wealthy merchant in 1920s China after her father’s death forces her into marriage.
The article emphasizes Gong Li’s powerful and emotionally complex performance, which is often described as haunting. Through her portrayal, Songlian transforms from a proud and independent young woman into someone gradually crushed by the strict patriarchal structure of the household. Inside the merchant’s compound, she becomes entangled in a brutal system of rivalry between the four wives, where favor is symbolized by the l

item.title: How inflated ARR metrics are used to promote and legitimize AI startups
item.description:
Article from TechCrunch investigates concerns among founders, investors, and finance professionals about the misuse and inflation of annual recurring revenue (ARR) metrics in the AI startup ecosystem. It highlights allegations that some AI companies are overstating revenue by substituting “contracted ARR” or “committed ARR” (CARR) for true ARR, or by using annualized run-rate revenue based on short-term performance. These practices can significantly exaggerate a company’s financial health, especially when long-term contracts, pilot programs, or usage-based billing are involved.
Several sources cited in the report claim that some startups count signed contracts or even unpaid pilot programs as ARR, despite uncertainty about whether the revenue will ever be realized. In some cases, investors are aware of these adjustments but tolerate them due to competitive pressures and the rapid growth narrative surrounding AI companies. One VC noted that companies may report CARR significantly highe

item.title: Anthony Hopkins’ Early Years in the British Army Before His Acting Career
item.description:
The article explores an often overlooked chapter in the early life of acclaimed actor Anthony Hopkins: his compulsory military service in the British Army during the late 1950s. Before achieving global fame for iconic roles such as Hannibal Lecter, Hopkins served two years of National Service from 1958 to 1960. He was assigned to the Royal Artillery, where he was officially known as Gunner Hopkins, carrying the service number 23449720.
At the time, Hopkins had already completed studies at the Royal Welsh College of Music & Drama, graduating in 1957. However, like many young men of his generation, he was required to fulfill National Service obligations. The transition from artistic training to the strict and disciplined environment of military life was challenging for him. The article highlights that Hopkins was a creative and somewhat nontraditional student, more inclined toward music and performance than rigid academic or military structures. Despite this, he later acknowledged that

item.title: Lucien Lelong and His Role in Preserving Parisian Haute Couture During WWII
item.description:
Lucien Lelong (1889–1958) was a prominent French couturier who played a crucial role in preserving the integrity and global leadership of Parisian haute couture during the early to mid-20th century. Renowned for his refined craftsmanship and elegant designs, Lelong rose to particular historical importance during World War II, when France was under Nazi occupation. As president of the Chambre Syndicale de la Couture Parisienne, he actively resisted German attempts to relocate the French fashion industry to Berlin. His firm stance emphasized that haute couture could only truly exist in Paris, helping ensure that the industry remained rooted in the French capital during a period of intense political and cultural pressure. This leadership is widely credited with safeguarding the continuity of Paris as the world center of high fashion.
Beyond his wartime influence, Lelong was also an innovator in the business of fashion. He was among the early couturiers to successfully develop luxury perf

item.title: Tesla Cybercab Achieves Record Efficiency as Purpose-Built Robotaxi
item.description:
Tesla’s upcoming Cybercab robotaxi has been officially certified at 165 Wh/mi, making it the most energy-efficient electric vehicle ever produced, according to reporting from Electrek. Tesla Vice President of Vehicle Engineering Lars Moravy confirmed the figure, emphasizing that it is a certified rating rather than an internal estimate or marketing claim. This level of efficiency places the Cybercab roughly 28% ahead of the Lucid Air Pure, previously considered one of the most efficient EVs on the market. However, the achievement comes with important context: the Cybercab is a highly specialized vehicle designed specifically for autonomous ride-hailing rather than general consumer use.
The vehicle is a compact two-seat robotaxi that removes traditional driving controls such as a steering wheel and pedals. It also uses a relatively small battery pack under 50 kWh, contributing significantly to reduced weight and improved efficiency. Tesla’s design prioritizes aerodynamics and minimal e

item.title: Google Launches Optional Disco-Ball App Icons for Pixel Phones After Spotify Trend
item.description:
Google has introduced a playful new customization option for Pixel smartphones by releasing disco-ball-themed Android app icons. The feature was announced by Android ecosystem head Sameer Samat, who shared screenshots of a Pixel home screen filled with glittery, reflective icons and jokingly asked users whether they still wanted the feature after seeing how dramatic it looked in practice. The move was inspired by the recent online attention surrounding Spotify’s temporary disco-ball app icon, which the music streaming company launched as part of its 20th anniversary celebration.
The new icon pack is available through Pixel’s custom icons system, a feature added during Google’s March Pixel Drop update. That update expanded Android personalization by allowing users to apply AI-generated visual styles to app icons beyond simple color matching. Existing styles include themes such as “Scribbles,” “Treasure,” and “Easel,” and the disco-ball look is now another experimental option.
Although

item.title: Vintage Polaroid Photos of Donna Summer Highlight Her Career and Legacy
item.description:
This article from a vintage photography blog presents a collection of Polaroid images featuring Donna Summer, the legendary American singer widely known as the “Queen of Disco.” Alongside the visual focus, it offers a brief overview of her life and musical legacy. Born LaDonna Adrian Gaines in 1948, Donna Summer rose to international fame in the 1970s with groundbreaking hits such as “Love to Love You Baby” and the electronic masterpiece “I Feel Love,” which helped shape the future of dance and electronic music. She became one of the defining voices of the disco era and achieved remarkable commercial success, including multiple chart-topping albums and singles.
The article emphasizes her versatility as an artist, noting that she worked across genres including disco, rock, R&B, pop, and gospel. Her 1979 success included several No. 1 hits and recognition as the first Black artist to win a Grammy for Best Rock Vocal Performance, Female for “Hot Stuff.” After the decline of disco’s mains

item.title: Weekly Roundup of Nuclear, Climate, Geopolitical, and Media-Related News
item.description:
This article from the Nuclear News blog presents a broad weekly roundup of stories focused on nuclear energy, climate change, geopolitics, war, media criticism, and social justice issues. The post combines links and brief references to dozens of topics from around the world, reflecting the publication’s strongly anti-nuclear and anti-war editorial perspective. Major themes include concerns about nuclear power plant safety, the environmental and health risks associated with radiation exposure, criticism of Western foreign policy, and fears of escalating international conflicts involving countries such as Iran, Russia, China, Israel, and the United States.
The article highlights several nuclear-related developments, including debates over the cost and safety of nuclear energy projects like the UK’s Sizewell C plant, studies examining possible cancer risks near nuclear facilities, and warnings about military threats to nuclear infrastructure. It also references incidents involving drone

item.title: Teen Artist Struggles With Pressure to Continue Competitive Water Polo
item.description:
In this advice column, a high school student describes her growing emotional conflict between her identity as an artist and the pressure she feels to continue playing competitive water polo. The teenager explains that she has participated in club water polo since elementary school and became a varsity starter during high school. However, after being promoted to the top-level club team during the winter season, her confidence and enjoyment of the sport sharply declined.
She says her athletic performance had worsened, and she became increasingly anxious because her father placed strong importance on her success in water polo. The pressure intensified after a close friend on the team quit midway through the season, leaving her isolated among unfamiliar teammates. The student also shares that she has always struggled socially within team environments, and the combination of poor performances, missed shots, and visible frustration from teammates made her feel alienated and emotionally exha

item.title: The History and Modern Tradition of Post Office Bay in the Galápagos Islands
item.description:
The article explores the unusual history of Post Office Bay in the Galápagos Islands, considered one of the oldest and most unique postal systems in the Americas. Located on Floreana Island, the site began operating in the late 18th century when British whalers and sailors created a rudimentary communication system. They placed letters in a box or barrel so that passing ships heading toward the recipients’ destinations could deliver them by hand. Unlike traditional postal systems, the service relied entirely on trust, goodwill, and the cooperation of travelers.
The story also connects the Galápagos Islands with famous historical figures such as Charles Darwin, who reportedly left a letter there for his sister that never arrived. Over time, the original mailbox evolved from buried bottles and wooden boxes into the iconic barrel that still exists today.
Although modern communication reduced the practical need for the system, tourism revived the tradition during the 1960s and 1970s. Vis

item.title: Article Highlights Research Linking Low-Dose Radiation Exposure in Children to Increased Cancer Risk
item.description:
The article discusses concerns about the health effects of low-dose ionizing radiation exposure in children, particularly in relation to medical imaging and emissions from nuclear facilities. It cites comments by philosopher and risk analyst Kristin Shrader-Frechette, who references recent findings from university physicians indicating that radiation doses as low as 1 to 5 millisieverts may lead to a small but statistically significant increase in cancer risk among children. A millisievert is a measurement used to estimate the biological impact of radiation on human tissue. According to the article, millions of children in the United States receive X-rays and other imaging procedures every year, raising questions about the cumulative effects of even low levels of exposure.
The article also compares these medical radiation levels with the annual public radiation exposure limits allowed for nuclear facilities in the United States, which are stated to range between 1 and 5 millisieverts.

item.title: Anthony Scaramucci Says Trump Would Likely Insult Him Even After His Death
item.description:
Former White House communications director Anthony Scaramucci posted a highly critical message about President Donald Trump on X, continuing his long-running public feud with his former boss. In the post, Scaramucci argued that while Trump is politically powerful and charismatic to many supporters, he is not genuinely liked by most people around him. He claimed Trump relies on narcissism, intimidation, and personal attacks to maintain influence and react to criticism.
Scaramucci suggested that Trump responds aggressively whenever people publicly criticize his character or behavior. He then made a darkly humorous prediction, saying that if he were to die before Trump, the president would probably insult him publicly even after his death. Scaramucci added that he would personally find such a reaction amusing, even 'from wherever I end up,' emphasizing the sarcastic tone of his remarks.
The article notes that Scaramucci served only briefly in the Trump White House in 2017 before being d

item.title: Photographs Documenting the Extreme Winter of 1962–1963 in the United Kingdom
item.description:
The article presents a curated collection of 45 historical photographs illustrating the severe winter known as the Big Freeze of 1962–1963 in the United Kingdom. This period is widely regarded as one of the harshest winters in British recorded history, with freezing temperatures lasting from just before Christmas 1962 through early March 1963. During this time, temperatures dropped dramatically, reaching as low as -22.2°C (-8°F) in parts of Scotland. The extreme cold led to widespread disruption across England, Wales, and Scotland.
Snowstorms produced enormous drifts, in some cases reaching up to 20 feet (6 meters), which buried roads, railways, and even isolated entire homes. Major waterways such as the River Thames froze solid, and rare coastal freezing occurred in places like Kent, where sea ice extended up to a mile from shore. Transportation networks collapsed, causing severe shortages of essential supplies including coal and food. Milk deliveries were often frozen before reachin

item.title: Study Links Hypothalamic Menin Decline to Aging and Cognitive Impairment in Mice
item.description:
Researchers from Xiamen University reported new findings suggesting that a protein called Menin may play an important role in regulating aging processes in mice. The study, published in PLOS Biology, focused on the hypothalamus, a brain region involved in metabolism, hormones, sleep, and stress regulation. Scientists observed that Menin levels declined significantly in aging mice, particularly in neurons located in the ventromedial hypothalamus. This reduction was associated with increased neuroinflammation, memory impairment, reduced bone density, thinning skin, balance problems, and shorter lifespan.
To better understand the effect of Menin loss, researchers genetically reduced the protein in younger mice and found that they developed several premature aging characteristics. The team also discovered that declining Menin levels reduced the production of D-serine, an amino acid and neurotransmitter linked to learning and memory. D-serine synthesis depends on enzymes that appear to be

item.title: Photos Document Priscilla Beaulieu’s Teenage Years in West Germany Before Moving to Elvis Presley’s World
item.description:
The article looks back at a series of photographs showing Priscilla Beaulieu, later known as Priscilla Presley, during her teenage years in West Germany between 1960 and 1962. The period followed her first meeting with Elvis Presley in 1959 while he was stationed overseas during his military service. After Elvis returned to the United States in March 1960, 14-year-old Priscilla remained in Wiesbaden, Germany, where she continued attending school and living with her family.
According to the article, Priscilla’s appearance during those years reflected a typical teenager of the early 1960s. She wore minimal makeup, kept her naturally lighter brown hair, and often styled it casually with ponytails or bangs. The story describes how she struggled with public attention after Elvis left Germany, including reporters approaching her at school and uncertainty about whether the relationship would continue.
Despite the distance, Elvis and Priscilla stayed in contact through occasional late-night

item.title: Boston Blue Season 1 Finale Ends on Cliffhanger as Producers Address Character Fate and Season 2 Plans
item.description:
The first season of CBS’s “Boston Blue” concluded with a dramatic cliffhanger involving a violent car crash that leaves the fate of Det. Brian Rodgers uncertain. In the finale, Lena Silver survives a prior shooting incident, but her boyfriend Brian is later targeted in what appears to be a deliberate attack. After picking up flowers for Lena, his vehicle is struck by another car that forces him into the harbor, leaving his survival unknown. Showrunners confirmed they already know what happens to the character, and that the event will significantly shape the direction of Season 2.
Beyond the cliffhanger, the episode set up several major storylines for the next season. Danny Reagan considers taking the sergeant’s exam, partly to secure a higher salary that could allow Maria Baez and her family to relocate to Boston, signaling that the couple is still committed to building a future together. Producers emphasized that Danny and Baez are not breaking up, instead framing their storyline aro

item.title: Orange County chemical leak forces mass evacuations with uncertain return timeline
item.description:
Thousands of residents across multiple cities in Orange County, California, were forced to evacuate their homes due to a hazardous chemical leak at an aerospace plastics facility in Garden Grove. Officials expanded evacuation orders to parts of six cities, including Garden Grove, Cypress, Stanton, Anaheim, Buena Park, and Westminster, after a tank containing methyl methacrylate was deemed at risk of releasing toxic vapors and potentially exploding. Emergency shelters quickly filled, while hotels in the region also reached capacity or significantly raised prices due to demand during the Memorial Day weekend.
Evacuees described chaotic and stressful departures, often leaving with little notice and limited time to gather essentials such as medications, documents, and pet supplies. Many families reported confusion and anxiety, particularly about how long displacement might last. Local shelters, including community centers and school gyms, provided basic accommodations such as cots, food,

item.title: Vintage Photos Highlight the Evolution of Young Women’s Fashion During the 1960s
item.description:
The article published by Vintage Everyday explores the changing fashion styles worn by young women throughout the 1960s, a decade widely recognized for its cultural transformation and influence on modern fashion. The piece explains how the era marked a departure from the conservative and formal clothing trends of the 1950s, replacing them with youthful, experimental, and more expressive styles. One of the major influences discussed is the “Youthquake” movement, which shifted fashion leadership away from elite designers and toward younger generations.
The article highlights the popularity of the “Mod” style during the early 1960s. This trend featured bold geometric patterns, bright colors, PVC materials, and futuristic “space-age” aesthetics. Designers such as Mary Quant helped popularize iconic garments like the mini-skirt, which became one of the defining symbols of the decade. The article also references the rise of the A-line shift dress and Peter Pan collars, styles associated wit

item.title: Washington Post Publishes Daily Sports TV and Radio Schedule for the D.C. Area
item.description: The Washington Post published an updated schedule of sports television and radio programming available in the Washington, D.C., region. The article serves as a viewing and listening guide for local sports fans, listing upcoming games, broadcast times, and the channels or platforms carrying the events. One of the highlighted listings included a Major League Baseball game between the Pittsburgh Pirates and the Toronto Blue Jays scheduled for 12:15 p.m. and available on Peacock. The format of the article is informational rather than analytical, offering readers a convenient reference for sports coverage throughout the day. Such listings are commonly updated frequently to reflect schedule changes, broadcast adjustments, or newly added programming. The article appears to be part of a recurring service feature produced by Washington Post staff to help audiences keep track of live sports events across television, streaming, and radio platforms. While brief, the content is useful for sports vi

item.title: Debbie Harry’s 1982 Official Calendar Showcasing Early 1980s Iconic Photo Sessions
item.description: The article highlights the 1982 official wall calendar dedicated to Debbie Harry, the lead singer of Blondie, which has become a notable collectible among fans of 1980s pop culture. Although released for 1982, most of the photographs were taken in 1981, a transitional period in Harry’s career as she moved from her work with Blondie toward her solo projects. At that time, she was preparing to launch her solo debut album, KooKoo, which marked a dramatic shift in both her musical direction and visual identity. The album’s striking cover, designed by Swiss artist H.R. Giger, presented a darker, avant-garde aesthetic that contrasted sharply with her earlier glamorous pop image. The calendar itself features a series of stylized studio and location photographs, many of them described as intimate and candid. These images were taken by Chris Stein, Harry’s partner and Blondie bandmate, offering fans a behind-the-scenes perspective on her life at the peak of her early 1980s fame. The article als

item.title: Former Mueller prosecutor questions whether Trump immunity protects him in alleged IRS settlement fund case
item.description:
Former federal prosecutor Andrew Weissmann, who previously served as a senior deputy on Special Counsel Robert Mueller’s team, has raised concerns about whether President Donald Trump could face criminal liability related to a controversial $1.776 billion fund tied to a settlement with the IRS. In remarks made during a recent media appearance, Weissmann suggested that the arrangement may fall outside the scope of presidential immunity established by the Supreme Court’s 2024 ruling in Trump v. United States, which protects presidents from prosecution for official acts.
Weissmann argued that the creation and use of the fund may constitute a personal act rather than an official presidential action, which would make immunity inapplicable. He emphasized that if the conduct is proven to be entirely personal, then both Trump and any individuals who assisted could potentially face legal consequences. The fund originated from a settlement linked to a lawsuit Trump filed against the IRS, initia

item.title: UK nuclear site police report repeated internal security breaches involving lost sensitive materials
item.description:
The article reports on ongoing internal security issues within the UK’s Civil Nuclear Constabulary (CNC), the armed police force responsible for protecting nuclear facilities and escorting nuclear materials. According to findings obtained through a freedom of information request, the CNC recorded 35 security breaches in the past year. These incidents included the loss or theft of classified or sensitive items, such as a classified laptop, police warrant cards used for officer identification, and identity passes belonging to armed officers. Some warrant cards were reportedly lost during courier transport, while other breaches involved contractors working without appropriate background vetting.
Additional incidents highlighted include confidential materials being mishandled, such as documents left inside body armor sent for destruction, unauthorized access to restricted information by staff members, and cases involving compromised personal data. The article notes that at least three bre

item.title: Study Finds High-Altitude Red Auroras May Reveal Underestimated Geomagnetic Storms
item.description:
A recent study conducted by researchers from Hokkaido University and the Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology has revealed that unusual red auroras observed over Japan may indicate that some geomagnetic storms are stronger than current measurement systems suggest. The research analyzed five auroral events recorded between June 2024 and March 2025 in Hokkaido, Japan. Scientists discovered that these red auroras extended between 500 and 800 kilometers above Earth’s surface, far higher than the 200 to 400 kilometers typically associated with auroras appearing at similar latitudes.
Auroras occur when charged solar particles interact with Earth’s magnetic field and collide with atmospheric gases. Red auroras are usually linked to strong geomagnetic storms, but in these cases the storms were only classified as moderately intense according to the Dst index, a standard method used to measure geomagnetic activity. Researchers believe the index may underestimate storm intensity because d

item.title: UK Audit Report Says Sizewell C Nuclear Power Could Cost Consumers More Than Hinkley Point
item.description:
A report from the United Kingdom’s National Audit Office (NAO) has concluded that electricity generated by the planned Sizewell C nuclear power station in Suffolk is likely to cost consumers more than electricity from the Hinkley Point C project in Somerset. This finding comes despite earlier government and industry claims that Sizewell C would be cheaper overall because its construction costs are projected to be lower.
According to the NAO, Sizewell C is expected to cost between £38 billion and £48 billion to build, around 22 percent less than Hinkley Point C. However, the report explains that the financing and pricing structure of the two projects differs significantly. Hinkley Point C agreed to a fixed electricity price before construction costs escalated, meaning its developer, EDF, absorbs many of the overruns. In contrast, Sizewell C’s financing model and higher borrowing costs are expected to increase the final price paid by consumers.
The watchdog estimates that electricity f

item.title: Photographer Steven Martin Captured Everyday Life in Miami During 1983
item.description:
The article published by Vintage Everyday presents a collection of photographs taken by photographer Steven Martin that document daily life in Miami, Florida, during 1983. The piece focuses on the atmosphere of the city in the early 1980s, a period when Miami was experiencing rapid cultural and economic changes. The article describes the city as a place where glamour, urban decay, multicultural influences, and growing modernization coexisted side by side.
The photographs highlight several iconic areas and landmarks throughout Miami, including Biscayne Boulevard, Coconut Grove, Little Havana, Watson Island, and downtown Miami. Readers are shown views of historic buildings such as the Freedom Tower, the Dade County Courthouse, the Coconut Grove Playhouse, and the old U.S. Post Office and Federal Courthouse. Other images capture ordinary street life, storefronts, sailboats, traffic scenes, and construction projects such as the Southeast Financial Center, which was still under development

item.title: Fox Releases First Teaser Trailer for Upcoming ‘Baywatch’ Reboot Series
item.description:
Fox has officially unveiled the first teaser trailer for its upcoming reboot of the iconic television series ‘Baywatch,’ giving audiences an early look at the new cast and direction of the franchise. Actor Stephen Amell shared the teaser on Instagram on May 23, 2026, accompanied by the playful caption, “I dream in slow motion.” Although the preview is brief and does not reveal major plot details, it showcases Amell alongside Brooks Nader, Livvy Dunne, and Jessica Belkin wearing the franchise’s recognizable red lifeguard uniforms. The teaser also introduces the tagline, “Same beach. New blood,” signaling a modern continuation of the classic series.
According to the synopsis released through Variety, the reboot centers on Hobie Buchannon, the son of legendary lifeguard Mitch Buchannon from the original show. Hobie is now a Baywatch captain following in his father’s footsteps. His life changes dramatically when Charlie, a daughter he never knew he had, arrives hoping to continue the Buch

item.title: Mass Evacuations in Orange County After Chemical Tank Instability at Aerospace Facility
item.description:
A rapidly escalating industrial emergency at GKN Aerospace in Garden Grove, California, forced tens of thousands of residents to evacuate as a compromised chemical tank continued to deteriorate. The incident involved a large storage tank containing methyl methacrylate (MMA), a reactive industrial chemical used in producing durable plastics. According to fire officials, a failure in the tank’s cooling system triggered a dangerous rise in internal temperatures, increasing the risk of either a catastrophic explosion or a toxic chemical release.
By Saturday, temperatures inside the tank had risen from 77 degrees to around 90 degrees, prompting urgent concerns about thermal runaway—a chain reaction in which increasing heat accelerates further chemical instability. Officials warned that if the reaction continues unchecked, the tank could rupture or explode, potentially releasing hazardous vapors across surrounding communities. The evacuation zone, spanning roughly one mile around the facili

item.title: Françoise Arnoul, a leading actress of 1950s French cinema
item.description:
Françoise Arnoul (1931–2021) was a prominent French actress who rose to fame during the 1950s, a formative period for postwar European cinema. She became widely recognized as one of the era’s most captivating screen figures and was often described as a leading sex symbol before the international emergence of Brigitte Bardot. With her dark hair, expressive eyes, and natural screen presence, Arnoul embodied a blend of elegance, sensuality, and emotional authenticity that made her a favorite among major directors of the time.
One of her most significant collaborations was with acclaimed filmmaker Jean Renoir, who cast her in the 1954 film French Cancan. In this production, she played Nini, a young laundress who rises to become a vibrant cabaret dancer, a role that allowed her to showcase both her dramatic talent and her energetic charm. The performance helped cement her status as one of the defining actresses of French cinema’s golden age.
Throughout her career, Arnoul worked across a v

item.title: Officials Race to Prevent Explosion at Overheating Chemical Tank in Orange County
item.description:
A rapidly escalating chemical emergency at an aerospace facility in Garden Grove, Orange County, has raised fears of a potential explosion or large-scale toxic release. The crisis centers on a pressurized tank containing approximately 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate (MMA), a highly reactive chemical used in industrial processes. Officials report that the internal temperature of the tank has risen from 77 degrees to 90 degrees in a short period, moving closer to the chemical’s boiling point and increasing the risk of a dangerous reaction known as thermal runaway.
Fire authorities and chemists believe the heat inside the tank is being generated by MMA monomers reacting and forming polymers, a process that releases energy and can accelerate uncontrollably. The main complication is that a valve needed to inject a neutralizing agent is clogged, leaving officials unable to directly stop the reaction or drain the tank. As a result, responders are relying on continuous water spraying to

item.title: Mike Johnson faces criticism over inaccurate claim about Iran negotiations involving Trump
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson faced widespread criticism after he posted a statement on social media praising former President Donald Trump’s role in international diplomacy with Iran. In his post on X, Johnson claimed that Trump was the “ONLY” leader capable of bringing Iran, described as the world’s largest state sponsor of terrorism, to the negotiating table. He further suggested that a new peace deal involving Iran was underway and expressed optimism about learning more details. The remarks quickly drew pushback from political commentators, journalists, and online critics who pointed out that diplomatic engagement with Iran had occurred under previous administrations. Critics specifically referenced the Obama administration’s role in negotiating the 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran, which included limits on uranium enrichment and monitoring measures enforced by international inspectors. Opponents of Johnson’s statement argued that his characterization overlooked prior diplomatic effor

item.title: FAA clears Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket to resume flights after upper-stage anomaly
item.description:
Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket has been cleared to return to flight following a brief grounding triggered by a failure during its April launch. According to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), the company is now permitted to resume operations after reviewing the incident and submitting a corrective action report. The issue occurred during New Glenn’s third flight, when the upper stage experienced what Blue Origin described as an “off-nominal thermal condition.” This anomaly affected one of the vehicle’s three engines, causing it to generate lower-than-expected thrust. As a result, the mission’s payload, an AST SpaceMobile satellite, failed to reach orbit and was instead lost after burning up in Earth’s atmosphere. AST SpaceMobile later confirmed that insurance covered the cost of the satellite.
Despite the upper-stage failure, other aspects of the mission were successful. Blue Origin achieved a milestone by successfully reusing and landing the New Glenn booster for the second t

item.title: Civil War Veteran Samuel Decker Created Functional Prosthetic Arms After Losing Both Forearms in Battle
item.description:
Samuel H. Decker, a Union artilleryman who served during the American Civil War, became known for developing innovative prosthetic arms after suffering severe injuries in combat. In October 1862, during the Battle of Perryville in Kentucky, Decker was operating artillery equipment with the 4th U.S. Artillery when a cannon accidentally exploded as it was being reloaded. The blast caused the traumatic loss of both of his forearms.
Following the accident, Decker became dissatisfied with the limited prosthetic technology available during the 19th century. Rather than accepting simple cosmetic replacements, he spent several years designing mechanical prosthetic arms that would restore practical use and independence. Although he could not physically construct the devices himself because of his injuries, he carefully sketched the designs and supervised their assembly with the assistance of his wife and skilled craftsmen.
The resulting prosthetic arms were considered highly advanced for the

item.title: Fans petition Sony to approve Destiny 3 as Destiny franchise winds down
item.description:
A growing online petition is calling on Sony to greenlight Destiny 3 after recent reports suggest the Destiny franchise is entering a wind-down phase. According to the article, Bungie is ending major development support for Destiny 2, canceling planned expansions and shifting its focus toward other projects such as Marathon. While the game will remain online, it will no longer receive new content updates, effectively marking a transition into maintenance mode. In response, fans have organized a Change.org petition urging Sony to approve a full sequel, Destiny 3, arguing that the franchise still has strong creative and commercial potential.
The petition has gathered significant momentum, reportedly surpassing tens of thousands of signatures and continuing to grow rapidly. Supporters of the initiative emphasize their long-standing engagement with the Destiny universe and express a desire for a fresh entry that could revitalize the series and attract new players. Some community members b

item.title: Dietitians Recommend Eating Breakfast Within One Hour of Waking to Support Regular Bowel Movements
item.description:
A new article from EatingWell explains that eating breakfast within one hour of waking up may help improve regular bowel movements and support digestive health. Registered dietitians interviewed for the article say the colon is naturally most active in the morning, making early breakfast an effective way to stimulate digestion and encourage a bowel movement. Experts describe this process as part of the gastrocolic reflex, a natural response in which eating triggers contractions in the colon that help move stool through the digestive system.
According to the article, delaying breakfast for several hours after waking may reduce the effectiveness of this morning digestive activity. Dietitians also emphasize that maintaining a consistent breakfast schedule helps regulate the body’s circadian rhythm and can train the digestive system to become more predictable over time, especially for people dealing with constipation or irregularity.
The article highlights additional strategies that may

item.title: The 1970 Florence, Oregon Exploding Whale Disposal Attempt Using Dynamite
item.description:
In November 1970, a large sperm whale measuring about 45 feet long and weighing approximately eight tons washed up dead on a beach near Florence, Oregon. At the time, Oregon’s beaches were considered public highways, which placed responsibility for the carcass on the Oregon State Highway Division rather than wildlife authorities. As the whale decomposed, it produced a strong odor and became a public nuisance, prompting officials to act quickly to remove it.
After considering several disposal methods, including burial, engineers consulted with the U.S. Navy and decided to use explosives. The plan, led by engineer George Thornton, was to treat the whale like a massive object that could be broken apart using a large quantity of dynamite. Officials believed that detonating about 20 cases of dynamite—roughly half a ton—would disintegrate the carcass into small pieces that scavengers such as seagulls and crabs would consume.
However, not everyone agreed with the plan. A military veteran re

item.title: U.S. pauses $14 billion Taiwan arms sale amid Middle East conflict and improving ties with China
item.description:
A major U.S. arms sale to Taiwan valued at approximately $14 billion has been placed on hold, according to a senior U.S. military official, raising questions about the future of Washington’s security commitments to the island. The pause comes at a sensitive moment in U.S. foreign policy, as the administration balances escalating tensions in the Middle East with a simultaneous effort to improve relations with Beijing. The reported decision has alarmed both U.S. lawmakers and Taiwanese officials, who view continued American military support as critical to deterring Chinese pressure on Taiwan.
The situation reflects a broader strategic strain facing the United States. President Donald Trump is described as navigating a complex geopolitical environment that includes an ongoing Middle East conflict involving Iran, while also pursuing a diplomatic thaw with China. This shift toward détente with Beijing appears to be influencing defense policy decisions, including arms transfers to Taiwan, w

item.title: Disputes Over Renewable Energy Permits Threaten Bipartisan Permitting Reform Effort in Congress
item.description:
A report published by Politico and highlighted by Raw Story states that ongoing disputes over renewable energy policy within the Trump administration are threatening bipartisan negotiations on permitting reform in Congress. Permitting reform, which aims to streamline approvals for energy, infrastructure, and construction projects, has attracted support from both Republicans and Democrats as lawmakers seek ways to accelerate development and modernize regulations.
However, Democratic lawmakers argue that the administration’s approach to renewable energy projects, especially wind power, is undermining trust needed for a bipartisan agreement. Senator Martin Heinrich of New Mexico, the ranking Democrat on the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee, said the administration has slowed or stalled approvals for solar, wind, and battery projects while continuing to move fossil fuel projects forward. Heinrich described this imbalance as the biggest obstacle to reaching a deal.
According

item.title: Linus Torvalds Says AI Is Increasing Linux Kernel Development and Bug Reports
item.description:
During the Linux Foundation's Open Source Summit North America, Linux creator Linus Torvalds discussed how artificial intelligence tools are changing Linux kernel development and bug discovery. According to Torvalds, the Linux project has experienced roughly a 20% increase in commits during the last two kernel release cycles compared to previous years, largely because AI-assisted development tools have become useful enough for many contributors.
Torvalds explained that AI has both positive and negative effects on open-source development. On the positive side, AI systems are helping developers identify bugs that may otherwise remain hidden for years. He argued that finding and fixing bugs ultimately strengthens software quality and security over time. However, he also warned that the rapid growth of AI-generated bug reports is creating serious challenges for maintainers, especially for small development teams.
One of the biggest problems involves low-quality or incomplete bug reports

item.title: Authorities warn of potential blast impact zones at Garden Grove aerospace facility amid chemical tank crisis
item.description:
Authorities in Orange County, California, have released new details outlining the potential impact zones if a damaged chemical tank at an aerospace facility in Garden Grove were to explode. According to officials from the Orange County Fire Authority, the possible blast could result in a range of damage levels depending on proximity to the site. Within roughly 1,100 feet of the tank, structures could suffer severe damage, while areas up to about 0.3 miles away may experience moderate damage. A broader zone extending to approximately 0.4 miles could see lighter structural impacts. Officials emphasized that dozens of residential homes in nearby Stanton neighborhoods fall within the most dangerous zones, raising concern among local communities.
Emergency planners have issued evacuation orders across portions of Garden Grove, Anaheim, Buena Park, Cypress, Stanton, and Westminster as they attempt to prevent a catastrophic failure of the tank. Fire officials and health authorities have des

item.title: Behind-the-scenes promotional stills of Mel Gibson as William Wallace in Braveheart (1995)
item.description:
The article highlights a set of promotional still photographs featuring Mel Gibson in his role as William Wallace for the 1995 historical epic film “Braveheart.” The film, directed and produced by Gibson, tells the story of William Wallace, a Scottish warrior who led a rebellion against King Edward I of England during the First War of Scottish Independence. The narrative follows Wallace’s transformation from a peaceful farmer into a determined revolutionary leader after the tragic death of his wife, Murron, at the hands of English soldiers.
These promotional images capture Gibson fully in character, including the iconic blue face paint known as woad, long hair, and rugged warrior attire that symbolize the defiant spirit of the Scottish resistance. The article places emphasis on the visual presentation of the character during production, reflecting the film’s commitment to creating a dramatic and immersive historical atmosphere.
“Braveheart” became both a critical and commercial succe

item.title: Damaged Chemical Tank in Orange County Prompts Mass Evacuations Amid Explosion Risk
item.description:
Emergency crews in Orange County, Southern California, are responding to a hazardous situation involving a pressurized chemical tank that overheated at a GKN Aerospace facility in Garden Grove. The tank, which contains between 6,000 and 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate used in manufacturing plastic components, began venting vapors after a malfunction was detected. Authorities reported that pressure relief systems on the tank are no longer functioning properly, increasing concerns that the vessel could either rupture or explode if the internal temperature continues to rise.
As a precaution, evacuation orders have been issued for approximately 40,000 residents across multiple cities, including Garden Grove, Cypress, Stanton, Anaheim, Buena Park, and Westminster. Several shelters have been opened in local schools as displaced residents seek temporary refuge. Officials from the Orange County Fire Authority stated that efforts to cool the tank are ongoing, but early assessments were co

item.title: Florida Airport Licensing Deal Gives Trump Organization Control Over Branding and Merchandise Rights
item.description:
Representative Mike Levin, a Democrat from California, criticized a controversial agreement involving the renaming of Palm Beach International Airport to President Donald J. Trump International Airport. Levin argued that the arrangement has received insufficient public attention because it grants the Trump Organization extensive control over the airport’s branding, trademarks, and merchandise licensing rights. According to reports referenced in the article, the Palm Beach County Commission approved the agreement in a narrow 4-3 vote, with Democratic commissioner Maria Sachs casting the deciding vote.
The agreement was made with DTTM Operations LLC, a Trump Organization affiliate managed by Donald Trump Jr. that oversees trademarks and intellectual property for the Trump family business. Under the deal, the Trump Organization can select vendors to produce branded merchandise associated with the airport and license the airport’s branding to third parties. Although direct sales profits f

item.title: How 1920s Women Adopted Jodhpurs as a Symbol of Freedom and Modern Style
item.description:
The 1920s marked a major turning point in women’s fashion, as restrictive garments like corsets were increasingly abandoned in favor of clothing that allowed greater freedom of movement and self-expression. One of the most striking examples of this shift was the adoption of jodhpurs by women. Originally designed as practical riding trousers for men in military and polo contexts, jodhpurs featured a distinctive flared hip and tapered lower leg, making them well-suited for horseback riding and later aviation.
As the decade progressed, jodhpurs became closely associated with the emerging image of the “New Woman,” a cultural ideal representing independence, athleticism, and modernity. Women who embraced more active lifestyles found jodhpurs both functional and symbolic, as they challenged traditional gender norms in fashion. High-profile figures such as Amelia Earhart helped popularize this style, wearing practical flight and riding attire that reinforced her identity as a pioneering avia

item.title: U.S. House Committee Advances Bill to Make Daylight Saving Time Permanent
item.description: A proposal in the United States Congress to make daylight saving time permanent has taken a significant step forward, reigniting a long-running national debate over the biannual clock changes. The House Energy and Commerce Committee approved the Sunshine Protection Act by an overwhelming 48-1 vote, allowing the bill to move forward in the legislative process. The proposal has also received public backing from the President, who stated he would work to support and sign the measure into law if it reaches his desk. Under the current version of the bill, states would be given the option to exempt themselves from the federal framework governing time changes, effectively allowing flexibility in how daylight saving time is applied across the country. However, the bill still faces major hurdles, as it must pass a full vote in the House of Representatives before moving on to the Senate, where its future remains uncertain. Supporters of the measure argue that eliminating the twice-yearly clock c

item.title: 1954 Alfa Romeo B.A.T. 7 Concept Car and Its Aerodynamic Design Legacy
item.description:
The 1954 Alfa Romeo B.A.T. 7 is presented as the most visually striking and advanced of the Berlina Aerodinamica Tecnica concept cars created by designer Franco Scaglione for the Bertone coachworks. Revealed at the Turin Motor Show, the vehicle embodied a bold exploration of automotive aerodynamics during the 1950s, a period when manufacturers and designers were actively experimenting with futuristic forms and performance-driven shapes.
The B.A.T. 7 is especially noted for its dramatic, almost bird-like silhouette. Its design features sweeping curves and pronounced tail fins that were intended not only for visual impact but also to reduce air resistance. This emphasis on aerodynamic efficiency contributed to its remarkably low drag coefficient, allowing the car to achieve high speeds even when equipped with a relatively modest engine compared to conventional sports cars of the era.
Finished in a deep blue tone, the vehicle enhances its flowing, sculptural appearance, reinforcing the

item.title: Humorous and Creative Vintage Novelty Photographs from the Early 20th Century
item.description:
The article explores the playful and imaginative side of early photography through the lens of vintage novelty images. Contrary to the common belief that historical portraits were always serious and rigid, these photographs reveal that people in earlier eras also enjoyed humor, creativity, and visual tricks. Before the digital age and tools like Photoshop, photographers and subjects relied on practical effects to create amusing and surreal compositions.
Among the most popular styles highlighted are “tall-tale postcards,” which used forced perspective and double exposure techniques to make objects or animals appear dramatically oversized. These images often exaggerated scale for comedic or fantastical effect, creating scenes that looked like giants interacting with everyday environments or people. Another widespread trend was found at carnivals and boardwalk attractions, where “comic foregrounds” allowed participants to insert their faces or bodies into painted cutout boards. These set

item.title: Florida wildlife agency settles First Amendment lawsuit with fired biologist for $485,000
item.description:
Former Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission biologist Brittney Brown reached a $485,000 settlement with the state of Florida after filing a lawsuit alleging that her First Amendment rights were violated when she was fired in September 2025. Brown had shared on her private Instagram account a repost from a comedic whale-themed social media account that mocked conservative commentator Charlie Kirk following reports of his shooting. After the repost gained attention online, the right-wing social media account Libs of TikTok shared screenshots of Brown’s post and LinkedIn profile, publicly identifying her as a state employee and calling for her dismissal.
According to the lawsuit filed by the ACLU of Florida, Brown’s supervisors contacted her less than a day after the online backlash and allegedly told her to choose between resigning or being terminated. The Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission later announced her firing publicly, stating that it maintained a zer

item.title: María Félix: Life and Legacy of a Golden Age Mexican Film Icon
item.description:
María Félix (1914–2002) was one of the most influential figures in the Golden Age of Mexican cinema, widely admired for her commanding screen presence, striking beauty, and strong-willed personality. Often referred to as “La Doña,” she became a cultural symbol of independence and female empowerment during a time when traditional gender roles dominated Latin American society. Rather than conforming to expectations, Félix built a career defined by autonomy and artistic control.
Over the course of her career, she starred in 47 films produced across Mexico, Spain, France, and Italy. Her performances in acclaimed films such as “Doña Bárbara” and “Enamorada” helped solidify her reputation as one of the most powerful actresses of her generation. Unlike many international stars of her era, Félix famously rejected opportunities in Hollywood, choosing instead to remain loyal to Spanish-language cinema and to maintain creative sovereignty over her image and career.
Beyond her work in film, Marí

item.title: Collection of 1920s–1930s Postcards Featuring Actress Anna May Wong
item.description:
The article presents a curated collection of 25 vintage postcards featuring Anna May Wong, one of the first Chinese American film stars to achieve international recognition in Hollywood. Born Wong Liu Tsong in 1905, she began her acting career as a teenager and quickly rose to prominence in early cinema, including silent films such as The Toll of the Sea (1922), the first feature film shot in two-color Technicolor, and The Thief of Bagdad (1924). Despite her talent and growing fame, Wong faced systemic racism in Hollywood, including discriminatory anti-miscegenation laws and the Hays Code, which prevented her from portraying romantic leads opposite white actors. As a result, she was often confined to stereotypical supporting roles, such as the “Dragon Lady” archetype, while Asian lead roles were frequently given to white performers in makeup.
Frustrated by limited opportunities, Wong moved to Europe in the late 1920s, where she found greater artistic freedom in Germany and the United

item.title: Suspect killed after firing on Secret Service outside White House; bystander critically injured
item.description:
A shooting incident occurred outside the White House in Washington, D.C., when a 21-year-old man allegedly pulled a revolver from a bag and opened fire toward U.S. Secret Service agents. According to reporting cited by CNN and referenced in the article, the suspect engaged in an armed confrontation with agents stationed outside the executive residence. During the exchange, multiple shots were fired, and the suspect was ultimately shot by law enforcement personnel. He later died after being transported to a hospital.
Authorities also confirmed that a bystander was struck during the incident, reportedly as a result of crossfire. This individual was also hospitalized and was reported to be in critical condition following the shooting. Initial reports indicated that two people had been wounded during the encounter, but subsequent clarification from law enforcement established that one of the injured parties was not involved in the attack.
The identity of the shooter has not yet been rele

item.title: White House Gunman Identified After Shooting at Secret Service Checkpoint
item.description:
A 21-year-old man identified as Nassir Best was named as the suspect involved in a shooting incident near the White House on May 23, 2026. According to reports cited by MS NOW and Raw Story, Best allegedly approached a Secret Service security checkpoint carrying a revolver and opened fire on agents stationed outside the White House complex in Washington, D.C. Secret Service personnel responded by shooting the suspect during the confrontation.
Authorities stated that Best was transported to a nearby hospital after being critically injured in the exchange of gunfire, where he later died. The incident also resulted in injuries to at least one bystander, according to an official statement from the Secret Service. Security around the White House was immediately reinforced following the shooting, with National Guard personnel deployed near the area.
Reports indicated that Best had a documented history of mental health issues and allegedly claimed to be Jesus Christ before or during the inc

item.title: Suspected gunman killed after exchange of fire with Secret Service near White House checkpoint
item.description:
An armed confrontation occurred near the White House complex on Saturday evening when a suspect exchanged gunfire with U.S. Secret Service officers at a security checkpoint. According to the Secret Service, the incident took place near the intersection of 17th Street and Pennsylvania Avenue NW, an area close to the Eisenhower Executive Office Building. The suspect, whose identity has not been released, fired shots at officers stationed at the checkpoint, prompting an immediate armed response from law enforcement. During the exchange of gunfire, the suspect was struck and later died from his injuries, as confirmed by the Secret Service.
Authorities also reported that a bystander was hit by gunfire during the incident. Details regarding the condition of the civilian have not been fully disclosed. The area surrounding the White House was quickly secured following the shooting, and federal law enforcement agencies began investigating the circumstances surrounding the attack. Officials hav

item.title: Physician Raises Concerns Over Unusual Use of Cognitive Screening by Donald Trump
item.description:
An emergency room physician and healthcare advocate has raised concerns about President Donald Trump’s repeated public discussion of cognitive screening tests, suggesting the behavior is medically unusual. Dr. Rob Davidson, head of the Committee to Protect Healthcare, discussed the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) during an interview on Jim Acosta’s program, where he explained that the test is not typically administered as a routine evaluation. According to Davidson, the MoCA is designed to detect early signs of cognitive impairment and is usually only given when there are specific concerns raised by a doctor or family member about potential cognitive decline.
Davidson emphasized that the test is not intended for frequent repetition or for use as a self-promotional measure. He noted that in normal clinical practice, a patient would not repeatedly take the same screening without a clear medical reason. While he stopped short of diagnosing Trump or suggesting any specific condition,

item.title: Drone Strike Near UAE Nuclear Plant Forces Reactor to Use Backup Generators
item.description:
A drone strike near the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates temporarily cut off external power to reactor number 3, forcing the facility to rely on emergency diesel generators for approximately 24 hours. According to UAE authorities, three drones launched from Iraqi territory targeted the site. Two drones were intercepted, while one caused a fire near the plant’s electrical infrastructure. Officials stated that the strike occurred outside the plant’s inner security perimeter and that no radioactive material was released.
The incident has raised international concerns about the vulnerability of civilian nuclear facilities during armed conflicts. Experts cited in the report explained that the loss of off-site power may have been linked to damage to the switch yard, a critical system responsible for routing electricity into and out of the facility. Although the Barakah complex contains four reactors that could theoretically support one another, the disruption was seri

item.title: Concerns rise over Elon Musk’s shifting stance on terrestrial solar energy amid push for space-based power
item.description:
A recent TechCrunch analysis examines whether Elon Musk has effectively abandoned his earlier commitment to solar power and a fully electrified, renewable energy economy on Earth. Tesla’s “Master Plans” historically emphasized transitioning away from fossil fuels toward a solar-electric future. However, new developments across Musk’s companies suggest a more complex and potentially contradictory direction.
The article highlights that xAI, one of Musk’s newer ventures, has relied heavily on natural gas turbines to power its data centers, signaling a continued dependence on fossil fuels. Reports indicate plans to invest billions more into such infrastructure, raising questions about alignment with Tesla’s original clean-energy vision. At the same time, inter-company transactions continue, such as SpaceX purchasing Tesla Cybertrucks and xAI investing in Tesla Megapack battery systems for energy management. Despite these ties, there has been little evidence of large-scale adoption of sola

item.title: A Look Back at Victoria Principal’s Early Career in the 1970s
item.description:
This article presents a curated collection of 30 photographs highlighting American actress Victoria Principal during her early career in the 1970s, a formative decade that shaped her path to international fame. Born Vicki Ree Principal in 1950, she moved to Los Angeles in 1970 with the ambition of pursuing acting. Shortly after arriving, she began landing notable roles that demonstrated her range and screen presence. One of her earliest major recognitions came in 1972 when she portrayed Marie Elena in “The Life and Times of Judge Roy Bean,” a performance that earned her a Golden Globe nomination for Most Promising Newcomer.
Throughout the early 1970s, Principal continued to build her profile in Hollywood. In 1973, she gained additional visibility while promoting her role in the comedy film “The Naked Ape,” including an appearance in Playboy magazine. In 1974, she appeared in the disaster film “Earthquake,” where she made a striking physical transformation by cutting her long hair into

item.title: Internal backlash emerges among Trump supporters over reported U.S.–Iran peace framework
item.description:
A reported emerging peace framework involving the United States, Iran, and a coalition of Arab and Muslim nations has sparked significant backlash among some of Donald Trump’s most vocal supporters. The controversy intensified after Trump announced from the Oval Office that an agreement had been “largely negotiated,” prompting reactions across social media, particularly on X, where self-identified MAGA supporters expressed anger and frustration over the reported terms.
According to the article, critics within Trump’s base argue that the proposed deal could be perceived as overly favorable to Iran. Some interpretations circulating online suggest the framework may allow Iran to retain certain nuclear capabilities in exchange for sanctions relief and the reopening of key trade routes such as the Strait of Hormuz. These perceived concessions have triggered strong criticism from conservative commentators and grassroots supporters who view the arrangement as inconsistent with an “America Fi

item.title: Mary Lou McDonald Backs Sinn Féin Candidate Janice Boylan Despite Tight Dublin Central By-Election Race
item.description:
Sinn Féin leader Mary Lou McDonald defended her party’s decision to select Janice Boylan as its candidate in the Dublin Central by-election after early vote tallies placed Boylan in second place behind Social Democrats candidate Daniel Ennis. According to the tallies, Ennis led with approximately 20% of first-preference votes, while Boylan followed closely with 18%. Green Party candidate Janet Horner and independent candidate Gerry “The Monk” Hutch were also competing strongly, along with Fine Gael’s Ray McAdam.
Speaking to reporters, McDonald stressed that the race remained highly competitive because thousands of transfer votes were still to be counted. She rejected suggestions that Sinn Féin was underperforming in Dublin Central, a constituency she described as one of the party’s strongest areas. McDonald acknowledged that she would prefer Sinn Féin to win the seat but emphasized that political contests are always competitive.
The Sinn Féin leader also defended Boylan personally, p

item.title: A Retrospective on Greta Plattry’s Influence on American Sportswear and Knit Fashion of the 1950s
item.description:
The article explores the career and lasting fashion legacy of Greta Plattry, a German-American designer celebrated for her refined sportswear and knitwear during the 1940s and 1950s. Born in Berlin in 1909, Plattry emigrated to the United States in 1937, where she quickly became known for blending European sophistication with the relaxed practicality of American fashion. Her work stood out for its elegant knitted separates, Tyrolean-inspired details, resort wear, and feminine silhouettes designed for modern active women of the postwar era.
The feature presents an extensive gallery of vintage fashion photography showcasing Plattry’s designs in leading magazines such as Vogue, Harper’s Bazaar, Glamour, and Junior Bazaar. Renowned models including Suzy Parker, Barbara Mullen, Evelyn Tripp, Carmen Dell’Orefice, Elsa Martinelli, and Anne St. Marie modeled her clothing in photographs captured by legendary photographers like Richard Avedon, Horst P. Horst, Richard Rutledge, Louise Dahl-Wolfe

item.title: California GOP candidates court Central Valley voters in final push before primary
item.description:
In the final stretch before California’s primary election, Republican gubernatorial candidates Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco and former Fox News host Steve Hilton appeared together at a forum in Clovis to make their closing arguments to Central Valley voters. The event highlighted a largely civil tone between the two rivals, who have spent much of the campaign criticizing each other but focused their remarks on common political opponents, particularly Gov. Gavin Newsom and Democratic state leaders. Hilton, backed by a Donald Trump endorsement and leading in some polls, and Bianco both sought to position themselves as the strongest Republican contender capable of advancing through California’s top-two “jungle primary” system.
Both candidates outlined broadly conservative agendas centered on public safety, water management, housing development, and regulatory rollback. They criticized state environmental and regulatory agencies and pledged to expand water storage infrastructure s

item.title: Suspect killed after opening fire near White House checkpoint, Secret Service says
item.description:
A security incident occurred near the White House on Saturday evening when an armed individual approached a security checkpoint and opened fire on Secret Service officers, according to federal authorities. The U.S. Secret Service reported that the suspect arrived at the checkpoint shortly after 6 p.m. Eastern Time, retrieved a weapon from a bag, and began shooting at uniformed officers stationed at the site. Officers returned fire, striking the suspect. He was later transported to a nearby hospital, where he was pronounced dead.
Authorities confirmed that a bystander was also struck during the exchange of gunfire. Officials said it was not immediately clear whether the bystander was hit by the suspect’s bullets or by shots fired by law enforcement during the response. No Secret Service personnel were injured in the incident. The agency stated that President Donald Trump, who was inside the White House at the time, was not impacted by the attack.
The area surrounding the White House w

item.title: Washington, D.C. area weather: Rain expected late tonight with unsettled conditions through Memorial Day
item.description:
This weather update from the Capital Weather Gang provides a short-term outlook for the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area, focusing on unsettled conditions extending through Memorial Day weekend. Meteorologists report that rain is expected to arrive or become more noticeable later tonight, contributing to a damp and changeable weather pattern. The overall forecast indicates that the region will remain under unstable atmospheric conditions for several days, with intermittent periods of clouds and rain showers rather than prolonged dry weather.
Temperatures are expected to stay on the cooler side compared to seasonal norms, especially during nighttime and early morning hours, when a chilly breeze may still be noticeable. However, forecasters note that this breeze should gradually weaken over time, allowing for slightly more comfortable conditions as the weekend progresses.
Looking ahead, the forecast suggests that while the weather will remain somewhat unsettled through Memorial Day

item.title: Guidance for Parents on Preventing and Identifying Online Extremism in Teens
item.description:
The article explores growing concerns about how teenagers can be exposed to and influenced by extremist content online, particularly through social media and algorithm-driven platforms. It references a recent violent attack in San Diego carried out by two teenage suspects who allegedly engaged with far-right extremist material online. Authorities reported that the attackers left behind a manifesto promoting hate, antisemitism, anti-Islam ideology, and violence, and investigators are examining the role of online radicalization in the incident.
Experts interviewed in the article explain that modern extremist recruitment no longer depends solely on in-person networks. Instead, digital platforms can create highly personalized environments where vulnerable youth may be gradually exposed to increasingly radical content. Psychologists describe processes such as “identity fusion,” where individuals develop a deep sense of belonging to online groups, sometimes blurring personal identity with e

item.title: Cosmic Voids Are Becoming Key Tools for Understanding Dark Energy, Gravity, and the Expansion of the Universe
item.description:
The article explores how cosmic voids, once considered empty and unimportant regions of space, are now emerging as some of the most valuable environments for studying major cosmological mysteries. These voids are enormous under-dense regions located between the filament-like structures of the cosmic web that formed after the Big Bang. Although they contain far fewer galaxies than typical regions of space, they are not completely empty and can span hundreds of millions of light years.
Researchers argue that cosmic voids provide unusually clean environments for observing the effects of gravity, dark energy, dark matter, and neutrinos because they contain little matter to interfere with measurements.
