Scientists such as Alice Pisani and Nico Schuster explain that new telescope surveys, including the Dark Energy Survey Instrument (DESI) and the Euclid space telescope, are dramatically increasing the number of known voids and improving the precision of observations. At the same time, advanc

item.title: Lindsey Graham criticizes proposed Trump Iran framework, warning it could strengthen regional militias
item.description:
U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, a Republican from South Carolina and typically a strong ally of Donald Trump on foreign policy, issued an unusual public criticism of the former president’s emerging Iran peace framework. Speaking on social media platform X, Graham warned that the reported terms of the agreement—described by Trump as largely negotiated between the United States, Iran, and a coalition of Arab and Muslim nations—could unintentionally strengthen Iran’s influence in the Middle East.
Graham argued that if the deal is perceived as allowing the Iranian government to survive and gradually expand its regional power, it could worsen instability in key conflict zones such as Lebanon and Iraq. He specifically cautioned that Iranian-backed groups, including Hezbollah in Lebanon and Shiite militias in Iraq, could become significantly emboldened. In one of his more striking statements, he claimed such an outcome would put these groups “on steroids,” suggesting a dramatic increase in thei

item.title: Deep Fission Pursues Nasdaq IPO Amid Financial Struggles and Technical Uncertainty
item.description:
Deep Fission, a nuclear startup focused on building underground reactors to power AI data centers, is attempting to go public once again through a traditional Nasdaq IPO. The company is seeking to raise approximately $157 million at a proposed valuation of up to $1.66 billion. This move follows a previously completed reverse merger with Surfside Acquisition, a Delaware shell company, which technically made Deep Fission a reporting entity but did not result in its shares actively trading on public markets. The company had also considered listing on the OTCQB, though no evidence suggests its stock ever traded there.
Despite the renewed IPO effort, the company’s financial and operational situation appears strained. Deep Fission’s filings include a continued “going concern” warning, indicating it may run out of cash within 12 months if it fails to raise additional capital. Its financial deficit has increased significantly in recent months, rising to roughly $88.1 million, while its cash r

item.title: Childhood Photos of Noel and Liam Gallagher Highlight Their Difficult Upbringing in Manchester
item.description:
The article reflects on the difficult childhood experiences of Noel and Liam Gallagher, the brothers who later became famous as members of the rock band Oasis. Raised in the working-class neighborhood of Burnage in Manchester during the 1970s and early 1980s, the brothers grew up in a household marked by poverty, domestic violence, and strong Irish-Catholic roots. Their father, Tommy Gallagher, was reportedly abusive and alcoholic, with Noel and their older brother Paul suffering the worst of the violence. The trauma affected both boys deeply, leading Noel and Paul to develop stammers that required years of speech therapy.
In 1982, their mother Peggy left Tommy and moved the children to another home in Burnage while he was away. Noel and Liam were forced to share a bedroom in both homes, a situation that contributed to the arguments and tension that would later define their adult relationship. The article describes Noel as introverted and creative, often isolating himself and teaching

item.title: AMD to Restrict Linux Access in Free Tier of Vivado FPGA Development Tool Starting 2026.1
item.description:
AMD has announced changes to the licensing model of its Vivado FPGA development tool beginning with the 2026.1 release, affecting the availability of its free tier. According to a report based on a Slashdot submission, the updated licensing structure expands support for additional FPGA devices in the free tier, which AMD positions as an improvement for users. However, this benefit is accompanied by significant restrictions, including the removal of certain debugging features and, most notably, the elimination of Linux support from the free edition of the tool.
The change is expected to have a substantial impact on hobbyists, developers, and small-scale FPGA users, many of whom rely on Linux systems for development workflows. While AMD has indicated that existing licenses for version 2025.2 will remain valid, allowing users to continue using that version indefinitely, uncertainty remains about whether installation and support will continue smoothly once newer releases are introduced. A

item.title: A Look Back at 1950s Practical Motorist Magazine Covers and DIY Car Culture
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday explores the history and cultural significance of the British magazine Practical Motorist, first published in 1934 by George Newnes. The magazine became a trusted resource for car owners who preferred maintaining and repairing their own vehicles instead of relying on professional mechanics. During the post-war years, when automobile ownership expanded rapidly across Britain, Practical Motorist offered practical guidance that helped ordinary drivers better understand and care for their cars.
The publication featured detailed repair instructions, technical diagrams, maintenance advice, and cost-saving mechanical tips covering everything from engine tuning to bodywork restoration. Its approachable style encouraged motorists to develop hands-on skills and fostered a community of enthusiastic DIY drivers who took pride in maintaining reliable vehicles.
The article mainly highlights a large collection of Practical Motorist magazine covers from the 1950s. T

item.title: Photograph of Norman Lindsay and Rose Soady Highlights Their Artistic Partnership in Early 20th-Century Australia
item.description:
A historical article published by Vintage Everyday revisits a rare circa-1909 photograph of Australian artist Norman Lindsay and his wife and muse, Rose Soady, taken by Norman’s brother Lionel Lindsay inside the artist’s Bond Street studio in Sydney. The image provides insight into the personal and creative life of one of Australia’s most influential artists of the early twentieth century.
Norman Lindsay, born in 1879, became widely recognized for his provocative paintings, illustrations, and etchings that often challenged conservative social standards of the time. Rose Soady, born in 1885, played a central role in both his artistic and personal life. Introduced to Lindsay in 1902 by artist Julian Ashton, she quickly became his primary model and inspiration, appearing in many of his best-known works. Their relationship began while Lindsay was still married to his first wife, and after many years together they officially married in 1920.
The article also emphasizes Rose Lindsay’s own

item.title: Vintage Fashion Portraits of Model Ludmila Feodoseyevna (“Lud”) in the 1930s and 1940s
item.description:
This article presents a curated collection of vintage fashion portraits featuring Ludmila Feodoseyevna, a model known simply as “Lud,” who became prominent in the 1930s and 1940s fashion scene. Born in St. Petersburg in 1913 to a Russian vice-governor’s family, Lud is described as having fled the Russian Revolution and later establishing herself in Paris, where her aristocratic demeanor and striking appearance quickly attracted leading photographers and fashion houses. She rose to prominence as one of the most sought-after models of her era, frequently photographed by influential figures such as Horst P. Horst and other notable fashion photographers of the time.
The article highlights her collaborations with major designers including Jean Patou, Elsa Schiaparelli, Lucien Lelong, Balenciaga, Mainbocher, and Madame Grès (Alix), showcasing the evolution of high fashion across the late interwar period and World War II era. Lud’s images are described wearing elegant evening gowns, tailored

item.title: The 1939 Survival of Boy Scout Donn Fendler on Mount Katahdin
item.description:
In July 1939, 12-year-old Donn Fendler from Rye, New York, became the center of a massive search effort after disappearing during a family hike on Mount Katahdin in Maine. While hiking toward the 5,267-foot summit with his father, brothers, and family friends, Donn moved ahead of the group and became separated when dense fog suddenly covered the mountain. Attempting to find his way back, he became disoriented and inadvertently entered the vast wilderness of northern Maine.
Over the course of nine days, Donn survived alone in the forest, traveling an estimated 80 to 100 miles through rugged terrain. Drawing on Boy Scout training and advice from his father, he followed streams downhill in hopes they would lead to civilization. He survived by eating wild berries and endured extreme hardships, including exposure, insect bites, leeches, and encounters with wildlife such as bears and deer. At one point, he lost most of his clothing while crossing a stream and used a burlap sack as makeshift

item.title: Whole-body vibration training: benefits and scientific evidence on fat loss and muscle strength
item.description:
This article examines the growing popularity of whole-body vibration training, a fitness method that involves standing on a vibrating platform for short sessions, typically around 15 minutes several times per week. The practice is promoted by some advocates who claim it can help burn fat, improve muscle strength, and enhance overall physical fitness with relatively little effort. Users often describe the experience as standing on a platform similar in size to a bathroom scale while the machine produces rapid vibrations that cause the body to subtly or visibly shake.
Despite its popularity in gyms and wellness settings, the scientific evidence supporting these claims is mixed and more cautious than marketing suggests. Research indicates that while vibration training may offer some benefits—such as improving balance, stimulating muscle activation, and potentially aiding rehabilitation or physical therapy—it does not appear to be a standalone solution for significant fat loss. Any metabo

item.title: Reports of gunfire outside the White House prompt security response
item.description:
On Saturday, May 23, 2026, multiple journalists reported hearing what sounded like gunshots outside the White House in Washington, D.C. According to ABC senior White House correspondent Selina Wang, she and other reporters were present at the White House complex when they heard multiple loud bangs consistent with gunfire. As a precaution, journalists were instructed to immediately move into the press briefing room for safety.
Social media posts on X, citing coverage attributed to NBC and ABC, claimed that as many as 20 to 30 gunshots may have been heard in the vicinity of the White House. However, these figures remained unverified at the time of reporting, and no official explanation for the sounds had been provided by authorities.
The incident triggered an immediate and precautionary security response inside the White House press area, with reporters and staff taking shelter while awaiting further instructions. Despite the alarm caused by the reports, there was no confirmation from

item.title: IAEA Chief Warns UN Security Council of Severe Risks Following Drone Strike Near UAE Nuclear Plant
item.description:
International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi addressed the United Nations Security Council following a drone strike incident near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates. The attack reportedly caused a fire in an electrical generator located outside the plant’s inner security perimeter. Although the damage did not directly affect the reactor units, emergency diesel generators were activated to maintain power for Unit 3 until off-site electricity was restored. Authorities confirmed that radiation levels remained normal throughout the incident and no injuries were reported.
The UAE has stated that its investigation indicates the drones involved originated from Iraqi territory, with some additional drones intercepted before reaching their targets. Grossi emphasized that the situation highlights serious vulnerabilities at nuclear facilities located in conflict-prone regions. He warned that a direct hit on an operating nuclear power p

item.title: SolarSquare negotiates $55–60M Series C led by B Capital and Lightspeed amid India rooftop solar growth
item.description:
SolarSquare, a Mumbai-based rooftop solar startup founded in 2015, is in advanced discussions to raise a new Series C funding round led by B Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners. The round is expected to bring in between $55 million and $60 million, valuing the company at approximately $450 million to $500 million. This represents more than a doubling of its valuation since its Series B round in December 2024, when it raised $40 million at around a $200 million valuation. Existing investor Elevation Capital is also expected to participate, although the deal has not yet been finalized and terms could still change.
The company operates a full-stack residential solar platform in India, handling design, installation, and maintenance of rooftop solar systems for homes, housing societies, and businesses. Since its founding, SolarSquare has installed more than 150 megawatts of solar capacity and serves around 50,000 homes and roughly 400 housing societies across 29 cities in nine Indian s

item.title: J.K. Rowling Drafting Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone in Edinburgh Cafes, 1998
item.description: In 1998, renowned author J.K. Rowling was captured writing by hand in an Edinburgh café, a place where she developed significant portions of her first Harry Potter novel, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone. During this period, Rowling was a single mother living on welfare in Scotland. She often wrote in local cafés like The Elephant House and Nicolson's Cafe because they offered a warm environment while her infant daughter slept during walks. This approach was more economical than heating her apartment. Rowling preferred handwriting her drafts using pens, napkins, and sticky notes rather than computers. She meticulously planned the entire series this way despite facing numerous challenges. The manuscript was rejected by twelve publishers before Bloomsbury accepted it, launching one of the most successful literary franchises in history. From these modest beginnings in Scottish cafés, the Harry Potter series grew into a global phenomenon, inspiring books, films, and a massive fan c

item.title: Prior Mental Health Concerns and Extremist Online Radicalization Preceded Deadly San Diego Mosque Shooting
item.description:
A Los Angeles Times investigation revealed that Caleb Liam Vazquez, one of the gunmen responsible for the deadly shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego, had previously attracted police attention because of extremist behavior and mental health concerns. In January 2025, Chula Vista police obtained a gun violence restraining order after discovering social media writings in which Vazquez allegedly admired Nazis and mass shooters. Authorities noted he had been placed on a psychiatric 5150 hold, indicating he was considered a danger to himself or others. Police sought to remove firearms from the family home under California’s gun violence prevention laws.
According to court records, Vazquez’s father initially refused officers entry into the home but later stated that all firearms had been secured and transferred to a licensed firearms dealer. The family said they had taken steps to monitor their son’s online activities, secure weapons, and provide therapy and rehabilitation treatment.

item.title: Jim Acosta critiques political use of presidential cognitive tests after taking MoCA exam
item.description:
Former CNN reporter Jim Acosta, a frequent critic of Donald Trump, participated in a televised demonstration of the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) on his show, administered by Dr. Rob Davidson of the Committee to Protect Healthcare. During the test, Acosta completed several standard cognitive tasks, including drawing shapes, identifying animals, naming the date, and performing serial subtraction exercises. He reportedly required a minor prompt on one memory task but otherwise completed the screening successfully.
Following the exercise, Acosta argued that presidents of the United States should not be subjected to cognitive testing as part of their public role, emphasizing that voters should elect individuals without raising concerns about cognitive impairment in the first place. He suggested that the continued focus on such testing—particularly in relation to Donald Trump’s public comments about his own results—has become more of a political talking point than a meaningful asses

item.title: National Audit Office Report Highlights Elevated Risks to Taxpayers from Sizewell C Nuclear Project Financing
item.description: The UK’s National Audit Office (NAO) has released a critical report on the financing of the Sizewell C nuclear power station in Suffolk. The project, which aims to generate 3.2GW of electricity, received final investment approval in 2022 with an estimated cost of around £38 billion — nearly double the original projection. According to the NAO, the financing model shifts more financial risks onto taxpayers and electricity consumers compared to other energy initiatives. While the government argues this approach lowers overall financing costs and supports timely delivery, the benefits to consumers are not expected to outweigh the costs until after 2060. The report notes potential net benefits of up to £18 billion through lower energy bills and progress toward net zero goals, but these projections rely on optimistic assumptions. Concerns are raised about repeating issues seen at Hinkley Point C, which has faced significant delays and cost overruns. The Regulated Asset Base (RAB) model allo

item.title: Capitol Police officer Daniel Hodges criticizes GOP lawmaker’s claims that January 6 riot was staged
item.description:
Former Washington, D.C. Metropolitan Police Officer Daniel Hodges, who became widely known after being injured while defending the U.S. Capitol during the January 6, 2021 attack, publicly condemned recent remarks by a Republican congressman who claimed the riot was “staged” or fabricated. Speaking in a televised interview, Hodges reacted strongly to the lawmaker’s statements, calling them “disgusting” and emphasizing that real lives were lost as a result of the attack. He noted that not only officers but also rioters died in the aftermath, including deaths linked to trauma and suicide, underscoring the long-term consequences of the violence.
During the interview, Hodges rejected conspiracy theories suggesting the riot was orchestrated or exaggerated. He said there is no credible evidence supporting claims that the events of January 6 were staged and stressed that the fear experienced by lawmakers, staff, and law enforcement personnel was genuine. He pointed out that officers were over

item.title: Scouts and Volunteers Place 90,000 Flags at Los Angeles National Cemetery for Memorial Day
item.description:
Hundreds of Scouts from the Western Los Angeles County Council, Scouting America, along with veteran groups and community volunteers, gathered at the Los Angeles National Cemetery to take part in a long-standing Memorial Day tradition honoring fallen service members. On Saturday, participants placed approximately 90,000 American flags across gravesites, transforming the cemetery into a field of red, white, and blue in remembrance of those who served in the U.S. military. The event, which organizers say has been held for more than 50 years, is considered one of the largest flag-placement ceremonies in California dedicated to honoring veterans.
Families and young Scouts participated side by side, emphasizing intergenerational remembrance and civic engagement. Children as young as six were seen placing flags and learning about the meaning of Memorial Day through direct participation. Some Scouts saluted graves, while others carefully placed flags on headstones, reflecting solemn respect

item.title: Papa Penny explains resignation from MK Party and joins Floyd Shivambu’s movement
item.description:
Gezane “Papa Penny” Kobane, a veteran South African musician and former Member of Parliament for the MK Party, has publicly explained the reasons behind his resignation from the party. Speaking in an interview with Newzroom Afrika, he denied any personal conflict with former President Jacob Zuma, emphasizing that his departure was not driven by disagreements with the party’s national leadership. Instead, he alleged that internal issues within the MK Party forced him out, claiming that certain individuals operating within party structures—particularly in Limpopo—were working against him.
Papa Penny described these individuals as ANC-linked operatives who he believes were attempting to undermine the MK Party from within. He said he repeatedly warned the leadership about what he considered infiltration and internal sabotage, but his concerns were allegedly not addressed. According to him, the lack of action from leadership created a toxic internal environment that ultimately made his con

item.title: Early Behind-the-Scenes Photos of Audrey Hepburn in Monte Carlo (1951) and Her Path to Roman Holiday
item.description:
The article recounts a behind-the-scenes story from 1951 involving photographer Edward Quinn and a young, then-unknown Audrey Hepburn during the production of the film ‘Monte Carlo Baby.’ Quinn describes encountering Hepburn at the Hotel de Paris in Monte Carlo while observing rehearsals. At the time, she was not a leading actress and was credited under a slightly different name, with limited recognition in the film industry. Despite her minor role, her presence immediately stood out to Quinn, who was struck by her distinctive elegance and natural charm.
Quinn approached Hepburn and invited her to participate in an informal outdoor photo session. She agreed, and the following day they traveled together through Monte Carlo and nearby Eze Village in a small two-seater car. The experience was filled with challenges, including steep roads, brake issues, and even a moment when the car would not start, requiring Hepburn herself to help push it. Throughout the session, she reportedly remaine

item.title: Early Photos Highlight Diane Keaton’s Rise and Distinctive Style in 1960s New York
item.description:
The article looks back at actress Diane Keaton’s early years in New York City during the late 1960s, before she became one of Hollywood’s most recognizable performers. Born Diane Hall in 1946, Keaton moved to New York at age 19 to study acting at the Neighborhood Playhouse under renowned teacher Sanford Meisner. Because another actress already used the name Diane Hall professionally, she adopted her mother’s maiden name, becoming Diane Keaton.
In 1968, Keaton earned her first major stage role in the original Broadway production of the musical “Hair.” The article notes that she stood out among the cast because she refused to participate in the production’s famous nude scene, a decision presented as an early sign of her strong individuality and personal values. A year later, she auditioned for Woody Allen’s Broadway play “Play It Again, Sam,” where her performance as Linda Christie earned her a Tony Award nomination. The collaboration also marked the beginning of both a professional and

item.title: House Republicans’ committee vote on eligibility for Jan. 6 compensation fund sparks ethics concerns
item.description:
A report details controversy after House Republicans on the Appropriations Committee voted on a measure related to eligibility for a $1.8 billion fund tied to claims of government “weaponization” following the January 6 Capitol attack. According to Rep. Mike Levin (D-Calif.), the committee action would allow members of Congress, as well as President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance, to potentially seek payments from the fund created under Trump’s initiative. Levin said he introduced an amendment aimed at preventing sitting members of Congress, the president, and the vice president from receiving any compensation from the fund unless explicitly ordered by a court. However, he reported that all Republican members of the committee voted against his amendment.
Levin and other critics argued that the decision raises serious ethical concerns and could be interpreted as enabling self-dealing, allowing political figures to benefit financially from claims of victimization tied to gover

item.title: U.S. Layoff Rates Remain Stable as Tech Hiring Offsets Industry Job Cuts
item.description:
Recent labor data suggests that layoffs in the United States have remained relatively stable despite widespread public attention on job cuts, especially in the technology sector. According to reports cited in the article, layoff levels are close to or below pre-pandemic norms, even though they are somewhat higher than the unusually low levels seen in 2022 during the rapid post-pandemic economic recovery. A workforce-adjusted measure showed that layoffs affected around 1.2% of employed Americans in March, a figure that has remained largely consistent over several years outside of the pandemic period.
The technology industry presents a more nuanced picture than headlines may suggest. While major companies such as Meta have announced layoffs, economists note that hiring activity within the broader information sector has also increased. This means that new hiring has largely balanced out the number of workers being dismissed. Economists argue that high-profile layoffs at large technology

item.title: Vintage Portraits of Romanian Women Showcasing Traditional 'Ia' Blouses in the Interwar Period
item.description: The article presents a collection of captivating photographic portraits featuring Romanian girls and young women from the 1920s and 1930s. These images beautifully capture the essence of the interwar era in Romania, showcasing a harmonious blend of traditional customs and natural elegance. Many of the subjects are depicted wearing the iconic 'Ia,' a hand-embroidered blouse that represents regional identity and the rich artisanal heritage of Romania. The portraits were taken both in rural settings, particularly in areas like Transylvania, and in formal studio environments. The young women often have their hair adorned with flowers or delicate silk headscarves, adding to their ethereal and graceful appearance. Beyond mere fashion documentation, these photographs preserve the 'Romanian soul' by highlighting qualities of innocence, dignity, and cultural pride. The images serve as a valuable historical record of the period's social and cultural atmosphere. They continue to inspire contempor

item.title: Study finds association between living near nuclear power plants and higher cancer rates, but no proven causality
item.description:
The article discusses recent research exploring whether living near nuclear power plants is associated with higher cancer incidence and mortality. The study, conducted by environmental epidemiologist Yazan Alwadi from Harvard’s T.H. Chan School of Public Health, examined population-level data from Massachusetts and across the United States. It was motivated in part by public concern in communities near nuclear facilities, including areas with decommissioned plants, where residents reported perceived increases in cancer cases.
Using ecological study methods, the researcher compared cancer registry data with geographic proximity to nuclear power plants. The analysis suggested a statistical association: populations living closer to nuclear facilities, particularly within 5 kilometers, showed higher reported cancer incidence among older age groups. For example, the study reported elevated relative risks for certain age and gender groups living within a few kilometers of plants. A broader

item.title: U.S., Iran and mediators discuss extending fragile ceasefire amid rising tensions
item.description: Officials from the United States, Iran, and Pakistan reported signs of cautious progress on Saturday toward extending a fragile, month-long ceasefire amid ongoing tensions in the region. The diplomatic discussions come after a period of intensified engagement among the involved parties, aimed at preventing a renewed escalation in hostilities. According to officials participating in the talks, there are early indications that the ceasefire agreement could be extended, though significant challenges remain. The truce, which has held for approximately one month in the context of a broader U.S. military confrontation with Iran, is considered highly unstable and dependent on continued negotiations between regional and international actors. Diplomatic activity has increased across the Middle East in recent days, reflecting concern over the possibility of renewed conflict if the agreement collapses. While the parties have not announced a formal extension, the signals of progress suggest that d

item.title: Trump Says U.S. and Iran Are Nearing a Peace Agreement Involving Regional Leaders
item.description:
President Donald Trump announced from the Oval Office that the United States is close to finalizing a major diplomatic agreement involving Iran and several countries in the Middle East and South Asia. According to Trump, he held discussions with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman as well as leaders from the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Pakistan, Türkiye, Egypt, Jordan, and Bahrain. The conversations focused on a proposed memorandum of understanding related to peace and regional stability involving the Islamic Republic of Iran.
Trump stated that the agreement has been “largely negotiated” and is now awaiting final details before an official announcement. He also confirmed that he separately spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu regarding the negotiations. The president indicated that discussions are continuing over the final elements of the deal.
One of the most significant points mentioned by Trump was the planned reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a strategically

item.title: Review Says Drake’s ‘Iceman’ Is Overshadowed by Grievances and Rap Beef Complaints
item.description:
The article reviews Drake’s new 43-song project, split into three albums titled “Iceman,” “Habibti,” and “Maid of Honour.” While acknowledging Drake’s continued commercial dominance and ability to produce chart-topping hits, the writer argues that the project is weighed down by Drake’s inability to move past his rap feud with Kendrick Lamar. The review highlights tracks such as “Ran To Atlanta,” “Janice STFU,” and “Shabang” as examples of Drake’s musical versatility and hit-making talent. However, the central criticism is that much of the album focuses on complaints about betrayal, streaming numbers, and personal grudges.
The author compares Drake’s attitude to Donald Trump’s repeated claims about the 2020 U.S. election, suggesting that both figures struggle to accept defeat and frequently portray themselves as victims. Songs like “Make Them Pay,” “First Friends,” and “Dust” are cited as examples where Drake complains about manipulated streaming numbers and perceived unfair treatment.

item.title: AI whole-body mapping study links obesity to facial nerve damage and systemic inflammation
item.description:
Researchers from Helmholtz Munich, Ludwig Maximilians University Munich, and partner institutions have developed an advanced artificial intelligence system called MouseMapper that can analyze and map disease-related changes across an entire mouse body at cellular resolution. Using this technology, scientists discovered that obesity may have far more widespread effects than previously understood, including damage to facial sensory nerves and extensive inflammation throughout multiple organ systems.
The study involved creating highly detailed 3D images of transparent mice whose tissues were labeled with fluorescent markers. Advanced light-sheet microscopy captured millions of cellular structures, and the AI system automatically identified and segmented organs, nerves, and immune-cell clusters across 31 different tissue types. This allowed researchers to study how obesity affects the body as an interconnected system rather than focusing on individual organs in isolation.
In mice fed a h

item.title: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick calls for congressional oversight of Trump’s $1.776B Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description:
Republican Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick of Pennsylvania publicly criticized President Donald Trump’s proposed $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund,” describing it as fundamentally flawed and urging Congress to intervene. Speaking during a CNN interview, Fitzpatrick argued that the fund amounts to an inappropriate use of taxpayer money and that lawmakers have a constitutional duty under Article I to control federal appropriations. He stated that Congress should actively block what he sees as an overreach and ensure that public funds are allocated transparently and with taxpayer consent.
The fund has sparked controversy within Republican ranks, with several GOP lawmakers reportedly questioning its purpose and structure. Critics, including Fitzpatrick, have characterized it as resembling a “slush fund” that could potentially benefit political allies. The initiative reportedly emerged following Trump’s legal dispute with the IRS over the release of his tax returns, adding further political

item.title: Jane Wyman's Early Career: Photos from Her Time as a Warner Bros. Contract Player in the 1930s
item.description: Jane Wyman, born Sarah Jane Mayfield on January 5, 1917, was an accomplished American actress who enjoyed success in both film and television. She earned an Academy Award for Best Actress, multiple Golden Globe Awards, and received stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame for her contributions to motion pictures and television. Before achieving these milestones, Wyman spent the 1930s as a dedicated working actress at Warner Bros. Studios. During this period, she was frequently cast as a wisecracking, fast-talking blonde, a character type created by the studio that differed from her later dramatic roles. She started her career appearing as an extra and chorus girl in films such as Gold Diggers of 1933 and Anything Goes in 1936. She also performed briefly as a radio singer under the name Jane Durrell. In 1936, she signed a contract with Warner Bros., which led to her name change to Jane Wyman. By the late 1930s, she progressed from small parts to leading roles in B-movies like Public Wedding

item.title: Recent Developments in AI and Robotics Across Industry, Research, and Consumer Applications
item.description:
This news collection highlights recent developments in artificial intelligence and robotics, showcasing how rapidly the field is advancing across industrial, research, and consumer domains. The coverage includes breakthroughs in autonomous systems, humanoid robotics, medical applications, and software-driven automation.
One major highlight is Anthropic’s Claude model reportedly identifying around 10,000 critical software vulnerabilities in a single month, underscoring the growing role of AI in cybersecurity and automated code analysis. In robotics, innovations range from industrial collaborations to experimental humanoids. Google’s partnership with Japanese robotics manufacturer FANUC aims to advance autonomous factory robots capable of flexible, next-generation manufacturing. Meanwhile, Chinese research initiatives are testing humanoid robots in real-world agricultural environments such as tea farms, demonstrating early steps toward replacing or assisting human labor in complex outdo

item.title: Google AI Search Briefly Misinterpreted Queries Containing the Word 'Disregard'
item.description:
A reported issue with Google's AI-powered search experience caused unusual behavior when users searched for the word 'disregard'. According to TechCrunch and discussions highlighted on Slashdot, Google's AI Overview responded to the single-word query with a message saying, 'Understood. Let me know whenever you have a new prompt or question!' instead of providing a dictionary definition or standard search results. The response also left a large blank area on the page, while the expected links and definitions were pushed much farther down. The glitch appeared shortly after Google expanded its new AI-focused search interface, which emphasizes AI-generated summaries above traditional search links.
Commenters speculated that the AI system mistakenly interpreted the word 'disregard' as an instruction directed at the AI itself rather than as a search term. Some users suggested the issue may have been related to prompt-injection protections designed to prevent people from manipulating AI syst

item.title: Insider claims Elise Stefanik’s brief New York governor bid followed withdrawal of Trump UN nomination
item.description:
According to reporting cited by Raw Story and Politico, Rep. Elise Stefanik’s short-lived campaign for governor of New York was not the result of a long-planned political strategy, but rather a reaction to a setback involving former President Donald Trump. Sources described the effort as being launched after Trump withdrew his nomination of Stefanik for U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, a role she had prepared for by stepping away from her House leadership position, leaving committee assignments, and releasing staff tied to her congressional office.
The withdrawal of the nomination reportedly left Stefanik politically and professionally stranded after she had already made significant career adjustments in anticipation of the UN post. According to a New York Republican cited in the Politico report, the decision to enter the gubernatorial race in New York was driven more by frustration than long-term planning, with one insider characterizing it as being launched “almost out of spit

item.title: Apple, Google, and WhatsApp Expand Security Features to Defend Users Against Advanced Spyware Attacks
item.description:
The article explains how advanced spyware attacks targeting journalists, activists, political dissidents, and civil society members have become increasingly common and sophisticated. It highlights recent incidents involving Israeli spyware company Paragon Solutions, whose Graphite spyware allegedly targeted WhatsApp and iPhone users through zero-click attacks, allowing devices to be compromised without victims clicking on malicious links. The piece notes that spyware gives attackers extensive control over smartphones, including access to calls, messages, photos, microphones, cameras, and location data.
To counter these threats, major technology companies such as Apple, Google, and Meta have introduced optional security protections designed specifically for high-risk users. Apple’s Lockdown Mode limits many iPhone and macOS functions, including attachment handling, FaceTime calls from unknown users, certain web technologies, and public Wi-Fi connections, in exchange for stronger protec

item.title: Unplanned return of Vice President Vance to White House amid reported Iran security meeting sparks online speculation
item.description:
Reports circulated on Saturday indicating that U.S. Vice President JD Vance made an unplanned return to Washington, D.C., with his motorcade reportedly heading back toward the White House. The sudden movement coincided with President Donald Trump reportedly convening his national security team for an emergency meeting focused on Iran, triggering widespread speculation on social media about possible geopolitical developments.
According to conservative commentator Nick Sortor, Vance’s motorcade was observed rapidly returning to the White House, while Trump was said to have called together senior national security officials. Additional posts on X (formerly Twitter) suggested that Trump was also scheduled for a conference call with leaders of several Arab nations later in the day, further fueling online theories about heightened tensions in the Middle East.
Other commentators and influencers amplified the situation, linking it to unrelated but simultaneous reports, including Defense Secr

item.title: Lindsey Graham Questions Purpose of Iran War as Trump Reportedly Nears Peace Agreement
item.description:
Sen. Lindsey Graham drew criticism and confusion after publicly questioning the rationale behind the U.S. war against Iran, despite having previously advocated for military action against the country. His comments came as reports emerged that President Donald Trump was close to negotiating a peace agreement with Iranian officials to end the conflict. Graham expressed concern that any deal leaving Iran with the ability to threaten the Strait of Hormuz or damage Gulf oil infrastructure would significantly shift the regional balance of power and create long-term security risks for Israel and neighboring countries.
In a social media post, Graham argued that if Iran retained these military capabilities, it raised questions about why the war had been initiated in the first place. Critics quickly pointed out that Graham himself had consistently supported aggressive military action toward Iran and other countries throughout his political career. The article notes that earlier in the year Grah

item.title: Loretta Young: Vintage Postcards Showcasing Her Career in the 1930s and 1940s
item.description: Loretta Young, born Gretchen Michaela Young on January 6, 1913, was a prominent American actress with a career spanning from childhood in 1916 until 1989. The article highlights a collection of 30 vintage postcards featuring the star during the 1930s and 1940s, showcasing her evolution as a Hollywood icon. In the early 1930s, Young worked extensively for Warner Bros. and 20th Century Fox, often appearing in up to six to nine films per year. This period captured a more raw and complex side of her acting before the Hays Code enforced stricter censorship in mid-1934. She took on challenging roles such as a gangster's moll in Midnight Mary (1933) and portrayed ethically ambiguous characters in films like Employees’ Entrance (1933). Young starred alongside leading men including Tyrone Power in five films and Spencer Tracy. Her personal life also made headlines, notably when she became pregnant while filming The Call of the Wild with Clark Gable. As a devout Catholic, she concealed the pregn

item.title: SpaceX Successfully Conducts Starship V3 Flight 12 Test with Indian Ocean Splashdown
item.description: SpaceX achieved a significant milestone on May 22, 2026, with the successful launch of its next-generation Starship V3 vehicle on Flight 12 from Starbase, Texas. This marked the first test flight of the upgraded Starship design and the company's first Starship launch of 2026 after a seven-month hiatus. The fully stacked rocket lifted off from the new Pad 2 at 6:30 p.m. EDT despite one engine on the Super Heavy booster shutting down during ascent. The upper stage, Ship 39, continued its suborbital trajectory after stage separation, reaching space successfully by burning its remaining engines longer to compensate. During the coast phase, Ship 39 deployed 22 dummy Starlink satellites, including two specialized 'Dodger Dogs' equipped with cameras to image the vehicle's heat shield. The mission skipped a planned Raptor engine relight but proceeded smoothly otherwise. The Ship then survived peak reentry heating with no visible heat shield burnthrough, performed a controlled bank and flip man

item.title: WHO Raises Ebola Risk Level in DR Congo as Bundibugyo Outbreak Expands
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised the public health risk from the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DR Congo) from “high” to “very high” at the national level. WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said the regional risk across Africa remains high, while the global risk is still considered low. The outbreak involves the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which there is currently no proven vaccine. This strain is estimated to kill about one-third of infected patients.
According to WHO figures, the outbreak has caused hundreds of suspected infections and deaths, with dozens of cases already confirmed in DR Congo. Neighboring Uganda has also reported confirmed cases linked to travelers arriving from eastern Congo. Health officials say the situation in Uganda is stable so far.
Scientists at Oxford University are working to develop a vaccine using technology similar to that used for the AstraZeneca Covid-19 vaccine. Researchers hope c

item.title: Supreme Court Conservatives Decline to Join Clarence Thomas in Challenging Protections for Intellectually Disabled Death Row Inmates
item.description:
A recent dissent by Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas in the Alabama death penalty case Hamm v. Smith revealed divisions within the court’s conservative majority regarding constitutional protections for intellectually disabled inmates facing execution. Thomas argued that the Supreme Court should overturn the 2002 Atkins v. Virginia decision, which ruled that executing people with intellectual disabilities violates the Eighth Amendment’s ban on cruel and unusual punishment. In his opinion, Thomas claimed there is no historical basis for prohibiting such executions and asserted that constitutional protections should return to what he described as their “fixed meaning.”
According to reporting highlighted by Slate writers Dahlia Lithwick and Mark Joseph Stern, Thomas’s position was considered too extreme even for several conservative colleagues. Chief Justice John Roberts and Justices Samuel Alito and Neil Gorsuch declined to join his dissent. Although those justices indicated some op

item.title: Trump considers Iran proposal as U.S. and mediators move toward potential framework to end war
item.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump held meetings with Gulf and other regional leaders as his administration evaluated a new Iranian proposal aimed at ending the ongoing conflict. According to officials, discussions have been facilitated through mediation efforts involving countries such as Qatar and Pakistan, with negotiators reporting that talks in Tehran have brought both sides closer to a possible framework agreement. However, significant differences remain unresolved, particularly regarding Iran’s stockpile of enriched uranium and the status of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated that diplomatic efforts are continuing behind the scenes and suggested that developments could emerge within days, though he emphasized that no final agreement has been reached. He reiterated that the U.S. priority remains preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon while addressing concerns about its nuclear materials.
Trump described the chances of reaching a

item.title: A Brief Spotlight on Frank Sinatra and His Musical Legacy
item.description:
The article titled “It Never Entered My Mind: Frank Sinatra – Sinatra In The Spotlight” appears to be a short entertainment or music-related post focusing on legendary American singer Frank Sinatra. Although the provided text contains only limited information, the article references Sinatra as the central subject and highlights his enduring influence on music and popular culture. The phrase “Sinatra In The Spotlight” suggests that the piece may have been intended as part of a recurring feature or tribute series examining notable moments from Sinatra’s career, his recordings, or his artistic legacy.
Frank Sinatra remains one of the most iconic performers in American music history, known for his smooth vocal style, emotional interpretations, and timeless standards. Songs associated with Sinatra continue to influence singers and musicians decades after their release. The title itself references “It Never Entered My Mind,” a classic standard that Sinatra performed during his career, indic

item.title: Steve McQueen’s First New Car: The 1958 Porsche 356 Speedster and Its Racing Legacy
item.description:
This article explores the history of Steve McQueen’s 1958 Porsche 356 A 1600 Super Speedster, a car widely regarded as the first brand-new vehicle the actor ever purchased and a key milestone in his transition into professional motorsports. McQueen acquired the black-on-black Speedster in late 1958 from a dealership in Alhambra, California, while his acting career was rising through the TV series “Tales of Wells Fargo.” The vehicle was specially configured with a 1600cc Super engine producing around 75 horsepower, along with rare Rudge knock-off racing wheels and a cigarette lighter mounted on the steering column. McQueen also modified the car by removing exterior badges to achieve a cleaner appearance.
The Porsche became central to McQueen’s early racing career. In May 1959, he entered his first official Sports Car Club of America race in Santa Barbara, where he won his class against a large field of novice drivers. He continued to race the Speedster throughout 1959 at prominent Amer

item.title: D.C. mayoral race highlights tensions over federal cooperation, policing, and city management
item.description:
The race to become the next mayor of Washington, D.C. is intensifying, with seven Democratic candidates competing to replace Mayor Muriel Bowser in a contest shaped by complex relationships between the city government and the federal government, particularly under President Donald Trump. The role of D.C. mayor is described by candidates as requiring a careful balance between defending local home rule and cooperating with federal initiatives that influence public safety, infrastructure, and city aesthetics.
Key issues in the race include the management of public spaces such as parks and national monuments, funding for infrastructure improvements, and oversight of the Metropolitan Police Department (MPD). A symbolic example discussed by candidates is the Meridian Hill Park fountain, which has remained shut off since 2019. Candidate Janeese Lewis George emphasized the need for sustained federal investment rather than one-time funding, noting concerns about instability in National Park Se

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth warns West Point graduates they may be deployed to war amid rising geopolitical tensions
item.description:
U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth delivered a commencement address at the U.S. Military Academy at West Point, where he told graduating cadets that their next assignment could potentially involve combat. Speaking before nearly 1,000 graduates, Hegseth stated that the administration was sending them to lead and develop soldiers, adding that they may “perhaps” be sent to war. He emphasized that the world is currently at a critical geopolitical crossroads and that the graduates are entering military service at a moment of heightened global instability.
His remarks drew attention because of the explicit acknowledgment that newly commissioned officers could soon face active conflict. The speech comes amid increased speculation about potential U.S. military actions abroad, particularly regarding Iran. Several reports suggest that President Donald Trump has been considering possible strikes against Iranian targets, with tensions rising around an already fragile ceasefire. The article no

item.title: Rare Rehearsal Photos Highlight Rowan Atkinson’s Early Comedy Work on “Not the Nine O’Clock News”
item.description:
The article looks back at Rowan Atkinson’s early television career through a collection of behind-the-scenes rehearsal photographs from the British sketch comedy series “Not the Nine O’Clock News,” which aired from 1979 to 1982. Long before becoming internationally famous as Mr. Bean or starring in “Blackadder,” Atkinson gained recognition on the satirical BBC program alongside Mel Smith, Griff Rhys Jones, and Pamela Stephenson. The show was created as a fast-paced and irreverent alternative to traditional television news programming and quickly became influential in British comedy.
The piece highlights Atkinson’s distinctive performance style, especially his expressive facial acting and ability to portray both highly intelligent and absurdly foolish characters. Several memorable sketches are mentioned, including “Gerald the Gorilla,” in which Atkinson plays an articulate gorilla who appears more sophisticated than the scientist studying him. Other examples include his exaggerated aut

item.title: Ro Khanna Criticizes Trump After AI-Generated Social Media Attack
item.description:
Representative Ro Khanna, a Democrat from California, publicly responded to a social media attack launched by President Donald Trump on Saturday. Trump used his Truth Social platform to post an insulting message directed at Khanna, calling him derogatory names and sharing what appeared to be an AI-generated image portraying the congressman with exaggerated and demonic features, including horns and vampire-like fangs while holding cash. The post quickly drew criticism from political commentators and media figures, who described the image and Trump’s behavior as extreme and inappropriate.
Independent journalist Aaron Rupar labeled the image “absolutely demented,” while political commentator Cenk Uygur called it offensive. Instead of directly escalating the conflict, Khanna responded with sarcasm and criticism aimed at Trump’s priorities. In a post on X, Khanna congratulated Donald Trump Jr. on his wedding in the Bahamas and mocked the president for reportedly skipping the family event w

item.title: Study Shows Vitamin D2 Supplements May Reduce Beneficial Vitamin D3 Levels
item.description: A new study from the University of Surrey, in collaboration with the John Innes Centre and Quadram Institute Bioscience, has raised concerns about vitamin D2 supplements. Researchers found that taking vitamin D2, a common form of the supplement derived from plants, can actually lower the body's levels of vitamin D3, which is the form naturally produced through sunlight exposure and considered more effective for raising overall vitamin D status. The meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials, published in Nutrition Reviews, showed consistent drops in vitamin D3 concentrations among participants using D2 supplements compared to control groups. Vitamin D is crucial for bone health, immune function, and overall wellness, particularly in regions like the UK where winter sunlight is insufficient for natural production. The UK government recommends 10 micrograms daily, especially from October to March. Lead researcher Emily Brown emphasized that while supplements are important during low-s

item.title: John Kasich declines endorsement in Ohio Senate race, predicts close contest between Husted and Brown
item.description:
Former Ohio Republican Governor John Kasich declined to endorse Senator Jon Husted in his reelection bid during a recent appearance on MS NOW’s ‘The Weekend,’ offering a cautious and noncommittal view of the upcoming Ohio Senate race. When directly pressed on whether he would support Husted or Democratic challenger Sherrod Brown, Kasich refused to reveal his voting intentions, stating that he does not publicly disclose how he votes. His comments come at a time of heightened political tension within the Republican Party and growing national attention on key Senate races expected to shape control of Congress.
Kasich characterized the Ohio race as highly competitive, suggesting it could be decided by narrow margins. He noted that Husted faces challenges despite his incumbency, particularly in building statewide name recognition and maintaining political advantages typically associated with the office. He also acknowledged Sherrod Brown’s long political career and name recognition, while

item.title: Sandra Bullock Helped Demonstrate One of the First Online Movie Ticket Purchases in 1995
item.description:
In 1995, actress Sandra Bullock participated in what became an important early moment in the history of online commerce when she purchased movie tickets over the internet to promote her thriller film The Net. At a time when internet shopping was still experimental and unfamiliar to most people, Bullock used the MovieLink website to buy two tickets for a screening at the Egyptian Theater in Hollywood. The total purchase cost was reported to be $17.70. The event was filmed for an Entertainment Tonight segment, showing Bullock using a large desktop computer and manually entering credit card information, highlighting how different online transactions were during the early days of the web.
The promotion was closely tied to the themes of The Net, a film focused on computers, identity, and the growing influence of digital technology. The online ticket purchase was intended to demonstrate the possibilities of internet-based commerce and increase public interest in both the movie and emerging

item.title: Ferrari and IBM expand AI-powered fan engagement through personalized Formula 1 app experiences
item.description:
Ferrari and IBM have partnered to improve fan engagement in Formula One by redesigning Ferrari’s fan application with artificial intelligence-powered features and personalized content. IBM, which has been expanding its presence in sports technology partnerships, selected Scuderia Ferrari HP because of the team’s historic success and global popularity. The collaboration focuses on using AI and racing data to strengthen the connection between Ferrari and its worldwide fan community, known as the Tifosi.
The updated Ferrari app introduces several new features designed to keep fans engaged throughout the year instead of only during race weekends. These additions include AI-generated race summaries, prediction games, interactive fan competitions, behind-the-scenes stories about drivers and crew members, and an AI assistant capable of answering fan questions. Ferrari and IBM also improved localization, including making the app available in Italian for the first time.
According to IBM execu

item.title: A 1909 Lewis Hine Photograph of Mrs. S.J. Bonner and Her Sons Working in a Georgia Textile Mill
item.description:
The article presents a historical photograph taken in 1909 by documentary photographer Lewis Hine in Macon, Georgia, as part of his work for the National Child Labor Committee. The image shows Mrs. S.J. Bonner alongside her two sons, all of whom were employed at Bibb Mill No. 1. The family’s circumstances reflect the economic hardships faced by many working-class Americans in the early 20th century, particularly in industrializing Southern states. After the death of her husband, Mrs. Bonner and her children left their farm and moved to the city in search of stable income, ultimately finding work in the textile mills. Mrs. Bonner earned approximately $3.50 per week, while her two sons together earned about $4.90, highlighting how entire families, including children, were often required to contribute financially to survive.
The text emphasizes the harsh realities of mill work during this era, including long hours, low wages, and difficult working conditions. Child labor was a common and

item.title: Troy Cassar-Daley supports winter vaccination campaign urging First Nations Australians to get protected
item.description:
As winter approaches in Australia, health authorities are warning of a rise in respiratory illnesses, including influenza, COVID-19, and respiratory syncytial virus (RSV). After recording the highest flu case numbers in five years the previous year, the Australian Government has launched the ‘Get ready for winter. Get vaccinated’ campaign to encourage people to protect themselves early. Country music artist Troy Cassar-Daley, a proud Gumbaynggirr and Bundjalung man, is supporting the initiative by urging First Nations Australians, particularly those aged 50 and over, to speak with healthcare professionals about recommended vaccinations.
The campaign highlights that respiratory diseases can cause severe illness, hospitalization, prolonged recovery, and even death, especially among older adults and those with underlying health conditions. Health officials emphasize that vaccination remains one of the most effective tools for reducing the risk of serious outcomes and lessening symptom se

item.title: New Climate Projections Suggest Extreme Warming May Be Avoided, but Paris Targets Are Likely Out of Reach
item.description:
New climate research suggests that the most catastrophic warming scenarios once widely discussed by scientists are becoming less likely, mainly because renewable energy adoption has accelerated and dependence on coal is declining in many regions. Researchers now believe the previous worst-case pathway, which projected approximately 4.5 degrees Celsius of warming by the end of the century, no longer reflects the world’s current trajectory. Updated estimates place the likely upper-end warming scenario closer to 3.5 degrees Celsius.
However, scientists also warn that the most optimistic climate goals are increasingly unattainable. According to the new scenarios, limiting global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels — the central goal of the 2015 Paris Climate Agreement — is no longer considered realistic under current global emissions trends. Even the best-case scenario now projects temperatures temporarily exceeding that threshold, potentially reaching around 1.7 de

item.title: Expert warns Trump risks escalation in potential military action against Iran
item.description:
President Donald Trump is reportedly considering a significant escalation in U.S. military actions against Iran, including the possibility of launching a new wave of strikes that could effectively end the current fragile ceasefire between the two countries. According to reporting cited in the article, Trump has publicly suggested the possibility of a decisive and overwhelming attack on Iran, heightening concerns about a broader regional conflict.
International security expert Robert Pape, a political science professor at the University of Chicago, warned that the administration may be falling into what he describes as a “smart bomb trap.” This concept refers to the belief that a single precise strike—potentially targeting Iran’s leadership—could quickly end the conflict by collapsing the adversary’s command structure and restoring deterrence. Pape argues that this assumption is dangerously misleading and historically comparable to early miscalculations in past conflicts.
He notes tha

item.title: Fernand Toussaint and the Elegance of Belle Époque Portraiture
item.description:
Fernand Toussaint (1873–1956) was a Belgian painter widely recognized for his refined portraits of women during the Belle Époque and the interwar period. Trained within the academic traditions of his era, he developed a distinctive artistic approach that blended classical realism with subtle Impressionist influences. His works are especially celebrated for their portrayal of aristocratic women in elegant domestic or social settings, often surrounded by luxurious interiors, delicate floral arrangements, and finely detailed fabrics.
Toussaint’s artistic strength lay in his ability to depict softness and atmosphere through controlled lighting and a delicate color palette. His portraits frequently feature women dressed in silk gowns, captured in contemplative or intimate moments such as reading, writing letters, admiring mirrors, or posing in quiet interiors. These scenes emphasize not only physical beauty but also mood, psychology, and a sense of refined stillness.
Beyond portraiture, T

item.title: Trump signals Sunday decision on Iran policy amid escalating tensions and reported military options
item.description:
According to the article, President Donald Trump indicated that a major decision regarding U.S. policy toward Iran is expected to be made by Sunday, as discussions within his administration continue over how to respond to escalating tensions. The report, citing Axios, describes Trump as characterizing the situation as a “50/50” choice between reaching a diplomatic agreement or potentially taking severe military action against Iran. He reportedly used highly aggressive language, suggesting that failure to secure a deal could result in the United States “blowing [Iran] to kingdom come,” underscoring the heightened rhetoric surrounding the situation.
The article states that Trump discussed the issue in a phone call with Axios reporter Barak Ravid, who later provided additional details in an exclusive report. The president is expected to meet with senior advisers, including his son-in-law Jared Kushner, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, and Vice President JD Vance, to evaluate Iran’s latest di

item.title: Surveys show declining Republican confidence in Trump’s handling of the economy
item.description:
Recent polling and survey data suggest that Republican voters are beginning to show reduced confidence in President Donald Trump’s handling of the economy, an issue that has historically remained one of his strongest areas of support. According to analysis cited by Axios and referenced in multiple surveys, including the University of Michigan’s consumer sentiment index and Gallup polling, overall economic sentiment in the United States has reached historic lows during Trump’s second term. While Democrats and independent voters have long expressed negative views of the economy, Republicans had largely continued to rate Trump’s performance positively.
However, new data indicates a shift within the GOP electorate. Earlier in the year, roughly eight in ten Republican voters approved of Trump’s economic leadership. More recent polling suggests that figure has dropped to around six in ten, signaling a noticeable decline in enthusiasm even among his core base. Analysts describe this as an ea

item.title: Linux kernel vulnerability enables local privilege escalation via ptrace and pidfd_getfd flaw
item.description:
Security researchers at Qualys Threat Research Unit have disclosed a serious vulnerability in the Linux kernel that allows local, unprivileged users to escalate privileges to root on affected systems. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-46333, stems from a logic issue in how the kernel handles credential dropping during process termination, combined with reachable ptrace-family operations and the pidfd_getfd() system call. This combination creates a window in which a process that should no longer be accessible remains vulnerable to interception of its file descriptors and inter-process communication channels.
According to the disclosure, attackers can exploit this condition to capture sensitive resources from privileged processes, including open file descriptors and authenticated communication channels. These can then be reused to access restricted data such as /etc/shadow, SSH host private keys stored under /etc/ssh, and other credential material. In some scenarios, the flaw can also be le

item.title: The Creation and Legacy of Queen Elizabeth II’s 1947 Wedding Cake
item.description:
Queen Elizabeth II’s wedding cake, created for her marriage to Prince Philip on November 20, 1947, was an extraordinary symbol of post-war celebration and unity. Standing approximately 9 feet (2.7 meters) tall and weighing more than 500 pounds (227 kg), the massive four-tier fruitcake was crafted by McVitie & Price Ltd. It became famously known as the “10,000 Mile Cake” because many of its ingredients were sourced from across the British Commonwealth, including sugar, flour, and dried fruits sent from countries such as Australia and South Africa. This international contribution reflected both cooperation and solidarity at a time when Britain was still recovering from the hardships of World War II and dealing with strict rationing.
The cake itself was richly decorated with royal icing and intricate sugarwork. Its design featured coats of arms, monograms of the bride and groom, and symbolic elements representing the unity of Britain and its Commonwealth nations. Beyond its visual grande

item.title: Former Kenyan Special Forces Officer Arrested in Nairobi for Possession of Ammunition and Military Equipment
item.description:
Detectives from Kenya’s Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested a former Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) special forces officer suspected of involvement in criminal activities in Nairobi’s Eastlands area. According to authorities, the arrest followed a tip-off from members of the public, which led the Operations Support Unit (OSU) to track the suspect to a hideout located within the Kayole dumpsite area in Embakasi. The suspect was apprehended during a targeted operation.
Following his arrest, the suspect reportedly led officers to his residence, where a search was conducted. During the search, investigators recovered several items believed to include government stores and military equipment. Among the items seized were one round of 9mm ammunition, military shirts, military working dress, a smoke jacket, military trousers, a military bag, and a military jackknife. Authorities indicated that the recovered materials raised concerns about unauthorized possession of military

item.title: NYT reporter says Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is under pressure amid criticism over alignment with Trump
item.description:
Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is facing heightened political and public scrutiny following a difficult week marked by congressional criticism and internal party tensions. According to remarks made by New York Times reporter Glenn Thrush during an appearance on MS NOW’s “The Weekend,” Blanche appears visibly strained in public settings, which Thrush attributed to the pressures of serving at the direction of former President Donald Trump without pushing back against requests. Thrush suggested that Blanche’s demeanor reflects the strain of his role, stating that he seems to be “reaping what he has sown” by consistently complying with Trump’s demands.
The commentary came in the context of recent contentious events, including a heated Senate hearing where Blanche faced questioning over Justice Department matters, as well as reports of a closed-door meeting with Republican senators described as highly hostile. These episodes have contributed to a perception of growing dissatisfa

item.title: Critique of Media Coverage and Climate Tipping Point Risks in a Warming World
item.description:
The article argues that mainstream media is failing to adequately report the escalating climate crisis, allowing political and economic systems driven by corporate interests to continue largely unchallenged. It highlights how urgent scientific warnings about climate change are often underrepresented in news coverage, while political drama and short-term events dominate public discourse.
A central example is the 100th birthday celebration of naturalist David Attenborough, where climate change—despite being a major focus of his recent work—was largely absent from the public narrative of the event. The article uses this to illustrate how even widely respected environmental voices are often depoliticized or sanitized in mainstream media contexts.
The piece reviews recent scientific findings warning that Earth may be approaching critical climate tipping points. These include potential collapse of major systems such as the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC), accelerated mel

item.title: Internal Republican Party divisions intensify amid disputes over spending bills and Trump influence
item.description:
This opinion article argues that the Republican Party is experiencing significant internal turmoil driven by conflicts over major spending decisions, policy priorities, and the continued influence of Donald Trump. The author describes a chaotic political week in which Senate and House Republicans struggled to maintain unity over legislation tied to immigration enforcement funding, proposed infrastructure spending, and controversial additions such as a costly presidential ballroom project. According to the piece, disagreements escalated as Senate Republicans clashed with party leadership and the Trump-aligned agenda, leading to canceled votes, stalled negotiations, and lawmakers leaving Washington without passing key measures.
The article highlights tensions between Republican senators and House members, as well as frustration with Trump-endorsed political strategies and personnel decisions. It claims that some GOP lawmakers openly criticized elements of the proposed funding package, i

item.title: Researchers Explore Quantum Jamming as a Way to Probe the Limits of Causality and Secure Communication
item.description:
The article explores a developing area of quantum physics and cryptography centered on a theoretical concept known as quantum jamming. Researchers working in quantum information science are re-examining the foundations of secure communication in anticipation of future advances in quantum computing and even possible post-quantum physical theories. While quantum key distribution currently relies on principles like entanglement and the monogamy of entanglement to detect eavesdropping, scientists are investigating whether these assumptions remain valid under more fundamental or yet-undiscovered physical laws.
Quantum jamming is a thought experiment in which an external agent could subtly alter the correlations between entangled particles without breaking the no-signaling principle of relativity. This would mean that while no information travels faster than light, the underlying structure of quantum correlations could still be modified in a way that affects cryptographic security. The idea

item.title: Officials Struggle to Stabilize Dangerous MMA Chemical Tank in Garden Grove
item.description:
Authorities in Orange County, California, spent Friday attempting to stabilize a large pressurized tank containing approximately 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate (MMA), a toxic and highly flammable chemical used in plastic manufacturing. The crisis began in Garden Grove when the tank unexpectedly increased in temperature, causing fears of a potential BLEVE, or boiling liquid expanding vapor explosion. Fire officials warned that such an explosion could create a massive fireball, spread toxic fumes, and propel debris over long distances.
Emergency crews focused primarily on cooling the tank with water sprinklers to prevent a thermal runaway reaction, a dangerous chemical process in which heat accelerates the reaction and generates even more heat. By Friday night, officials reported progress, reducing the tank temperature to around 61 degrees, although the target remained 50 degrees before safer mitigation efforts could begin.
One major complication involved damaged and clogged valv

item.title: GKN Aerospace facility linked to Orange County chemical tank emergency in Garden Grove
item.description:
A chemical emergency in Garden Grove, Orange County, has drawn attention to GKN Aerospace, a UK-based aerospace manufacturer operating a large facility on Western Avenue. The incident centers on a tank containing methyl methacrylate (MMA), a highly toxic industrial chemical used in plastics production. At least three large storage tanks are present at the site, with one holding approximately 7,000 gallons reportedly experiencing dangerous temperature increases starting Thursday. This triggered evacuations and a major emergency response, which continued into Friday as officials warned of escalating risks.
Fire authorities described two primary dangers: a catastrophic rupture that could release the chemical into surrounding soil and waterways, or a possible explosion. The Orange County Fire Authority coordinated with hazardous materials teams and worked on containment strategies to prevent contamination of storm drains and nearby environmental systems. Officials said tens of thousands o

item.title: Competitive Three-Way Race Shapes Los Angeles City Attorney Election Amid Controversy and Legal Challenges
item.description:
The Los Angeles city attorney race has become unexpectedly competitive, marked by controversy, shifting alliances, and high financial stakes. Incumbent Hydee Feldstein Soto is seeking reelection while facing strong challenges from two opponents with contrasting political profiles: Marissa Roy, a progressive deputy attorney general, and John McKinney, a county prosecutor backed by law enforcement groups and significant independent spending.
The race intensified following a major data breach involving leaked LAPD files that included sensitive records such as crime scene images and medical information. The incident led to backlash against the incumbent, including the withdrawal of support from the city’s police union, which redirected its endorsement to McKinney. Meanwhile, Roy has positioned herself as a progressive reformer who wants to transform the city attorney’s office into a public interest-focused institution, emphasizing tenant rights, wage theft enforcement, and resistance to f

item.title: DOJ Defends Removal of Jan. 6 Riot Records From Its Website Amid Political Backlash
item.description:
The article reports on controversy surrounding the U.S. Department of Justice under the Trump administration after officials acknowledged removing online records related to individuals convicted for crimes connected to the January 6 Capitol riot. One of the deleted records concerned Andrew Quentin Taake, a Texas man sentenced in 2024 for assaulting police officers during the Capitol attack while armed with bear spray and a metal whip. The original DOJ release also noted that Taake faced a separate criminal case involving online solicitation of a minor.
Washington Post reporter Maryl Kornfield highlighted the deletion of the DOJ webpage and criticized the administration for quietly removing information about Capitol riot prosecutions. In response, the DOJ’s official Rapid Response account publicly defended the decision, stating that the administration was reversing what it described as the “weaponization” of the Justice Department under President Biden. The statement further said the a

item.title: GOP strategist criticizes Trump administration’s $1.7B settlement as politically damaging ahead of midterms
item.description:
A Republican strategist appearing on CNN criticized a recent Trump administration decision involving a $1.7 billion taxpayer-funded settlement, warning that it could become politically damaging for Republicans heading into the midterm elections. The agreement, described by the administration as an “anti-weaponization fund,” follows President Donald Trump’s decision to drop a $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service tied to the 2019 leak of his tax returns. The settlement is intended to compensate individuals who claim they were unfairly targeted by the Biden administration’s Justice Department. According to reporting, eligibility for payouts includes a broad group of claimants, including individuals involved in the January 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, as well as some Trump donors, a detail confirmed by Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.
During a CNN interview, GOP strategist Jeanette Hoffman said the initiative represents a “political miscalculation,” arguin
