item.title: Research identifies PAHs in common foods and advances methods for detecting cooking-related contaminants
item.description:
A recent study from researchers at Seoul National University of Science and Technology highlights the presence of potentially harmful chemical compounds, known as polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs), in a variety of everyday foods. These substances can form when foods are exposed to high-temperature cooking methods such as grilling, frying, roasting, and smoking, or they may enter food through environmental contamination. Some PAHs have been associated with carcinogenic effects in animal studies, raising concerns about long-term exposure through diet.
The researchers focused on improving how PAHs are detected in food samples. Traditional testing methods are often time-consuming, chemically intensive, and require complex preparation steps. To address these limitations, the team used a streamlined technique called QuEChERS (Quick, Easy, Cheap, Effective, Rugged, and Safe), which simplifies sample preparation while improving efficiency and reducing chemical use. The method was tested

item.title: Barbara Parkins: A 1960s Hollywood Actress and Style Icon
item.description:
Barbara Parkins, born in 1942 in Vancouver, British Columbia, is a Canadian-American actress who rose to prominence during the 1960s and became widely recognized as both a television and film star as well as a fashion icon. She is best known for her role as Betty Anderson in the influential soap opera 'Peyton Place,' a series that helped define modern serialized television drama and brought her international fame. Her screen presence was marked by a refined and elegant style that resonated strongly with audiences during a transformative era in entertainment.
Parkins’ cinematic highlight came with the 1967 cult film 'Valley of the Dolls,' in which she played Anne Welles, a character navigating the glamorous yet challenging world of show business and fashion. The film further cemented her association with the visual and cultural aesthetics of the 1960s. Beyond acting, Parkins became a frequent subject for fashion photography, admired for her distinctive look characterized by dark hair,

item.title: NASA To Open JPL Management Contract To Competition After Decades Under Caltech
item.description:
NASA is preparing to open the management contract for the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) to outside competition for the first time in decades, potentially ending the long-standing relationship between JPL and the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). Caltech has overseen JPL since the laboratory’s origins in the 1930s and has managed it for NASA since the agency was established in 1958. The current contract is expected to expire in 2028.
According to statements from JPL and NASA, Caltech had already been preparing for the possibility of a competitive rebid since 2025. The move is part of a broader NASA restructuring effort intended to increase specialization among NASA centers and improve operational efficiency. JPL plays a major role in robotic space exploration, particularly Mars missions and deep-space science programs.
JPL operates under the federally funded research and development center (FFRDC) model, which allows it to function with greater independence than standard

item.title: Frank Hayden, Researcher Who Helped Build the Foundation of the Special Olympics, Dies at 96
item.description:
Frank Hayden, a Canadian researcher and educator whose work helped inspire the creation of the Special Olympics movement, has died at the age of 96. Hayden devoted much of his career to studying the relationship between sports, physical activity and intellectual disabilities at a time when many children with special needs were excluded from public life, denied educational opportunities, and often institutionalized. His research challenged the widespread assumption that individuals with intellectual disabilities were incapable of athletic participation or physical achievement.
Hayden’s work became especially influential in the 1960s, when he conducted studies showing that children with intellectual disabilities could greatly benefit from structured physical education and organized sports. His findings attracted the attention of Eunice Kennedy Shriver, who was developing programs focused on inclusion and opportunities for people with disabilities. Their collaboration ultimately contribu

item.title: Republicans Defend Trump Judicial Nominees Amid Questions About 2020 Election Response
item.description:
The article reports on a political dispute in the U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee involving Republican lawmakers and President Donald Trump’s judicial nominees. Democrats have been repeatedly asking these nominees a basic question during confirmation hearings: who won the 2020 presidential election. Many nominees have avoided directly stating that Joe Biden won, instead giving indirect or procedural responses referencing election certification processes. This has sparked criticism from Democrats, who argue the nominees are deliberately evading a straightforward factual answer to avoid contradicting Trump, who continues to falsely claim the 2020 election was stolen.
Sen. Chuck Grassley, the Republican chair of the Judiciary Committee, has defended the nominees and criticized Democrats for pressing the issue. In a recent hearing, Grassley invoked Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, pointing out that she declined to comment on election-related political questions during her conf

item.title: Opinion writer argues Trump should face accountability for January 6 and related legal controversies
item.description:
This opinion article by Tara Dublin reflects on the January 6, 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol and criticizes former President Donald Trump and his supporters. Written in a satirical legal-style format addressed to a fictional judge, the author claims emotional trauma from witnessing the Capitol riot unfold live on television and social media. The piece recounts the shock and fear experienced while watching rioters breach security barriers, attack Capitol Police officers, vandalize government property, and threaten political leaders, including chants directed at then-Vice President Mike Pence and House Speaker Nancy Pelosi.
The author argues that many Americans who opposed Trump experienced long-lasting psychological and emotional distress after the events of January 6. She contrasts this with what she describes as complaints and lawsuits from Trump supporters who faced consequences after participating in the riot. The article also criticizes the U.S. legal and political system for al

item.title: Columnist cautions Spencer Pratt about the realities of running for Los Angeles mayor
item.description:
In this opinion column, Los Angeles Times writer Steve Lopez addresses reality TV personality and mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt, urging him to reconsider his ambitions or at least better understand the challenges ahead. Lopez argues that running Los Angeles is far more complex than campaign slogans suggest, pointing to structural limits on mayoral power, fragmented governance, and chronic budget constraints. He highlights persistent city problems such as a backlog of more than 30,000 broken sidewalks awaiting repair and underfunded infrastructure, noting that there is often insufficient money to address even widely recognized needs.
Lopez also emphasizes that major issues like homelessness, drug addiction, and mental illness are not solely under the mayor’s control, as responsibilities are shared with county agencies and shaped by broader economic forces, including housing costs and inequality. He criticizes simplistic campaign proposals, such as dramatically increasing police hirin

item.title: Starship V3 Flight 12 Demonstrates Progress Despite Multiple Raptor Engine Failures
item.description: SpaceX conducted its 12th Integrated Flight Test (IFT-12) of the Starship system on May 22, 2026, marking the debut of the upgraded Starship V3 configuration. The Super Heavy Booster 19 and Starship Ship 39 launched from the new OLP-2 pad at Starbase, Texas, after an initial cancellation the previous day due to a ground system issue. The flight showcased significant technological advances, including the new Raptor 3 engines and structural improvements designed to increase thrust and reduce mass. The booster produced record thrust at liftoff with all 33 engines initially functioning, but the mission soon encountered complications. One engine failed during ascent, and later multiple Raptor engines in the booster did not ignite properly during the return burn sequence. A subsequent engine explosion led to the loss of additional engines, ultimately preventing controlled recovery of the booster, which disintegrated during its high-speed descent and impacted the ocean. Despite these issues,

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Look at Nicolas Cage’s Performance in “Leaving Las Vegas”
item.description:
The article revisits the 1995 romantic drama film “Leaving Las Vegas,” directed by Mike Figgis and based on John O’Brien’s semi-autobiographical novel. It highlights Nicolas Cage’s acclaimed performance as Ben Sanderson, a suicidal alcoholic who moves to Las Vegas with the intent of drinking himself to death after losing his job and family. In Las Vegas, he forms a complicated relationship with Sera, a sex worker portrayed by Elisabeth Shue, who becomes a brief emotional anchor in his downward spiral.
Cage’s portrayal is described as a defining moment in his career, earning him the Academy Award for Best Actor at the 68th Academy Awards. His performance is noted for its intense physical realism and emotional depth, blending tragic vulnerability with moments of dark humor. The article explains that Cage used highly immersive methods to prepare for the role, including studying the behavior of a “drinking coach,” observing real-life mannerisms, and recording himself while intoxicated to

item.title: Tom Brigance and the Evolution of American Sportswear in the 1940s and 1950s
item.description:
Tom Brigance (1913–1990), known professionally as Brigance, was a highly influential American fashion designer who helped redefine women’s sportswear in the mid-20th century. Working as an in-house designer for Lord & Taylor, he became closely associated with the development of the “American Look,” a style movement that emphasized comfort, practicality, and elegance over the rigid structure of traditional European couture.
Brigance gained particular recognition for his innovative swimwear designs. Instead of relying on restrictive corsetry, he used creative draping techniques, wrap silhouettes, and bold prints to enhance and flatter the natural female form. His approach reflected the changing cultural mood of the post-war United States, where fashion began shifting toward more relaxed, functional, and sun-oriented lifestyles.
Throughout the 1940s and 1950s, Brigance collaborated with prominent textile houses and fashion labels, producing ready-to-wear collections that appeared freque

item.title: Prince William Says He Hopes to Attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Rumored Wedding
item.description:
Prince William said during an appearance on Heart Breakfast that he hopes to receive an invitation to the rumored wedding of pop superstar Taylor Swift and NFL player Travis Kelce. The prince spoke openly about his family’s admiration for Swift, revealing that Prince George, Princess Charlotte, and Prince Louis are all fans of the singer. He specifically described Princess Charlotte as the biggest “Swiftie” in the family.
William recalled attending Swift’s Eras Tour concert at Wembley Stadium in 2024 with his children, describing the atmosphere as unforgettable. He said the crowd energy was so intense that the floor seemed to shake during the performance. During the concert, William, George, and Charlotte also met Swift and Kelce backstage and took a selfie together, which received significant attention online.
The article also revisited William’s long-standing friendly relationship with Swift, including their widely publicized 2013 charity performance at Kensington Palace alongside

item.title: Curated roundup of the best Memorial Day 2026 sales across major retailers and categories
item.description:
The article presents a comprehensive roundup of Memorial Day 2026 sales curated by CNN Underscored’s deals team. It highlights that 172 notable discounts are currently available across major retailers and brands for the holiday weekend. The sales span a wide variety of product categories, including tech devices, home appliances, outdoor and patio furniture, clothing, footwear, beauty products, kitchen items, and travel accessories. The editorial team emphasizes that these deals were selected after evaluating thousands of offers to identify the most meaningful price drops and reliable products.
Among the featured highlights are discounts on popular tech items such as laptops, smart TVs, chargers, and portable speakers, with brands like Apple, Sony, Samsung, and Anker included. Home and lifestyle deals also stand out, including vacuums, fans, grills, and patio seating sets, many of which are reaching some of their lowest prices of the year. Seasonal outdoor and summer-focused products s

item.title: MS NOW Host Reacts to Controversial Andy Barr Campaign Ad Focused on DEI and Identity Messaging
item.description:
MS NOW host Jonathan Capehart expressed shock and disbelief after viewing a controversial campaign advertisement from Kentucky Republican Congressman Andy Barr, who is running for the U.S. Senate seat being vacated by Mitch McConnell. The incident occurred during an interview with Democratic candidate Charles Booker, where Capehart played a clip of Barr’s ad for discussion.
In the advertisement, Barr criticizes diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives, describing DEI as “dumb evil indoctrination” and claiming that “woke liberals” and “corporate losers” promote it. The ad also references former President Donald Trump, suggesting that America is rejecting what Barr calls “that trash” and positioning himself as a leader in opposing it.
The most controversial moment comes when Barr states, “It’s not a sin to be white,” while also asserting that being male is “not against the law” and that being Christian should not be disqualifying. The phrasing sparked strong reactions, with C

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson to hold private Bahamas wedding after White House venue rejected
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson are set to celebrate their marriage with an intimate ceremony on a private island in the Bahamas. According to sources cited in the report, Anderson initially expressed interest in holding the wedding at the White House. However, both Trump Jr. and President Donald Trump reportedly considered the idea inappropriate given current geopolitical tensions involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Anderson ultimately agreed to the change in plans. The couple has already legally married, with a marriage certificate filed in Palm Beach County confirming they wed earlier in the week. Their celebration in the Bahamas will be small and private, with fewer than 50 guests expected, including close family members such as Eric Trump and Ivanka Trump, along with Trump Jr.’s five children. President Donald Trump will not attend the ceremony, citing the need to remain in Washington, D.C. during a critical period, though he extended public congratulations. Security conce

item.title: Body Camera Footage Shows Police Informing Dominic Russo’s Mother About Fatal 2022 Crash
item.description:
TMZ published newly resurfaced police body camera footage showing the emotional moment officers informed Christine Russo that her son, Dominic Russo, had died following the 2022 high-speed crash involving Mackenzie Shirilla in Strongsville, Ohio. The video captures officers arriving at the Russo family home late at night and carefully explaining that Dominic did not survive the collision. According to the report, Christine Russo became overwhelmed with grief, collapsing and attempting to run from the officers as she struggled to process the news.
Dominic Russo and his friend Davion Flanagan were passengers in a vehicle driven by Mackenzie Shirilla when the car crashed into a commercial building at nearly 100 miles per hour. Both young men were pronounced dead at the scene. Prosecutors later argued that Shirilla intentionally drove into the building following a dispute, while defense attorneys maintained that the crash was a tragic accident rather than an intentional act.
In 2023, Shi

item.title: Brooklyn Sports Bar Becomes Gathering Place for Knicks and Arsenal Fans During Rare Winning Moments
item.description:
The article highlights the growing excitement surrounding a Brooklyn sports bar called FancyFree, where devoted fans of the New York Knicks and Arsenal gather to watch games and celebrate long-awaited success. Located near Barclays Center in Brooklyn, the bar has become a lively meeting point for sports enthusiasts, local residents, celebrities, and political figures, including filmmaker Spike Lee and politician Zohran Mamdani. The story focuses on the emotional atmosphere among fans who have endured years of disappointment supporting teams that rarely delivered championship-level performances.
Marc Jean, a lifelong Knicks supporter, reflects on how unusual it is to see the bar overflowing with fans wearing team jerseys and filling the surrounding streets before games. For decades, Knicks fans have dealt with frustration and declining expectations, making the current playoff excitement feel especially meaningful. Arsenal supporters share a similar sense of cautious optimism after year

item.title: Poll Shows Strong Opposition to Kristi Noem’s Potential Political Comeback in South Dakota
item.description:
A new poll indicates that former Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem faces significant resistance from voters in her home state of South Dakota if she were to seek public office again. According to a KELOLAND Media Group and Emerson College survey, 67% of respondents said they would oppose Noem running for office in the state, while only 22% expressed support and 11% remained undecided. The poll included 875 South Dakota voters, with a sample that reflected Republicans, independents, and Democrats, and showed opposition to Noem across party lines.
The results mark a sharp contrast with Noem’s past electoral success. She previously won the South Dakota governorship in 2022 with 62% of the vote and had also served as a member of Congress. At one point, she was viewed as a rising figure within national Republican politics and reportedly expressed interest in higher office, including speculation about a possible role on Donald Trump’s presidential ticket.
However, the a

item.title: Republican Lawmakers Express Growing Distance from Trump Amid Policy and Election Concerns
item.description:
A Raw Story report highlights increasing tension within the Republican Party as some lawmakers reportedly distance themselves from President Donald Trump amid declining approval ratings and controversial policy decisions. According to reporting cited from MS NOW, a recent week of GOP resistance included efforts to strip around $1 billion in security-related funding from a reconciliation package, including $220 million earmarked for renovations linked to Trump’s East Wing ballroom project. Lawmakers also moved toward supporting a resolution requiring congressional authorization for continued military actions involving Iran, while delaying a $72 billion funding vote connected to immigration and deportation policies.
The political friction is framed alongside weakening public support for Trump, with a New York Times/Siena poll placing his approval rating at 37%, described as the lowest across both of his presidential terms. The article also references rising gasoline prices averaging abo

item.title: Trump Shares Provocative Post About Iran as Speculation Grows Over Possible U.S. Military Action
item.description:
A Raw Story article reported that President Donald Trump posted an image on Truth Social suggesting the idea of the United States taking control of Iran. The image reportedly showed a map of Iran covered with the American flag and included the caption “United States of the Middle East?” The post appeared amid growing speculation that the Trump administration could be preparing military action against Iran in the coming days.
According to the article, concerns about a possible attack intensified after Trump abruptly canceled planned weekend events in New Jersey. The report also noted that Iran had closed much of its airspace through Monday, a move interpreted by some observers as preparation for potential military escalation. The article described the current relationship between the United States and Iran as highly unstable despite a fragile ceasefire that has reportedly been in place since April.
The piece further stated that diplomatic negotiations between Washington and Tehran rem

item.title: David Bowie’s Iconic Role as Jareth in Jim Henson’s Labyrinth (1986)
item.description:
The article revisits David Bowie’s unforgettable portrayal of Jareth the Goblin King in Jim Henson’s 1986 fantasy film Labyrinth, a cult classic that has remained influential for decades. In the film, Jareth is the enigmatic ruler of a magical labyrinth who kidnaps baby Toby, forcing teenage Sarah, played by Jennifer Connelly, to navigate his dangerous and surreal world to rescue him. Rather than a traditional villain, Jareth is portrayed as a complex figure representing temptation, adolescence, and the allure of adulthood, blending charm, danger, and theatrical charisma.
The character was conceptually designed to reflect a teenage girl’s conflicted emotions toward adulthood, inspired by romantic literary figures such as Heathcliff and Rochester, as well as medieval and rock-inspired aesthetics. Bowie’s performance enhanced this duality, combining a commanding stage presence with an almost otherworldly elegance. His look in the film became iconic: a towering blonde wig, dramatic makeu

item.title: A humorous look at different styles of people packing for vacations
item.description:
This opinion piece by illustrator Edith Pritchett offers a lighthearted and humorous take on the different kinds of people when it comes to packing for vacations. Instead of focusing on travel destinations or logistics, the article explores personality-driven packing behaviors that many readers can recognize in themselves or others. It categorizes travelers into archetypes based on how they approach preparing their luggage, from those who meticulously plan every outfit in advance to those who throw things into a suitcase at the last minute.
The piece emphasizes the relatable nature of travel preparation stress, highlighting how packing often reflects broader personality traits such as organization, spontaneity, anxiety, or over-preparedness. Some travelers are portrayed as minimalists who bring only the essentials, while others are described as over-packers who prepare for every possible scenario, leading to overloaded suitcases. There are also references to procrastinators who pack a

item.title: Shirley Bassey’s Early Career and Rise to Fame in the 1950s
item.description:
This article explores the early life and career of Welsh singer Shirley Bassey during the 1950s, a formative decade that shaped her path to international stardom. Born in Cardiff in 1937, Bassey left school at the age of 14 to work in a packing factory while singing in local pubs and clubs during her free time. Her talent quickly became evident, and she secured her first professional contract in 1953 at just 16 years old, touring with a variety show called ‘Memories of Jolson.’ Although her early career was briefly interrupted by the birth of her first daughter, she soon returned to performing, working in Cardiff before re-entering the professional stage circuit.
In 1955, she was discovered by impresario Jack Hylton, who cast her in the West End revue ‘Such Is Life.’ This exposure led to a recording contract with producer Johnny Franz. Her debut single, ‘Burn My Candle’ (1956), gained notoriety after being banned by the BBC for its suggestive lyrics, ironically increasing her populari

item.title: A Rare Brain Condition Led to Emergency Surgery After Severe Headaches and Misdiagnosed Symptoms
item.description:
The article is a first-person account by a 64-year-old woman who experienced a sudden and severe medical crisis following months of unexplained headaches. Initially, she developed persistent head pain that felt different from typical migraines, prompting multiple doctor visits and misdiagnoses, including sinus issues, allergies, and other common conditions. Eventually, a CT scan revealed subdural hematomas, or bleeding near the brain. Further evaluation by specialists identified the underlying cause as a spontaneous cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) leak, a rare condition in which spinal fluid pressure drops, causing the brain to sag and leading to vascular strain and bleeding.
As her condition worsened, she underwent a blood patch procedure in an attempt to seal the leak, but complications followed. She later developed severe flank pain and was diagnosed with a pulmonary embolism, requiring hospitalization and blood thinners. Her neurological symptoms continued to escalate, culminating in em

item.title: FBI Seeks Expanded Real-Time Access to Nationwide License Plate Reader Data Amid Broader US Cybersecurity and Privacy Concerns
item.description:
WIRED’s weekly security roundup focused heavily on surveillance, cybersecurity vulnerabilities, online abuse, and government oversight in the United States. The main story revealed that the FBI is preparing to purchase nationwide access to automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems capable of providing near real-time vehicle tracking data. Procurement documents reportedly show the agency seeking broad access to roadside camera networks that collect vehicle license plates, timestamps, and location information across highways and urban areas. The development comes at the same time bipartisan lawmakers are proposing restrictions on police use of ALPR technology due to growing privacy concerns.
The article also covered a serious Chromium browser vulnerability unintentionally exposed by Google after proof-of-concept exploit code was published before a patch was available. The flaw could allow malicious websites to maintain persistent service workers capable of monitoring browsing activi

item.title: Piers Morgan Urges Trump to Avoid Renewed Military Action Against Iran Amid Rising Speculation
item.description:
British media personality Piers Morgan publicly urged President Donald Trump to avoid escalating tensions with Iran after reports and speculation suggested the United States and Israel could be preparing for a new military operation. In a social media post published Saturday morning, Morgan warned that another attack would create further instability and violence, arguing that the conflict had already caused significant destruction.
The concerns intensified after President Trump reportedly canceled planned travel to New Jersey and returned to Washington on Friday, a move interpreted by observers as a possible sign of impending military decisions. At the same time, Iran temporarily closed airspace in the western part of the country and suspended flights through Monday, reportedly out of concern over a potential U.S. strike.
The article also highlighted reactions from conservative activist Laura Loomer, described as a close ally of Trump and an influential right-wing figure. Unlike Morg

item.title: 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen: Design and Role of Germany’s WWII Utility Vehicle
item.description:
The 1944 Volkswagen Type 82 Kübelwagen was a key light utility vehicle used by German forces during World War II. Designed by Ferdinand Porsche, it was developed from the early Volkswagen Beetle platform but adapted specifically for military use. Unlike many other wartime vehicles, the Kübelwagen did not rely on four-wheel drive. Instead, its performance off-road came from a combination of very low weight, a rear-mounted air-cooled engine, and a self-locking differential system that improved traction on uneven terrain.
By 1944, the design had been refined through years of battlefield use. The vehicle featured simplified construction to improve reliability and ease of mass production during wartime shortages. Its corrugated metal body panels added structural strength without significantly increasing weight, making it more durable in harsh conditions. The air-cooled engine was particularly important, as it allowed the vehicle to operate in extreme climates ranging from the deserts of No

item.title: Hospital payment policies had greater impact on U.S. female sterilization rates than 1970s consent reforms
item.description:
A new study by a health economist and a political scientist examines long-term shifts in female sterilization trends in the United States and the policy forces that shaped them. The researchers compared two major historical inflection points: the 1970s reforms following the Relf v. Weinberger case, which exposed coercive and non-consensual sterilizations tied to welfare and federal programs, and the 1990s changes in hospital payment systems that reduced postpartum hospital stays.
The Relf case led to stronger consent requirements, including waiting periods and minimum age rules for federally funded sterilizations. While these reforms slowed the growth of sterilization rates, they did not reverse the overall trend. Sterilization continued to rise through the late 20th century, increasing from about 5% of women in 1970 to roughly 13% in 1975, and reaching nearly one in four married women of reproductive age by 1990. The study also found limited changes in populations historically target

item.title: Photos of Everyday American Family Life in the 1950s from the David Wipf Collection
item.description: The 1950s marked a significant period in American history characterized by economic prosperity, suburban expansion, and a strong emphasis on family values following the end of World War II. This era embodied the American Dream for many, with families settling into newly built suburban homes featuring modern appliances, green lawns, and community-oriented lifestyles. The nuclear family model became prominent, often depicted with a station wagon in the driveway and children playing safely in neighborhoods. Social life revolved around backyard barbecues, holiday celebrations, birthday parties, and quiet moments at home. These images from the David Wipf collection provide an authentic window into daily routines and family dynamics of the time. They showcase parents engaging with children, families gathering for meals, and the simplicity of mid-century domestic life. The photographs highlight the optimism and stability that defined postwar America, where consumerism boomed and homeownership

item.title: A Look at Rosemary Clooney’s Career and Fame Through 1950s Photographs
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday revisits the life and career of American singer and actress Rosemary Clooney through a collection of photographs from the 1950s. Clooney rose to prominence during the early part of the decade after transitioning from performing with Tony Pastor’s big band to launching a solo career at Columbia Records. Under producer Mitch Miller, she became known for novelty-style pop hits such as “Come On-a My House,” “Botch-a-Me,” and “Mambo Italiano,” songs that achieved major commercial success and established her as one of the leading female vocalists of the era.
Although these upbeat novelty recordings brought her widespread fame, Clooney preferred traditional pop standards and jazz music. Her artistic range became more visible later in the decade through collaborations with prominent musicians, including Duke Ellington. Their 1956 album “Blue Rose” is highlighted as one of the most critically respected works of her career.
The article also discusses Clooney’s

item.title: Paul McCartney Guests on Final ‘Late Show’ Episode, Performing ‘Hello Goodbye’ With Stephen Colbert at Ed Sullivan Theater Farewell
item.description:
The final episode of ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ featured a surprise and symbolic appearance by Paul McCartney, who helped close out the long-running program with a memorable musical and emotional sendoff. McCartney joined Colbert on stage at the historic Ed Sullivan Theater, a venue deeply tied to Beatles history, for a performance of the classic song ‘Hello Goodbye.’ The finale brought together a wide ensemble, including Elvis Costello, former bandleader Jon Batiste, and current bandleader Louis Cato, with the house band eventually transforming the performance into a New Orleans-style celebration. Staff members also joined in, dancing across the stage as part of the show’s festive conclusion.
A key moment in the broadcast showed Colbert escorting McCartney backstage to the theater’s electrical controls, where the musician ceremonially flipped the switch that turned off the lights. The act was staged as both a literal ending and a symbolic closure, as the theater briefly tra

item.title: Elvis Presley Interacts With Fans During His 1970 Las Vegas Residency at the International Hotel
item.description:
This article revisits a moment from Elvis Presley’s iconic Las Vegas residency during the summer of 1970, focusing on August 26, when he performed two shows at the International Hotel. At this stage in his career, Elvis was deeply engaged in his celebrated “Summer Festival” engagement, a period widely regarded as one of the highlights of his Las Vegas years. The residency was also being documented for the MGM film “Elvis: That’s the Way It Is,” which captured his return to live performance and helped redefine his public image as a mature, contemporary entertainer.
By late August 1970, the film crew had largely completed principal shooting, but Elvis remained at a creative and energetic peak. The article highlights how he interacted warmly with fans during this period, including taking photos and engaging playfully with the audience. His performances were backed by the TCB Band and showcased a dynamic mix of his early rock-and-roll classics and modern covers, reflecting his evolving mu

item.title: React Challenge Demonstrates Optimistic UI Updates with useOptimistic in React 19
item.description:
A new frontend coding challenge published by ReactChallenges introduces developers to React 19’s useOptimistic hook through a practical example involving instant UI updates. The challenge focuses on improving user experience by eliminating visible delays between user actions and server responses. Participants are tasked with building a small interface containing two cards displayed side by side: one representing a successful server update and the other simulating a failed request.
Both cards begin with an unliked heart icon. When users click the heart in the successful example, the UI should immediately display the liked state by turning the heart red, even before the server confirms the change. After the simulated server request succeeds, the heart remains red. In the second example, the heart also turns red instantly, but because the server request intentionally fails, React automatically restores the original neutral state.
The article highlights how useOptimistic simplifies optim

item.title: How Windows 95 Was Distributed on 28 Floppy Disks Before CD-ROMs Became Standard
item.description:
A post published by Vintage Everyday revisits one of the more memorable aspects of mid-1990s personal computing: installing Microsoft Windows 95 using a large set of floppy disks. The article explains that some editions of Windows 95 were distributed on as many as 28 separate 3.5-inch floppy disks, with each disk capable of storing only 1.44 MB of data. At the time, this represented a practical solution for users whose computers did not yet include CD-ROM drives, even though CD-based software distribution was already becoming common.
The article describes how installing the operating system was a slow and repetitive process. Users had to insert and remove disks one after another while following prompts from the installer. Because the installation depended on every disk functioning correctly, a single damaged or unreadable floppy could interrupt the process and force users to start over or replace the faulty disk. The experience became a defining memory for many early PC users.
The pi

item.title: Charlottesville Plans New Public Artwork Using Melted Robert E. Lee Statue Bronze
item.description:
The city of Charlottesville is moving forward with a new phase in the long aftermath of the 2017 white supremacist rally that erupted around a statue of Confederate Gen. Robert E. Lee. After years of public debate and legal disputes, the controversial monument was removed and later melted down to prevent it from ever being reinstalled. The remaining bronze material has now become the focus of a new public art initiative intended to address the city’s painful history while promoting reconciliation and reflection.
Artists, community leaders, and organizers are discussing how the melted bronze ingots can be transformed into a meaningful work of art that represents modern community values rather than Confederate symbolism. The project aims to acknowledge the violence and racial tensions connected to the 2017 Unite the Right rally, during which clashes between white supremacists and counterprotesters led to injuries and the death of Heather Heyer.
The article explains that organizers face

item.title: Marion Michael, German Film Star Who Rose to Fame with 'Liane, Jungle Goddess' in the 1950s
item.description:
Marion Michael (1940–2007) was a German actress who became an unexpected star in the mid-1950s after achieving major fame at just 15 years old. Her breakthrough role came in the 1956 adventure film 'Liane, Jungle Goddess' (originally 'Liane, das Mädchen aus dem Urwald'), which quickly turned her into a national sensation. Known for her blonde hair, athletic appearance, and natural screen presence, she embodied a new type of youthful femininity that resonated strongly with postwar audiences in West Germany. Her image was often described as fresh, untamed, and symbolic of changing cultural attitudes during the Economic Miracle era.
During the peak of her popularity, Marion Michael was frequently compared to French actress Brigitte Bardot, as both represented a shift toward more liberated and charismatic female screen icons in European cinema. While her early career was closely associated with her “jungle girl” persona, she did not remain confined to that image. Over time, she expanded h

item.title: Deep-Sea Golden Orb Identified as Remains of Rare Anemone
item.description: A mysterious golden orb discovered on the seafloor of the Gulf of Alaska during a NOAA Ocean Exploration expedition has finally been identified after years of scientific analysis. Initially retrieved because its smooth, shiny surface did not resemble any known marine organism, the object sparked widespread speculation, including theories that it might be an egg, a biofilm, or even an extraterrestrial artifact. Early examinations ruled out non-biological origins after researchers detected spirocytes—specialized cells found only in cnidarians, the animal group that includes jellyfish, corals, and anemones. This finding narrowed the possibilities significantly. Subsequent genetic sequencing provided a breakthrough, revealing a 99.9 percent match with Relicanthus daphneae, a rare and poorly understood deep-sea anemone that lives at depths between 1,600 and 4,000 meters. Despite the strong genetic link, scientists initially struggled to explain how the orb related to the known structure of

item.title: Rod Stewart’s Career and Style Evolution in the 1980s
item.description: The article reviews Rod Stewart’s transformation during the 1980s, a decade in which the British singer successfully adapted to changing musical trends while facing both commercial success and critical skepticism. Known for his distinctive raspy voice and long-standing status as a global music star, Stewart entered the decade distancing himself from the disco controversy of the late 1970s and embracing the polished sounds of New Wave and synth-pop. His 1981 album “Tonight I’m Yours” marked a strong commercial period, featuring the hit single “Young Turks,” which became one of his most recognizable songs of the era by blending energetic synthesizer production with his signature vocal style. Throughout the early and mid-1980s, Stewart continued releasing music such as “Body Wishes” (1983) and “Camouflage” (1984), which produced popular tracks like “Baby Jane” and “Some Guys Have All the Luck.” However, critics often dismissed these works as overly commercial and lacking depth, reflecting

item.title: Rare Childhood Photos Trace Joan Baez’s Early Life and Influences Before Her Music Career
item.description:
The article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 18 rare childhood photographs of American folk singer and activist Joan Baez, documenting her early years during the 1940s and 1950s. Alongside the images, the piece outlines key aspects of Baez’s upbringing and the experiences that shaped her later career in music and social activism.
Born on January 9, 1941, in Staten Island, New York, Joan Baez grew up in a multicultural family with Mexican, Scottish, and English roots. Her father, physicist Albert Baez, co-invented the X-ray microscope, while her mother, Joan Bridge Baez, came from a religious English family. Her parents later adopted Quaker beliefs, which strongly influenced Baez’s lifelong commitment to pacifism, civil rights, and social justice.
Because of her father’s academic and UNESCO-related work, the family moved frequently across the United States and internationally, including periods living in England, France, Switzerland, and Iraq. The article notes that Baez

item.title: Photographic Retrospective of Jimmy Page During His Session Work and Yardbirds Era in the 1960s
item.description:
This article presents a visual retrospective focused on Jimmy Page during the mid and late 1960s, a formative period before and during the early rise of Led Zeppelin. Born in 1944, Jimmy Page became one of the most influential guitarists in rock history, but before achieving global fame, he was already deeply embedded in the British music industry as a highly sought-after session musician. Because of his ability to read music fluently and perform across multiple genres—including rockabilly, jazz, and early rock—Page reportedly contributed to a large portion of recorded rock music in the UK between 1963 and 1966. During this time, he worked on sessions for major artists and bands such as The Who, The Kinks, Donovan, and Joe Cocker, earning a reputation as a versatile and reliable studio guitarist.
The article highlights how Page was known in studio circles as “Little Jim,” distinguishing him from another prominent session guitarist of the era, “Big Jim” Sullivan. It also describes his

item.title: RSPCA advises dog owners on safe use of damp towels to prevent heatstroke in pets
item.description:
The RSPCA has issued guidance to dog owners regarding the use of damp towels as a method to keep pets cool during hot weather. While many pet owners rely on this common technique to help prevent heatstroke, the charity warns that incorrect use can actually worsen a dog’s condition. According to the RSPCA, placing wet or damp towels directly over a dog’s body may trap heat instead of reducing it, potentially increasing the risk of overheating. Instead, the organization recommends placing damp towels beneath the dog, ensuring they are re-wetted frequently to maintain effectiveness.
Heatstroke in dogs is a serious and potentially fatal condition that occurs when an animal is unable to regulate its body temperature through panting. The RSPCA emphasizes the importance of acting quickly if a dog shows signs of overheating. Symptoms can include confusion, excessive drooling, dehydration, rapid heartbeat, noisy or labored breathing, weakness, muscle spasms, and in severe cases, collapse. Cert

item.title: Analysis of Speaker Mike Johnson’s Political Position Amid Growing GOP and Trump Tensions
item.description:
This opinion article by John Casey published on Raw Story examines the political standing of U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson in relation to President Donald Trump and internal Republican Party tensions. The author argues that Johnson has consistently aligned himself with Trump’s agenda, prioritizing loyalty over institutional independence in key legislative battles. Among the issues discussed are contentious House votes involving war powers legislation, a proposed White House construction project, and a controversial Department of Justice compensation fund proposal that critics claim could benefit politically connected individuals and January 6 defendants.
The piece suggests that Johnson’s leadership style has increasingly come under pressure from within his own party as some Republican members grow concerned about electoral risks ahead of the 2026 midterms. It describes internal GOP divisions, with some lawmakers allegedly worried about declining polling numbers and voter backlash ti

item.title: Erwin Blumenfeld and the Surrealist Revolution in Mid-Century Fashion Photography
item.description:
Erwin Blumenfeld (1897–1969) was a German-American photographer whose innovative approach to fashion imagery helped redefine visual aesthetics in the mid-20th century. Deeply influenced by Dadaism and Surrealism, he treated photography as an experimental art form rather than a simple means of documentation. During his work with major publications such as Vogue and Harper’s Bazaar, Blumenfeld introduced radical techniques including solarization, multiple exposures, dramatic color filtration, and extreme cropping. These methods gave his images a dreamlike, often unsettling quality that distinguished them from conventional fashion photography of the time.
His work frequently explored themes of identity, femininity, and psychological complexity, shaped in part by his experiences surviving both World Wars. Blumenfeld became especially known for his ability to merge commercial fashion demands with avant-garde artistic expression, elevating editorial photography to the level of fine art. Amo

item.title: Mental health experts warn looksmaxxing trends may indicate body dysmorphia and eating disorder risks in young men
item.description:
The article examines the growing social media trend known as “looksmaxxing,” which has gained significant popularity among teenage boys and young men on platforms like TikTok. The practice involves extreme efforts to improve physical appearance, ranging from strict dieting and intense exercise routines to more dangerous behaviors such as appetite suppression through drugs or even self-harming attempts to alter facial structure. While often framed online as self-improvement, mental health professionals argue that these behaviors closely resemble symptoms of eating disorders and body dysmorphic disorder.
The author highlights how looksmaxxing mirrors earlier online communities from the 2000s, such as pro-anorexia and pro-bulimia forums, which were eventually recognized as harmful and heavily regulated by social media platforms. However, looksmaxxing has not yet received similar scrutiny, partly because it is often associated with masculinity and self-optimization rather than explicit me

item.title: House Democrats condemn Texas congressional candidate over antisemitic remarks and calls for ICE facility conversion
item.description:
House Democrats publicly distanced themselves from Texas congressional candidate Maureen Galindo after she made controversial social media comments that sparked bipartisan backlash. Galindo reportedly suggested converting ICE detention centers into facilities that would imprison “American Zionists” and former ICE officers, remarks widely condemned as antisemitic by lawmakers in both parties. She also referenced extreme and inflammatory ideas in the same post, including claims about targeting individuals she associated with Zionism.
Prominent Democratic members of Congress criticized Galindo’s statements in strong terms. Rep. Ritchie Torres called her a “moral disgrace” and urged her to withdraw from the race, while Rep. John Olszewski said he could not support her candidacy under any circumstances. Rep. Tom Suozzi described her rhetoric as “demented,” reflecting the intensity of the backlash. Some lawmakers said they were unaware of the controversy or declined to comment in detail.
H

item.title: Claudia Cardinale Portraits and Behind-the-Scenes Stills from the 1962 Film ‘Cartouche’
item.description:
The article highlights a collection of vintage portraits of actress Claudia Cardinale taken during the production of the 1962 French adventure film ‘Cartouche,’ directed by Philippe de Broca. The film is remembered as a colorful and energetic example of 1960s French cinema, blending romance, action, and humor within a historical setting. It follows the semi-legendary story of Louis-Dominique Bourguignon, better known as Cartouche, a charismatic 18th-century highwayman who steals from the wealthy while living by his own moral code. Jean-Paul Belmondo stars in the title role, delivering a lively performance filled with physical agility, charm, and playful rebellion.
Claudia Cardinale plays Vénus, Cartouche’s fierce and devoted partner. Her presence in the film is often described as radiant and powerful, contributing both emotional depth and romantic intensity to the story. The portraits featured in the article capture Cardinale during filming in 1962, showcasing her elegance and screen

item.title: Konstantin Ukhtomsky and the Architectural Watercolors of Imperial Russia
item.description:
Konstantin Ukhtomsky (1818–1881) was a distinguished Russian painter and watercolorist renowned for his refined architectural views and interior scenes of imperial palaces. Serving as an official artist for the Russian Imperial Court, he became especially known for his detailed and luminous depictions of the Winter Palace in St. Petersburg and other grand aristocratic residences. His work captures not only architectural accuracy but also the atmosphere and opulence of 19th-century imperial Russia.
Ukhtomsky specialized in portraying elaborate interiors such as throne rooms, galleries, private apartments, staircases, and ceremonial halls. His paintings demonstrate exceptional technical precision and a masterful command of light, allowing him to transform ornate architectural spaces into visually poetic compositions. Through his watercolor technique, he highlighted textures, reflections, and spatial depth, giving viewers a vivid sense of the grandeur experienced by the Russian aristocra

item.title: EU accelerates push for digital sovereignty amid growing tensions with US tech companies
item.description: European governments are increasingly moving to reduce their dependence on US technology companies, driven by geopolitical tensions, concerns about data sovereignty, and fears over political and legal unpredictability linked to the United States. According to the article, France has emerged as the leading force in this shift, actively replacing tools like Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Outlook, and Gmail with domestic or European alternatives. Through its digital transformation agency DINUM, France is developing an integrated open-source productivity suite called “LaSuite,” which includes tools such as Visio for video conferencing, Tchap for messaging, Messagerie for email, Fichiers for storage, and Docs and Grist for document and spreadsheet work. These tools are increasingly used across government agencies, with tens of thousands of civil servants already adopting them. The French government requires that data be processed and stored within the country under approval from national cybersecur

item.title: Long-Married Couple Navigates Emotional Intimacy Despite Lack of Sexual Activity
item.description: A 70-year-old man shared his experience of being married to his 64-year-old wife for over four decades. They consider themselves soulmates and the husband is certain he will always love her. Despite this deep emotional connection, the couple has not engaged in sexual activity for the past five years. The letter highlights that long-term relationships can face challenges in maintaining sexual intimacy, even when love and emotional closeness remain strong. The husband emphasizes the importance of companionship, shared memories, and ongoing emotional support as pillars of their enduring relationship. The article invites reflection on how couples can maintain fulfillment, trust, and connection without sexual activity, showing that love can evolve into different forms as a marriage matures. It also touches on common experiences among older couples, acknowledging that intimacy can shift and that mutual respect and understanding can sustain a strong bond over decades.

item.title: How Night Train Guards Helped Passengers Feel Safe on the London Underground in the 1960s
item.description:
The article reflects on the role of train guards who worked on London Underground night services during the 1960s, especially on the Central Line. These guards, usually stationed in the final carriage of the train, became familiar figures for late-night passengers traveling through the city. Their job was not only operational but also psychological, as their visible presence helped create a sense of safety and reassurance for commuters returning home late at night. Workers finishing long shifts, people leaving social events, and individuals traveling alone often felt more secure knowing a uniformed guard was nearby.
According to the article, these guards eventually became part of the identity and culture of London’s subway system. Their uniforms, calm demeanor, and regular presence were seen as symbols of order and human connection within an otherwise mechanical and impersonal transportation network. The piece emphasizes that the guards represented a human face of public transit durin

item.title: Berlin AI startup Peec surpasses $10 million in annualized revenue as demand for AI search optimization grows
item.description:
Berlin-based startup Peec AI has reportedly surpassed $10 million in annualized recurring revenue only months after reporting more than $4 million in revenue growth following its launch. According to TechCrunch, the company achieved this milestone roughly six months after raising a $21 million Series A funding round at a valuation exceeding $100 million. Peec specializes in helping brands monitor and improve their visibility in AI-powered search tools such as ChatGPT through generative engine optimization (GEO), a growing field that adapts traditional SEO concepts to AI-generated search results.
The article highlights how Peec reflects broader changes in Europe’s startup ecosystem, where investors and founders are increasingly prioritizing measurable revenue growth over inflated valuations. Antler partner Christoph Klink described this shift as a reaction to the overvaluation trends seen during the 2021 startup boom, emphasizing that revenue metrics are now tracked much more closely a

item.title: The Story Behind Harris Lyons’ Dual Mugshots and His 1893 Criminal Arrest
item.description:
The article explores the unusual case of Harris Lyons, a criminal arrested in 1893 whose police records included two nearly identical mugshots taken in Newark, New Jersey. The photographs, preserved as carte de visite (CDV) cards, show Lyons in the same pose but with a notable difference: one image depicts him with facial stubble while the other shows him clean-shaven. The article explains that CDVs were commonly used during the late 19th century both for portraits and for police identification records.
According to reports from The Evening World in New York, Lyons and another man named James Goughty were arrested on December 8, 1893, for involvement in a 'sneak thief' robbery committed in Newark. The victim, storekeeper Emma Zillaux, reportedly identified the suspects after seeing their photographs in New York City’s Rogues’ Gallery, an early police collection of criminal images.
The timeline of the arrest helps explain why two mugshots were created. Lyons was not photographed in Ne

item.title: Spanish Artist Josep Renau and His Legacy of Political Photomontage and Anti-Fascist Art
item.description:
The article explores the life and artistic legacy of Josep Renau, a Spanish artist, graphic designer, and political activist whose work became deeply connected to social criticism and anti-fascist resistance during the 20th century. Born in 1907, Renau emerged as one of the most influential pioneers of political photomontage, heavily inspired by the Berlin Dada movement and its use of collage techniques to challenge political and economic systems. His artwork combined vivid colors, sharp visual contrasts, and bold compositions to criticize fascism, capitalism, war, and consumer culture.
During the Spanish Civil War, Renau served as Director General of Fine Arts for the Spanish Republic. In that role, he played an important cultural and political part in defending Spain’s artistic heritage. The article highlights his involvement in commissioning Pablo Picasso’s famous mural Guernica for the 1937 Paris World’s Fair and organizing the evacuation of masterpieces from the Prado Museum to p

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Linked to Prior Warning Signs and Seized Firearms
item.description: One of the teenagers involved in this week’s deadly shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego had previously exhibited alarming behavior, including idolizing Nazis and mass shooters, which prompted authorities to confiscate his father’s firearms in early 2025. Court records reveal that Caleb Vazquez, 18, had undergone an involuntary psychiatric hospitalization and faced mental health challenges, including being on the autism spectrum. Police seized 26 guns from his family after a welfare check highlighted his dangerous behavior. Vazquez and his accomplice, 17-year-old Cain Clark, were radicalized online and expressed white supremacist and violent ideologies in writings targeting multiple groups, including Jews, Muslims, and Black people. Both teens had struggled with social rejection and mental health issues. Vazquez’s family stated they attempted to help him through therapy, school monitoring, and gun restrictions, but exposure to extremist content online contributed to his radicali

item.title: A Look at 1990s Couples’ Fashion Trends: From Grunge to Minimalist Style
item.description:
The article explores the distinctive fashion trends that defined couples’ style in the 1990s, highlighting how the decade balanced two contrasting aesthetics: rebellious streetwear and refined minimalism. It describes how many couples embraced coordinated looks, often referred to as a “matching aesthetic,” which included denim-on-denim outfits, oversized flannel shirts, and grunge-inspired clothing influenced by alternative music culture. These styles reflected a relaxed, anti-establishment attitude and a preference for comfort and individuality within shared looks.
For everyday fashion, the piece notes that high-waisted “mom jeans,” chunky white sneakers, and windbreakers with bold neon colors were widely popular among couples. These items represented the casual and sporty side of the decade, emphasizing practicality while still maintaining a sense of shared identity between partners.
In contrast, the late 1990s introduced a shift toward minimalism and more polished fashion choices.

item.title: Claudia Mori’s Career as Actress, Singer, and Entertainment Producer in Italy
item.description:
Claudia Mori is a well-known Italian actress, singer, and entertainment producer who became a prominent figure in Italy’s cultural scene beginning in the 1960s. She first rose to fame as a film actress during a vibrant period for Italian cinema, quickly gaining recognition for both her screen presence and her distinctive charisma. Beyond her acting career, Mori also achieved significant success in music, establishing herself as a popular singer in Italy.
One of her most notable achievements in music was the 1970 hit song “Chi non lavora non fa l’amore,” which won the prestigious Sanremo Music Festival. This success helped solidify her status as a major figure in Italian popular culture during that era. Her artistic career was also closely tied to her personal life, particularly her marriage to legendary Italian singer and entertainer Adriano Celentano. Together, they became one of Italy’s most influential celebrity couples, often collaborating both personally and professionally.
As h

item.title: How American Home Design Evolved in the Early 20th Century
item.description:
The article explores the transformation of American residential architecture during the early 1900s, a period marked by significant shifts in both design philosophy and everyday living standards. At the turn of the century, American homes began moving away from the highly ornate and compartmentalized Victorian style that had dominated the 19th century. In its place emerged more functional and accessible designs that emphasized comfort, simplicity, and a closer relationship with nature.
One of the most influential architectural styles of this era was the Craftsman bungalow. This design rejected excessive industrial ornamentation and instead highlighted handcrafted details, natural materials, and practical layouts. Common features included low-pitched roofs, exposed wooden elements, and expansive front porches that encouraged outdoor relaxation and neighborhood interaction. These homes reflected a broader cultural shift toward simplicity and authenticity in domestic life.
Another major

item.title: Authorities Removed Firearms from Mosque Shooter’s Home Before 2026 Attack
item.description: In 2025, more than a year before a deadly attack at a San Diego mosque, local authorities had already intervened in one of the eventual shooter’s homes due to concerns about his behavior. Police filed a protective order to remove dozens of firearms from the residence, highlighting prior awareness of potential risks. Despite these early warnings, the attackers carried out a violent assault in which two teens killed three people at the mosque. The incident raises serious questions about whether existing protective measures could have prevented the tragedy. Law enforcement’s prior knowledge of the shooter’s history and behavior has led to scrutiny over the effectiveness of firearm removal orders and whether follow-up procedures were sufficient. The case underscores the broader challenges authorities face in anticipating violent acts, particularly when warning signs exist but a direct threat has not yet materialized. Community leaders and policymakers are calling for evaluations of gun con

item.title: Norman Seeff Captures Early Moments of Patti Smith and Robert Mapplethorpe in New York
item.description: In 1969, South African photographer Norman Seeff met Patti Smith and Robert Mapplethorpe, then young and relatively unknown, at Max's Kansas City, a popular New York club for artists and musicians. Struck by their presence, Seeff invited them to a photo session in a friend's apartment and studio on the Upper West Side. While the formal shoot produced some photos, the most iconic images emerged during a relaxed period when the three were simply hanging out. These photographs capture the intimacy and depth of the relationship between Smith and Mapplethorpe, reflecting both the end of their romantic involvement and the start of a lifelong artistic partnership. One notable detail is Smith wearing the skull necklace she made for Mapplethorpe, a moment later referenced in her memoir 'Just Kids.' Seeff's photos highlight authenticity and emotional resonance, documenting the pair at the very beginning of their journeys to becoming influential figures in New York's art scene. At this time, Mapp

item.title: Bob Weir of the Grateful Dead: A Look Back at His Early Years and Legacy
item.description: Bob Weir, the co-founder and rhythm guitarist of the Grateful Dead, passed away on January 10, 2026, at the age of 78. He died peacefully with family by his side after battling cancer and enduring underlying lung issues. Known as the youngest member of the iconic band during the 1960s, Weir brought high energy and a unique rhythm guitar style that complemented Jerry Garcia's leadership. He joined the band at just 16 and spent more than six decades performing, with his final concerts occurring in August 2025 at Golden Gate Park to mark the band's 60th anniversary. Weir's passing follows the death of fellow founding member Phil Lesh in 2024, leaving Bill Kreutzmann as the only remaining member of the original 1965 lineup. Bob Weir’s contributions helped shape the sound and spirit of the Grateful Dead, leaving a lasting legacy for fans and the music world. His youthful photos from the 1960s capture the early days of a legendary musician whose career spanned generations.

item.title: U.S. Invests $2 Billion in Quantum Computing to Strengthen Technology and Prepare for Cryptography Challenges
item.description: The U.S. government is investing over $2 billion in quantum computing, signaling its strategic importance for both technology and national security. The Department of Commerce announced letters of intent with nine companies, including IBM, GlobalFoundries, D-Wave, Rigetti, Infleqtion, PsiQuantum, Quantinuum, Atom Computing, and Diraq. These investments provide minority equity stakes to the government, reflecting a proactive industrial policy similar to initiatives in semiconductor manufacturing and other strategic sectors. IBM is set to receive $1 billion to establish a quantum foundry for superconducting wafers, while GlobalFoundries will get $375 million to expand domestic quantum hardware production. Funding also targets specific technological challenges across various quantum architectures such as neutral-atom, photonic, trapped-ion, silicon-spin, and superconducting systems. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick emphasized that these measures aim to boost American quantum capabilities

item.title: Prosecutors Seek Appeal After Court Strikes Off Extortion Case Involving Joe 'Ferrari' Sibanyoni
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) in Mpumalanga has filed an application for leave to appeal against several controversial orders issued by Chief Magistrate T. Tonjeni in the Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni extortion case. The orders, handed down on May 18 at Kwaggafontein Magistrate Court, included the conviction of a prosecutor for contempt of court, authorization of a warrant for his arrest, and the striking off of the criminal case due to alleged unreasonable delays under Section 342A(3)(c) of the Criminal Procedure Act. The appeal application immediately suspends the enforcement of these orders while the higher court reviews the matter. The NPA has also formally requested written reasons from the magistrate to clarify the basis for the rulings, ensuring a comprehensive record for the appeal process. A legal team led by Senior Counsel has been assembled to provide expert guidance on the complex legal issues involved. Advocates for the NPA emphasized the urgency of receiving the writ

item.title: Glenda Jackson: Celebrated Actress and Dedicated Public Servant
item.description: Glenda Jackson (1936–2023) was a remarkable figure who achieved extraordinary success in both acting and politics. She gained international acclaim in the 1970s for her intense and nuanced portrayals of complex female characters, winning two Academy Awards for Best Actress for 'Women in Love' and 'A Touch of Class.' Jackson's career also earned her a Tony Award for 'Three Tall Women' and three Emmy Awards, most notably for her portrayal of Queen Elizabeth I in 'Elizabeth R.' In 1992, at the height of her acting career, Jackson made a bold transition to politics, serving as a Member of Parliament for the Labour Party for 23 years. Known for her intellectual rigor and unwavering dedication, she became a respected figure in public service. After retiring from politics in 2015, she returned to the stage, captivating audiences once again with her performance as King Lear. Jackson remains celebrated for her integrity, versatility, and remarkable contributions to both the arts and political l

item.title: NASA Technician Inspects Echo II Satellite Using Tethered Balloon, 1963
item.description: In 1963, a NASA technician conducted a unique manual inspection of the Echo II communications satellite inside a hangar at Lakehurst, New Jersey. The satellite, a massive 135-foot-diameter Mylar balloon, was highly reflective and designed to passively relay radio signals back to Earth by reflecting them. To ensure the balloon’s surface was free from leaks or flaws, the technician floated up and down alongside it using a small personal balloon tethered to the ground. The Echo satellite program marked a pioneering step in space communications. Unlike modern satellites, Echo I and II carried no electronic instruments; instead, signals sent from Earth were simply bounced off their reflective surfaces to other ground stations. Echo I, launched in 1960, successfully transmitted the first transcontinental voice message, including one from President Dwight Eisenhower. Echo II, launched in 1964, was larger and used for both scientific experiments and international signal reflections, including

item.title: Neil Armstrong's Parents Watch Their Son Walk on the Moon in 1969
item.description: On July 20, 1969, Stephen and Viola Armstrong experienced an unforgettable day as their son, Neil Armstrong, became the first human to walk on the Moon. The couple faced a mixture of anxiety, pride, and overwhelming attention as the world watched Neil's historic moonwalk. Viola Armstrong later revealed that she was terrified during the landing, praying for Neil's safety and closely following the lunar module's fuel levels. Stephen Armstrong maintained a stoic presence, though his tension was apparent until the confirmation of a safe landing. The Armstrongs were surrounded by neighbors, friends, and numerous reporters as they watched the moonwalk on a television set in their garage. Viola's emotional reaction upon seeing Neil take his first steps on the Moon reflected the personal pride and awe experienced by parents witnessing such a historic event. Stephen expressed amazement at his son's achievement while continuing to participate in local civic activities to preserve a sense of norm

item.title: Jake Lang sentenced to 10 days for contempt during bond hearing in Tennessee
item.description: Jake Lang, a prominent far-right influencer and Jan. 6 rioter, received the maximum 10-day jail sentence for contempt of court in Tennessee after attempting to speak during the bond hearing of Dalton Eatherly, a white livestreamer charged with attempted murder. Lang, who was pardoned by former President Donald Trump in January 2025 following nearly four years awaiting trial for assaulting police officers with a baseball bat during the Capitol attack, has since been involved in anti-Islam rallies and far-right political activism, including performing Nazi salutes. Eatherly, the defendant in the bond hearing, faces charges for shooting a Black disabled veteran outside the Montgomery County Courthouse and has a documented history of using racial slurs against Black people. Despite these charges, Eatherly has gained a significant following and raised over $100,000 for his legal defense in a single day. Judge H. Reid Poland III ordered Lang removed from the hearing for attempting to speak w

item.title: Corpus Christi Faces Severe Water Shortage Amid Drought and Growing Industrial Demand
item.description: Corpus Christi, Texas, and the surrounding Coastal Bend region are facing a significant water shortage that has impacted residents, businesses, and industrial operations. Since August 2024, restrictions have limited lawn watering and other non-essential uses, with potential for stricter limits if drought conditions persist. The city relies on the Nueces, Lavaca-Navidad, and Colorado river basins for most of its water, with reservoirs such as Choke Canyon and Lake Corpus Christi currently at just 8% capacity. Industrial expansion, especially at the Port of Corpus Christi, has sharply increased water demand, and new water sources have not been developed since 2016. Plans for a desalination plant were abandoned due to rising costs, leaving the city to explore alternative measures. These include groundwater wells, reclaimed wastewater for industrial and irrigation use, and potential purchases from a nearly completed desalination facility. Long-term solutions will require careful planning,

item.title: Cameron Diaz’s Breakthrough Audition for 'The Mask' at Age 21
item.description: In 1993, Cameron Diaz, a 21-year-old model with no prior acting experience, auditioned for what would become her breakout role as Tina Carlyle in 'The Mask.' Initially seeking only a minor background role, Diaz caught the eye of director Chuck Russell, who was impressed by her headshot and insisted on bringing her in despite studio hesitations. Over several weeks, she faced a rigorous audition process, including multiple callbacks and a chemistry screen test with Jim Carrey. Russell recognized her instant likability and intelligence, believing she was the perfect fit for the role. Diaz admitted to struggling with stress during the audition, even developing an ulcer due to nerves. The studio only agreed to cast her seven days before filming began, influenced by Russell's firm support. This casting is now regarded as one of Hollywood's most notable 'happy accidents,' launching Diaz’s successful acting career.

item.title: Grateful Dead’s Inaugural Performance at Magoo’s Pizza in 1965
item.description: On May 5, 1965, the Grateful Dead, originally performing under the name The Warlocks, played their very first show at Magoo’s Pizza in Menlo Park, California. After discovering that another band already had the name, they later adopted the iconic name 'Grateful Dead' in December of that year. The original lineup featured Jerry Garcia, Bob Weir, Ron “Pigpen” McKernan, Phil Lesh, and Bill Kreutzmann. Known for their unique blend of musical styles, the band fused rock, blues, jazz, folk, country, bluegrass, gospel, reggae, and world music, often adding psychedelic influences. They gained fame for their improvisational live performances and developed a devoted fan base called 'Deadheads.' Musician and writer Lenny Kaye has described their work as exploring musical territory few other bands reach. The Grateful Dead are widely regarded as the pioneering forefathers of the jam band genre, influencing countless musicians and shaping the landscape of live improvisational rock music.

item.title: SpaceX successfully tests Starship V3 despite booster failure during return
item.description: On May 22, 2026, SpaceX conducted the first test flight of its upgraded Starship V3 from its Starbase facility in Texas. The 407-foot rocket, the largest and most powerful built by SpaceX, lifted off at 5:30 p.m. local time. Shortly after launch, the Starship upper stage separated from the Super Heavy booster and continued its journey into space. However, the booster failed to reignite its engines for the return burn, causing it to tumble into the Gulf of Mexico and likely explode. During ascent, Starship lost one of its six Raptor engines, yet it successfully deployed 20 Starlink satellite simulators and two modified Starlink satellites to record exterior footage. The Starship stage performed a simulated ocean landing in the Indian Ocean, tipping over and exploding as expected. This test flight was significant for evaluating the new Starship V3 hardware, which includes third-generation Raptor engines designed for increased thrust and simpler operation, as well as a new launchpad syste

item.title: 1942 Tennessee Case: 14-Year-Old Mattie Pearl Manning Convicted of Husband’s Murder
item.description: In June 1942, a tragic and highly publicized case unfolded in Lowland, Hamblen County, Tennessee. Mattie Pearl Manning, a 14-year-old child bride with only a second-grade education, and her father, Frank Morgan, were convicted of second-degree murder and sentenced to 20 years in prison. The case arose from the fatal shooting of Mattie Pearl’s 35-year-old husband, Daymon W. Manning, at the breakfast table in Frank Morgan’s home. The circumstances surrounding the murder were contentious, with conflicting accounts from both Mattie Pearl and her father. Initially, Mattie claimed full responsibility, stating she acted because her husband threatened her. Subsequently, her father submitted a confession to exonerate her, only to later retract it. The trial in Morristown, Tennessee, drew national attention due to the unusual nature of the crime and the dramatic shifts in testimony. The jury quickly found Mattie Pearl guilty of second-degree murder, and her father guilty of aiding and abetting.

item.title: U.S. Nonprofits Respond to Government Pressure and Threats
item.description: In recent years, social scientists have highlighted concerns about the rise of authoritarian strategies in democratic countries, often referred to as the “authoritarian playbook.” Although no official book exists, certain leaders have systematically used democratic mechanisms to consolidate power, as seen in countries like Russia and Venezuela. In the U.S., nonprofits have faced significant pressure under the second Trump administration, including major funding cuts to organizations supporting healthcare, childcare, food access, and international aid. Additionally, rules were changed to restrict student loan forgiveness for nonprofit employees and to threaten nonprofits with prosecution for legal advocacy. Congress contributed to this pressure by holding hearings and promoting legislation to label nonprofits as terrorist-supporting organizations. Despite these challenges, many U.S. nonprofits have united to defend their missions, signing open letters, participating in large-scale strat

item.title: Montreal Canadiens Show Remarkable Performance in Playoffs with Youthful Energy
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens are demonstrating a remarkable playoff performance, reminiscent of their 1971, 1986, and 1993 Stanley Cup-winning teams. Despite facing a dominant Carolina Hurricanes squad in the Eastern Conference Final, the Canadiens showcased resilience and skill, overturning an early deficit to win 6-2. Key players like Cole Caufield, Phillip Danault, Alexandre Texier, and Ivan Demidov contributed significantly, reflecting a young team averaging just 25.8 years of age. The article draws parallels between this youthful energy and the legendary 1993 team, emphasizing that youth brings a lack of desperation seen in aging stars and contributes to a seemingly intangible momentum or 'magic' during crucial moments.
